We\\n1419: \\t\\t\\t// don't need to store these, as we now know the ignored value is\\n1420: \\t\\t\\t// only within the map, not the map itself.\\n1421: \\t\\t\\tignoredVal, _ := ignored.value.Unmark()\\n1422: \\n1423: \\t\\t\\tswitch {\\n1424: \\t\\t\\tcase ignored.value.IsNull() || ignoredVal.LengthInt() == 0:\\n1425: \\t\\t\\t\\tpriorMap = map[string]cty.Value{}\\n1426: \\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n1427: \\t\\t\\t\\tpriorMap = ignoredVal.AsValueMap()\\n1428: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1429: \\n1430: \\t\\t\\tkey := ignored.key.AsString()\\n1431: \\t\\t\\tpriorElem, keep := priorMap[key]\\n1432: \\n1433: \\t\\t\\tswitch {\\n1434: \\t\\t\\tcase !keep:\\n1435: \\t\\t\\t\\t// this didn't exist in the old map value, so we're keeping the\\n1436: \\t\\t\\t\\t// \\\"absence\\\" of the key by removing it from the config\\n1437: \\t\\t\\t\\tdelete(configMap, key)\\n1438: \\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n1439: \\t\\t\\t\\tconfigMap[key] = priorElem\\n1440: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1441: \\t\\t}\\n1442: \\n1443: \\t\\tvar newVal cty.Value\\n1444: \\t\\tswitch {\\n1445: \\t\\tcase len(configMap) > 0:\\n1446: \\t\\t\\tnewVal = cty.MapVal(configMap)\\n1447: \\t\\tcase v.IsNull():\\n1448: \\t\\t\\t// if the config value was null, and no values remain in the map,\\n1449: \\t\\t\\t// reset the value to null.\\n1450: \\t\\t\\tnewVal = v\\n1451: \\t\\tdefault:\\n1452: \\t\\t\\tnewVal = cty.MapValEmpty(v.Type().ElementType())\\n1453: \\t\\t}\\n1454: \\n1455: \\t\\tif len(vMarks) > 0 {\\n1456: \\t\\t\\tnewVal = newVal.WithMarks(vMarks)\\n1457: \\t\\t}\\n1458: \\n1459: \\t\\treturn newVal, nil\\n1460: \\t})\\n1461: \\treturn ret, nil\\n1462: }\\n1463: \\n1464: // readDataSource handles everything needed to call ReadDataSource on the provider.\\n1465: // A previously evaluated configVal can be passed in, or a new one is generated\\n1466: // from the resource configuration.\\n1467: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) readDataSource(ctx EvalContext, configVal cty.Value) (cty.Value, *providers.Deferred, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n1468: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n1469: \\tvar newVal cty.Value\\n1470: \\tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\\n1471: \\n1472: \\tconfig := *n.Config\\n1473: \\tdeferralAllowed := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\\n1474: \\n1475: \\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n1476: \\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n1477: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n1478: \\t\\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\\n1479: \\t}\\n1480: \\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource)\\n1481: \\tif schema == nil {\\n1482: \\t\\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\\n1483: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"provider %q does not support data source %q\\\", n.ResolvedProvider, n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource.Type))\\n1484: \\t\\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\\n1485: \\t}\\n1486: \\n1487: \\tmetaConfigVal, metaDiags := n.providerMetas(ctx)\\n1488: \\tdiags = diags.Append(metaDiags)\\n1489: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n1490: \\t\\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\\n1491: \\t}\\n1492: \\n1493: \\t// Unmark before sending to provider, will re-mark before returning\\n1494: \\tvar pvm []cty.PathValueMarks\\n1495: \\tconfigVal, pvm = configVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\\n1496: \\n1497: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] readDataSource: Re-validating config for %s\\\", n.Addr)\\n1498: \\tvalidateResp := provider.ValidateDataResourceConfig(\\n1499: \\t\\tproviders.ValidateDataResourceConfigRequest{\\n1500: \\t\\t\\tTypeName: n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource.Type,\\n1501: \\t\\t\\tConfig:   configVal,\\n1502: \\t\\t},\\n1503: \\t)\\n1504: \\tdiags = diags.Append(validateResp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(config.Config, n.Addr.String()))\\n1505: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n1506: \\t\\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\\n1507: \\t}\\n1508: \\n1509: \\t// If we get down here then our configuration is complete and we're read\\n1510: \\t// to actually call the provider to read the data.\\n1511: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] readDataSource: %s configuration is complete, so reading from provider\\\", n.Addr)\\n1512: \\n1513: \\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n1514: \\t\\treturn h.PreApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, plans.Read, cty.NullVal(configVal.Type()), configVal)\\n1515: \\t}))\\n1516: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n1517: \\t\\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\\n1518: \\t}\\n1519: \\n1520: \\tvar resp providers.ReadDataSourceResponse\\n1521: \\tif n.override != nil {\\n1522: \\t\\toverride, overrideDiags := mocking.ComputedValuesForDataSource(configVal, mocking.MockedData{\\n1523: \\t\\t\\tValue: n.override.Values,\\n1524: \\t\\t\\tRange: n.override.ValuesRange,\\n1525: \\t\\t}, schema)\\n1526: \\t\\tresp = providers.ReadDataSourceResponse{\\n1527: \\t\\t\\tState:       override,\\n1528: \\t\\t\\tDiagnostics: overrideDiags,\\n1529: \\t\\t}\\n1530: \\t} else {\\n1531: \\t\\tresp = provider.ReadDataSource(providers.ReadDataSourceRequest{\\n1532: \\t\\t\\tTypeName:     n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource.Type,\\n1533: \\t\\t\\tConfig:       configVal,\\n1534: \\t\\t\\tProviderMeta: metaConfigVal,\\n1535: \\t\\t\\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\\n1536: \\t\\t\\t\\tDeferralAllowed: deferralAllowed,\\n1537: \\t\\t\\t},\\n1538: \\t\\t})\\n1539: \\n1540: \\t\\t// If we don't support deferrals, but the provider reports a deferral and does not\\n1541: \\t\\t// emit any error level diagnostics, we should emit an error.\\n1542: \\t\\tif resp.Deferred != nil && !deferralAllowed && !resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {\\n1543: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(deferring.UnexpectedProviderDeferralDiagnostic(n.Addr))\\n1544: \\t\\t}\\n1545: \\n1546: \\t\\tif resp.Deferred != nil {\\n1547: \\t\\t\\tdeferred = resp.Deferred\\n1548: \\t\\t}\\n1549: \\t}\\n1550: \\tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(config.Config, n.Addr.String()))\\n1551: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n1552: \\t\\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\\n1553: \\t}\\n1554: \\tnewVal = resp.State\\n1555: \\tif newVal == cty.NilVal {\\n1556: \\t\\t// This can happen with incompletely-configured mocks. We'll allow it\\n1557: \\t\\t// and treat it as an alias for a properly-typed null value.\\n1558: \\t\\tnewVal = cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType())\\n1559: \\t}\\n1560: \\n1561: \\t// We don't want to run the checks if the data source read is deferred\\n1562: \\tif deferred == nil {\\n1563: \\t\\tfor _, err := range newVal.Type().TestConformance(schema.ImpliedType()) {\\n1564: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n1565: \\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n1566: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider produced invalid object\\\",\\n1567: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n1568: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider %q produced an invalid value for %s.\\\\n\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\",\\n1569: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tn.ResolvedProvider, tfdiags.FormatErrorPrefixed(err, n.Addr.String()),\\n1570: \\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n1571: \\t\\t\\t))\\n1572: \\t\\t}\\n1573: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n1574: \\t\\t\\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\\n1575: \\t\\t}\\n1576: \\n1577: \\t\\tif newVal.IsNull() {\\n1578: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n1579: \\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n1580: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider produced null object\\\",\\n1581: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n1582: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider %q produced a null value for %s.\\\\n\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\",\\n1583: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tn.ResolvedProvider, n.Addr,\\n1584: \\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n1585: \\t\\t\\t))\\n1586: \\t\\t}\\n1587: \\n1588: \\t\\tif !newVal.IsNull() && !newVal.IsWhollyKnown() {\\n1589: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n1590: \\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n1591: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider produced invalid object\\\",\\n1592: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n1593: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider %q produced a value for %s that is not wholly known.\\\\n\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\",\\n1594: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tn.ResolvedProvider, n.Addr,\\n1595: \\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n1596: \\t\\t\\t))\\n1597: \\n1598: \\t\\t\\t// We'll still save the object, but we need to eliminate any unknown\\n1599: \\t\\t\\t// values first because we can't serialize them in the state file.\\n1600: \\t\\t\\t// Note that this may cause set elements to be coalesced if they\\n1601: \\t\\t\\t// differed only by having unknown values, but we don't worry about\\n1602: \\t\\t\\t// that here because we're saving the value only for inspection\\n1603: \\t\\t\\t// purposes; the error we added above will halt the graph walk.\\n1604: \\t\\t\\tnewVal = cty.UnknownAsNull(newVal)\\n1605: \\t\\t}\\n1606: \\t}\\n1607: \\tnewVal = newVal.MarkWithPaths(pvm)\\n1608: \\tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(newVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\\n1609: \\t\\tnewVal = marks.MarkPaths(newVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\\n1610: \\t}\\n1611: \\n1612: \\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n1613: \\t\\treturn h.PostApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, newVal, diags.Err())\\n1614: \\t}))\\n1615: \\n1616: \\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\\n1617: }\\n1618: \\n1619: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) providerMetas(ctx EvalContext) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n1620: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n1621: \\tmetaConfigVal := cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType)\\n1622: \\n1623: \\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n1624: \\tif err != nil {\\n1625: \\t\\treturn metaConfigVal, diags.Append(err)\\n1626: \\t}\\n1627: \\tif n.ProviderMetas != nil {\\n1628: \\t\\tif m, ok := n.ProviderMetas[n.ResolvedProvider.Provider]; ok && m != nil {\\n1629: \\t\\t\\t// if the provider doesn't support this feature, throw an error\\n1630: \\t\\t\\tif providerSchema.ProviderMeta.Block == nil {\\n1631: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n1632: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\\n1633: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSummary:  fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Provider %s doesn't support provider_meta\\\", n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.String()),\\n1634: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(\\\"The resource %s belongs to a provider that doesn't support provider_meta blocks\\\", n.Addr.Resource),\\n1635: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSubject:  &m.ProviderRange,\\n1636: \\t\\t\\t\\t})\\n1637: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n1638: \\t\\t\\t\\tvar configDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n1639: \\t\\t\\t\\tmetaConfigVal, _, configDiags = ctx.EvaluateBlock(m.Config, providerSchema.ProviderMeta.Block, nil, EvalDataForNoInstanceKey)\\n1640: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\\n1641: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1642: \\t\\t}\\n1643: \\t}\\n1644: \\treturn metaConfigVal, diags\\n1645: }\\n1646: \\n1647: // planDataSource deals with the main part of the data resource lifecycle:\\n1648: // either actually reading from the data source or generating a plan to do so.\\n1649: //\\n1650: // currentState is the current state for the data source, and the new state is\\n1651: // returned. While data sources are read-only, we need to start with the prior\\n1652: // state to determine if we have a change or not.  If we needed to read a new\\n1653: // value, but it still matches the previous state, then we can record a NoNop\\n1654: // change. If the states don't match then we record a Read change so that the\\n1655: // new value is applied to the state.\\n1656: //\\n1657: // The cases where a data source will generate a planned change instead\\n1658: // of finishing during the plan are:\\n1659: //\\n1660: //   - Its config has unknown values or it depends on a resource with pending changes.\\n1661: //     (Note that every data source that is DeferredPrereq should also fit this description.)\\n1662: //   - We attempted a read request, but the provider says we're deferred.\\n1663: //   - It's nested in a check block, and should always read again during apply.\\n1664: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) planDataSource(ctx EvalContext, checkRuleSeverity tfdiags.Severity, skipPlanChanges, dependencyDeferred bool) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, *states.ResourceInstanceObject, *providers.Deferred, instances.RepetitionData, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n1665: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n1666: \\tvar keyData instances.RepetitionData\\n1667: \\tvar configVal cty.Value\\n1668: \\n1669: \\tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\\n1670: \\tif dependencyDeferred {\\n1671: \\t\\t// If a dependency of this data source was deferred, then we're going\\n1672: \\t\\t// to end up deferring this whatever happens. So, our default status\\n1673: \\t\\t// is deferred. If the provider indicates this resource should be\\n1674: \\t\\t// deferred for another reason, that reason should take priority over\\n1675: \\t\\t// this one.\\n1676: \\t\\tdeferred = &providers.Deferred{\\n1677: \\t\\t\\tReason: providers.DeferredReasonDeferredPrereq,\\n1678: \\t\\t}\\n1679: \\t}\\n1680: \\n1681: \\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n1682: \\tif err != nil {\\n1683: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags.Append(err)\\n1684: \\t}\\n1685: \\n1686: \\tconfig := *n.Config\\n1687: \\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource)\\n1688: \\tif schema == nil {\\n1689: \\t\\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\\n1690: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"provider %q does not support data source %q\\\", n.ResolvedProvider, n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource.Type))\\n1691: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\\n1692: \\t}\\n1693: \\n1694: \\tobjTy := schema.ImpliedType()\\n1695: \\tpriorVal := cty.NullVal(objTy)\\n1696: \\n1697: \\tforEach, _, _ := evaluateForEachExpression(config.ForEach, ctx, false)\\n1698: \\tkeyData = EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, forEach)\\n1699: \\n1700: \\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\\n1701: \\t\\taddrs.ResourcePrecondition,\\n1702: \\t\\tn.Config.Preconditions,\\n1703: \\t\\tctx, n.Addr, keyData,\\n1704: \\t\\tcheckRuleSeverity,\\n1705: \\t)\\n1706: \\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\\n1707: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n1708: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags // failed preconditions prevent further evaluation\\n1709: \\t}\\n1710: \\n1711: \\tvar configDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n1712: \\tconfigVal, _, configDiags = ctx.EvaluateBlock(config.Config, schema, nil, keyData)\\n1713: \\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\\n1714: \\tdiags = diags.Append(\\n1715: \\t\\tvalidateResourceForbiddenEphemeralValues(ctx, configVal, schema).InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, n.Addr.String()),\\n1716: \\t)\\n1717: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n1718: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\\n1719: \\t}\\n1720: \\tunmarkedConfigVal, unmarkedPaths := configVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\\n1721: \\n1722: \\tcheck, nested := n.nestedInCheckBlock()\\n1723: \\tif nested {\\n1724: \\t\\t// Going forward from this point, the only reason we will fail is\\n1725: \\t\\t// that the data source fails to load its data. Normally, this would\\n1726: \\t\\t// cancel the entire plan and this error message would bubble its way\\n1727: \\t\\t// back up to the user.\\n1728: \\t\\t//\\n1729: \\t\\t// But, if we are in a check block then we don't want this data block to\\n1730: \\t\\t// cause the plan to fail. We also need to report a status on the data\\n1731: \\t\\t// block so the check processing later on knows whether to attempt to\\n1732: \\t\\t// process the checks. Either we'll report the data block as failed\\n1733: \\t\\t// if/when we load the data block later, or we want to report it as a\\n1734: \\t\\t// success overall.\\n1735: \\t\\t//\\n1736: \\t\\t// Therefore, we create a deferred function here that will check if the\\n1737: \\t\\t// status for the check has been updated yet, and if not we will set it\\n1738: \\t\\t// to be StatusPass. The rest of this function will only update the\\n1739: \\t\\t// status if it should be StatusFail.\\n1740: \\t\\tdefer func() {\\n1741: \\t\\t\\tstatus := ctx.Checks().ObjectCheckStatus(check.Addr().Absolute(n.Addr.Module))\\n1742: \\t\\t\\tif status == checks.StatusUnknown {\\n1743: \\t\\t\\t\\tctx.Checks().ReportCheckResult(check.Addr().Absolute(n.Addr.Module), addrs.CheckDataResource, 0, checks.StatusPass)\\n1744: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1745: \\t\\t}()\\n1746: \\t}\\n1747: \\n1748: \\tconfigKnown := configVal.IsWhollyKnown()\\n1749: \\tdepsPending := n.dependenciesHavePendingChanges(ctx)\\n1750: \\t// If our configuration contains any unknown values, or we depend on any\\n1751: \\t// unknown values then we must defer the read to the apply phase by\\n1752: \\t// producing a \\\"Read\\\" change for this resource, and a placeholder value for\\n1753: \\t// it in the state.\\n1754: \\tif depsPending || !configKnown {\\n1755: \\t\\t// We can't plan any changes if we're only refreshing, so the only\\n1756: \\t\\t// value we can set here is whatever was in state previously.\\n1757: \\t\\tif skipPlanChanges {\\n1758: \\t\\t\\tplannedNewState := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\n1759: \\t\\t\\t\\tValue:  priorVal,\\n1760: \\t\\t\\t\\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\\n1761: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1762: \\n1763: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, plannedNewState, deferred, keyData, diags\\n1764: \\t\\t}\\n1765: \\n1766: \\t\\tvar reason plans.ResourceInstanceChangeActionReason\\n1767: \\t\\tswitch {\\n1768: \\t\\tcase !configKnown:\\n1769: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] planDataSource: %s configuration not fully known yet, so deferring to apply phase\\\", n.Addr)\\n1770: \\t\\t\\treason = plans.ResourceInstanceReadBecauseConfigUnknown\\n1771: \\t\\tcase depsPending:\\n1772: \\t\\t\\t// NOTE: depsPending can be true at the same time as configKnown\\n1773: \\t\\t\\t// is false; configKnown takes precedence because it's more\\n1774: \\t\\t\\t// specific.\\n1775: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] planDataSource: %s configuration is fully known, at least one dependency has changes pending\\\", n.Addr)\\n1776: \\t\\t\\treason = plans.ResourceInstanceReadBecauseDependencyPending\\n1777: \\t\\t}\\n1778: \\n1779: \\t\\tproposedNewVal := objchange.PlannedDataResourceObject(schema, unmarkedConfigVal)\\n1780: \\n1781: \\t\\t// even though we are only returning the config value because we can't\\n1782: \\t\\t// yet read the data source, we need to incorporate the schema marks so\\n1783: \\t\\t// that downstream consumers can detect them when planning.\\n1784: \\t\\tproposedNewVal = proposedNewVal.MarkWithPaths(unmarkedPaths)\\n1785: \\t\\tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(proposedNewVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\\n1786: \\t\\t\\tproposedNewVal = marks.MarkPaths(proposedNewVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\\n1787: \\t\\t}\\n1788: \\n1789: \\t\\t// Apply detects that the data source will need to be read by the After\\n1790: \\t\\t// value containing unknowns from PlanDataResourceObject.\\n1791: \\t\\tplannedChange := &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\\n1792: \\t\\t\\tAddr:         n.Addr,\\n1793: \\t\\t\\tPrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\\n1794: \\t\\t\\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\\n1795: \\t\\t\\tChange: plans.Change{\\n1796: \\t\\t\\t\\tAction: plans.Read,\\n1797: \\t\\t\\t\\tBefore: priorVal,\\n1798: \\t\\t\\t\\tAfter:  proposedNewVal,\\n1799: \\t\\t\\t},\\n1800: \\t\\t\\tActionReason: reason,\\n1801: \\t\\t}\\n1802: \\n1803: \\t\\tplannedNewState := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\n1804: \\t\\t\\tValue:  proposedNewVal,\\n1805: \\t\\t\\tStatus: states.ObjectPlanned,\\n1806: \\t\\t}\\n1807: \\n1808: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n1809: \\t\\t\\treturn h.PostDiff(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, plans.Read, priorVal, proposedNewVal)\\n1810: \\t\\t}))\\n1811: \\n1812: \\t\\treturn plannedChange, plannedNewState, deferred, keyData, diags\\n1813: \\t}\\n1814: \\n1815: \\t// We have a complete configuration with no dependencies to wait on, so we\\n1816: \\t// can read the data source into the state.\\n1817: \\t// newVal is fully marked by the readDataSource method.\\n1818: \\tnewVal, readDeferred, readDiags := n.readDataSource(ctx, configVal)\\n1819: \\n1820: \\tif readDeferred != nil {\\n1821: \\t\\t// This will either be null or a value that indicates we're deferred\\n1822: \\t\\t// because of a dependency. In both cases we're happy to just overwrite\\n1823: \\t\\t// that with the more relevant information directly from the provider.\\n1824: \\t\\tdeferred = readDeferred\\n1825: \\t}\\n1826: \\n1827: \\t// Now we've loaded the data, and diags tells us whether we were successful\\n1828: \\t// or not, we are going to create our plannedChange and our\\n1829: \\t// proposedNewState.\\n1830: \\tvar plannedChange *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\\n1831: \\tvar plannedNewState *states.ResourceInstanceObject\\n1832: \\n1833: \\t// If we are a nested block, then we want to create a plannedChange that\\n1834: \\t// tells Terraform to reload the data block during the apply stage even if\\n1835: \\t// we managed to get the data now.\\n1836: \\t// Another consideration is that if we failed to load the data, we need to\\n1837: \\t// disguise that for a nested block. Nested blocks will report the overall\\n1838: \\t// check as failed but won't affect the rest of the plan operation or block\\n1839: \\t// an apply operation.\\n1840: \\n1841: \\tif nested {\\n1842: \\t\\taddr := check.Addr().Absolute(n.Addr.Module)\\n1843: \\n1844: \\t\\t// Let's fix things up for a nested data block.\\n1845: \\t\\t//\\n1846: \\t\\t// A nested data block doesn't error, and creates a planned change. So,\\n1847: \\t\\t// if we encountered an error we'll tidy up newVal so it makes sense\\n1848: \\t\\t// and handle the error. We'll also create the plannedChange if\\n1849: \\t\\t// appropriate.\\n1850: \\n1851: \\t\\tif readDiags.HasErrors() {\\n1852: \\t\\t\\t// If we had errors, then we can cover that up by marking the new\\n1853: \\t\\t\\t// state as unknown.\\n1854: \\t\\t\\tnewVal = objchange.PlannedDataResourceObject(schema, unmarkedConfigVal)\\n1855: \\n1856: \\t\\t\\t// not only do we want to ensure this synthetic value has the marks,\\n1857: \\t\\t\\t// but since this is the value being returned from the data source\\n1858: \\t\\t\\t// we need to ensure the schema marks are added as well.\\n1859: \\t\\t\\tnewVal = newVal.MarkWithPaths(unmarkedPaths)\\n1860: \\t\\t\\tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(newVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\\n1861: \\t\\t\\t\\tnewVal = marks.MarkPaths(newVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\\n1862: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1863: \\n1864: \\t\\t\\t// We still want to report the check as failed even if we are still\\n1865: \\t\\t\\t// letting it run again during the apply stage.\\n1866: \\t\\t\\tctx.Checks().ReportCheckFailure(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0, readDiags.Err().Error())\\n1867: \\t\\t}\\n1868: \\n1869: \\t\\t// Any warning or error diagnostics we'll wrap with some special checks\\n1870: \\t\\t// diagnostics. This is so we can identify them later, and so they'll\\n1871: \\t\\t// only report as warnings.\\n1872: \\t\\treadDiags = tfdiags.OverrideAll(readDiags, tfdiags.Warning, func() tfdiags.DiagnosticExtraWrapper {\\n1873: \\t\\t\\treturn &addrs.CheckRuleDiagnosticExtra{\\n1874: \\t\\t\\t\\tCheckRule: addrs.NewCheckRule(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0),\\n1875: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1876: \\t\\t})\\n1877: \\n1878: \\t\\t// refreshOnly plans cannot produce planned changes, so we only do\\n1879: \\t\\t// this if skipPlanChanges is false. Conversely, provider-deferred data\\n1880: \\t\\t// sources always generate a planned change with a different ActionReason.\\n1881: \\t\\tif !skipPlanChanges && deferred == nil {\\n1882: \\t\\t\\tplannedChange = &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\\n1883: \\t\\t\\t\\tAddr:         n.Addr,\\n1884: \\t\\t\\t\\tPrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\\n1885: \\t\\t\\t\\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\\n1886: \\t\\t\\t\\tChange: plans.Change{\\n1887: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tAction: plans.Read,\\n1888: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tBefore: priorVal,\\n1889: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tAfter:  newVal,\\n1890: \\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n1891: \\t\\t\\t\\tActionReason: plans.ResourceInstanceReadBecauseCheckNested,\\n1892: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1893: \\t\\t}\\n1894: \\t}\\n1895: \\n1896: \\t// Provider-deferred data sources always generate a planned change.\\n1897: \\tif deferred != nil {\\n1898: \\t\\tplannedChange = &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\\n1899: \\t\\t\\tAddr:         n.Addr,\\n1900: \\t\\t\\tPrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\\n1901: \\t\\t\\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\\n1902: \\t\\t\\tChange: plans.Change{\\n1903: \\t\\t\\t\\tAction: plans.Read,\\n1904: \\t\\t\\t\\tBefore: priorVal,\\n1905: \\t\\t\\t\\tAfter:  newVal,\\n1906: \\t\\t\\t},\\n1907: \\t\\t\\t// The caller should be more interested in the deferral reason, but this\\n1908: \\t\\t\\t// action reason is a reasonable description of what's happening.\\n1909: \\t\\t\\tActionReason: plans.ResourceInstanceReadBecauseDependencyPending,\\n1910: \\t\\t}\\n1911: \\n1912: \\t\\tplannedNewState = &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\n1913: \\t\\t\\tValue:  newVal,\\n1914: \\t\\t\\tStatus: states.ObjectPlanned,\\n1915: \\t\\t}\\n1916: \\t}\\n1917: \\n1918: \\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\\n1919: \\tif !diags.HasErrors() && deferred == nil {\\n1920: \\t\\t// Finally, let's make our new state.\\n1921: \\t\\tplannedNewState = &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\n1922: \\t\\t\\tValue:  newVal,\\n1923: \\t\\t\\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\\n1924: \\t\\t}\\n1925: \\t}\\n1926: \\n1927: \\treturn plannedChange, plannedNewState, deferred, keyData, diags\\n1928: }\\n1929: \\n1930: // nestedInCheckBlock determines if this resource is nested in a Check config\\n1931: // block. If so, this resource will be loaded during both plan and apply\\n1932: // operations to make sure the check is always giving the latest information.\\n1933: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) nestedInCheckBlock() (*configs.Check, bool) {\\n1934: \\tif n.Config.Container != nil {\\n1935: \\t\\tcheck, ok := n.Config.Container.(*configs.Check)\\n1936: \\t\\treturn check, ok\\n1937: \\t}\\n1938: \\treturn nil, false\\n1939: }\\n1940: \\n1941: // dependenciesHavePendingChanges determines whether any managed resource the\\n1942: // receiver depends on has a change pending in the plan, in which case we'd\\n1943: // need to override the usual behavior of immediately reading from the data\\n1944: // source where possible, and instead defer the read until the apply step.\\n1945: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dependenciesHavePendingChanges(ctx EvalContext) bool {\\n1946: \\tnModInst := n.Addr.Module\\n1947: \\tnMod := nModInst.Module()\\n1948: \\n1949: \\t// Check and see if any depends_on dependencies have\\n1950: \\t// changes, since they won't show up as changes in the\\n1951: \\t// configuration.\\n1952: \\tchanges := ctx.Changes()\\n1953: \\n1954: \\tdepsToUse := n.dependsOn\\n1955: \\n1956: \\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.DataResourceMode {\\n1957: \\t\\tif n.Config.HasCustomConditions() {\\n1958: \\t\\t\\t// For a data resource with custom conditions we need to look at\\n1959: \\t\\t\\t// the full set of resource dependencies -- both direct and\\n1960: \\t\\t\\t// indirect -- because an upstream update might be what's needed\\n1961: \\t\\t\\t// in order to make a condition pass.\\n1962: \\t\\t\\tdepsToUse = n.Dependencies\\n1963: \\t\\t}\\n1964: \\t}\\n1965: \\n1966: \\tfor _, d := range depsToUse {\\n1967: \\t\\tif d.Resource.Mode == addrs.DataResourceMode {\\n1968: \\t\\t\\t// Data sources have no external side effects, so they pose a need\\n1969: \\t\\t\\t// to delay this read. If they do have a change planned, it must be\\n1970: \\t\\t\\t// because of a dependency on a managed resource, in which case\\n1971: \\t\\t\\t// we'll also encounter it in this list of dependencies.\\n1972: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n1973: \\t\\t}\\n1974: \\n1975: \\t\\tfor _, change := range changes.GetChangesForConfigResource(d) {\\n1976: \\t\\t\\tchangeModInst := change.Addr.Module\\n1977: \\t\\t\\tchangeMod := changeModInst.Module()\\n1978: \\n1979: \\t\\t\\tif changeMod.Equal(nMod) && !changeModInst.Equal(nModInst) {\\n1980: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Dependencies are tracked by configuration address, which\\n1981: \\t\\t\\t\\t// means we may have changes from other instances of parent\\n1982: \\t\\t\\t\\t// modules. The actual reference can only take effect within\\n1983: \\t\\t\\t\\t// the same module instance, so skip any that aren't an exact\\n1984: \\t\\t\\t\\t// match\\n1985: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n1986: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1987: \\n1988: \\t\\t\\tif change != nil && change.Action != plans.NoOp {\\n1989: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n1990: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1991: \\t\\t}\\n1992: \\t}\\n1993: \\treturn false\\n1994: }\\n1995: \\n1996: // apply deals with the main part of the data resource lifecycle: either\\n1997: // actually reading from the data source or generating a plan to do so.\\n1998: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) applyDataSource(ctx EvalContext, planned *plans.ResourceInstanceChange) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, instances.RepetitionData, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n1999: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2000: \\tvar keyData instances.RepetitionData\\n2001: \\n2002: \\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n2003: \\tif err != nil {\\n2004: \\t\\treturn nil, keyData, diags.Append(err)\\n2005: \\t}\\n2006: \\tif planned != nil && planned.Action != plans.Read && planned.Action != plans.NoOp {\\n2007: \\t\\t// If any other action gets in here then that's always a bug; this\\n2008: \\t\\t// EvalNode only deals with reading.\\n2009: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\n2010: \\t\\t\\t\\\"invalid action %s for %s: only Read is supported (this is a bug in Terraform; please report it!)\\\",\\n2011: \\t\\t\\tplanned.Action, n.Addr,\\n2012: \\t\\t))\\n2013: \\t\\treturn nil, keyData, diags\\n2014: \\t}\\n2015: \\n2016: \\tconfig := *n.Config\\n2017: \\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource)\\n2018: \\tif schema == nil {\\n2019: \\t\\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\\n2020: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"provider %q does not support data source %q\\\", n.ResolvedProvider, n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource.Type))\\n2021: \\t\\treturn nil, keyData, diags\\n2022: \\t}\\n2023: \\n2024: \\tforEach, _, _ := evaluateForEachExpression(config.ForEach, ctx, false)\\n2025: \\tkeyData = EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.Addr.Resource.Key, forEach)\\n2026: \\n2027: \\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\\n2028: \\t\\taddrs.ResourcePrecondition,\\n2029: \\t\\tn.Config.Preconditions,\\n2030: \\t\\tctx, n.Addr, keyData,\\n2031: \\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n2032: \\t)\\n2033: \\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\\n2034: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n2035: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n2036: \\t\\t\\treturn h.PostApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, planned.Before, diags.Err())\\n2037: \\t\\t}))\\n2038: \\t\\treturn nil, keyData, diags // failed preconditions prevent further evaluation\\n2039: \\t}\\n2040: \\n2041: \\tif planned.Action == plans.NoOp {\\n2042: \\t\\t// If we didn't actually plan to read this then we have nothing more\\n2043: \\t\\t// to do; we're evaluating this only for incidentals like the\\n2044: \\t\\t// precondition/postcondition checks.\\n2045: \\t\\treturn nil, keyData, diags\\n2046: \\t}\\n2047: \\n2048: \\tconfigVal, _, configDiags := ctx.EvaluateBlock(config.Config, schema, nil, keyData)\\n2049: \\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\\n2050: \\tif configDiags.HasErrors() {\\n2051: \\t\\treturn nil, keyData, diags\\n2052: \\t}\\n2053: \\n2054: \\tnewVal, readDeferred, readDiags := n.readDataSource(ctx, configVal)\\n2055: \\tif check, nested := n.nestedInCheckBlock(); nested {\\n2056: \\t\\taddr := check.Addr().Absolute(n.Addr.Module)\\n2057: \\n2058: \\t\\t// We're just going to jump in here and hide away any errors for nested\\n2059: \\t\\t// data blocks.\\n2060: \\t\\tif readDiags.HasErrors() {\\n2061: \\t\\t\\tctx.Checks().ReportCheckFailure(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0, readDiags.Err().Error())\\n2062: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.OverrideAll(readDiags, tfdiags.Warning, func() tfdiags.DiagnosticExtraWrapper {\\n2063: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn &addrs.CheckRuleDiagnosticExtra{\\n2064: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tCheckRule: addrs.NewCheckRule(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0),\\n2065: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n2066: \\t\\t\\t}))\\n2067: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, keyData, diags\\n2068: \\t\\t}\\n2069: \\n2070: \\t\\t// Even though we know there are no errors here, we still want to\\n2071: \\t\\t// identify these diags has having been generated from a check block.\\n2072: \\t\\treadDiags = tfdiags.OverrideAll(readDiags, tfdiags.Warning, func() tfdiags.DiagnosticExtraWrapper {\\n2073: \\t\\t\\treturn &addrs.CheckRuleDiagnosticExtra{\\n2074: \\t\\t\\t\\tCheckRule: addrs.NewCheckRule(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0),\\n2075: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2076: \\t\\t})\\n2077: \\n2078: \\t\\t// If no errors, just remember to report this as a success and continue\\n2079: \\t\\t// as normal.\\n2080: \\t\\tctx.Checks().ReportCheckResult(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0, checks.StatusPass)\\n2081: \\t}\\n2082: \\n2083: \\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\\n2084: \\tif readDiags.HasErrors() {\\n2085: \\t\\treturn nil, keyData, diags\\n2086: \\t}\\n2087: \\n2088: \\tif readDeferred != nil {\\n2089: \\t\\t// Just skip data sources that are being deferred. Nothing, that\\n2090: \\t\\t// references them should be calling them.\\n2091: \\t\\treturn nil, keyData, diags\\n2092: \\t}\\n2093: \\n2094: \\tstate := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\n2095: \\t\\tValue:  newVal,\\n2096: \\t\\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\\n2097: \\t}\\n2098: \\n2099: \\treturn state, keyData, diags\\n2100: }\\n2101: \\n2102: // evalApplyProvisioners determines if provisioners need to be run, and if so\\n2103: // executes the provisioners for a resource and returns an updated error if\\n2104: // provisioning fails.\\n2105: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) evalApplyProvisioners(ctx EvalContext, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, createNew bool, when configs.ProvisionerWhen) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\\n2106: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2107: \\n2108: \\tif state == nil {\\n2109: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] evalApplyProvisioners: %s has no state, so skipping provisioners\\\", n.Addr)\\n2110: \\t\\treturn nil\\n2111: \\t}\\n2112: \\tif when == configs.ProvisionerWhenCreate && !createNew {\\n2113: \\t\\t// If we're not creating a new resource, then don't run provisioners\\n2114: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] evalApplyProvisioners: %s is not freshly-created, so no provisioning is required\\\", n.Addr)\\n2115: \\t\\treturn nil\\n2116: \\t}\\n2117: \\tif state.Status == states.ObjectTainted {\\n2118: \\t\\t// No point in provisioning an object that is already tainted, since\\n2119: \\t\\t// it's going to get recreated on the next apply anyway.\\n2120: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] evalApplyProvisioners: %s is tainted, so skipping provisioning\\\", n.Addr)\\n2121: \\t\\treturn nil\\n2122: \\t}\\n2123: \\n2124: \\tvar allProvs []*configs.Provisioner\\n2125: \\tswitch {\\n2126: \\tcase n.Config != nil && n.Config.Managed != nil:\\n2127: \\t\\tallProvs = n.Config.Managed.Provisioners\\n2128: \\tcase n.RemovedConfig != nil && n.RemovedConfig.Managed != nil:\\n2129: \\t\\tallProvs = n.RemovedConfig.Managed.Provisioners\\n2130: \\t}\\n2131: \\n2132: \\tprovs := filterProvisioners(allProvs, when)\\n2133: \\tif len(provs) == 0 {\\n2134: \\t\\t// We have no provisioners, so don't do anything\\n2135: \\t\\treturn nil\\n2136: \\t}\\n2137: \\n2138: \\t// Call pre hook\\n2139: \\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n2140: \\t\\treturn h.PreProvisionInstance(n.HookResourceIdentity(), state.Value)\\n2141: \\t}))\\n2142: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n2143: \\t\\treturn diags\\n2144: \\t}\\n2145: \\n2146: \\t// If there are no errors, then we append it to our output error\\n2147: \\t// if we have one, otherwise we just output it.\\n2148: \\tdiags = diags.Append(n.applyProvisioners(ctx, state, when, provs))\\n2149: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n2150: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] evalApplyProvisioners: %s provisioning failed, but we will continue anyway at the caller's request\\\", n.Addr)\\n2151: \\t\\treturn diags\\n2152: \\t}\\n2153: \\n2154: \\t// Call post hook\\n2155: \\treturn diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n2156: \\t\\treturn h.PostProvisionInstance(n.HookResourceIdentity(), state.Value)\\n2157: \\t}))\\n2158: }\\n2159: \\n2160: // filterProvisioners filters the provisioners on the resource to only\\n2161: // the provisioners specified by the \\\"when\\\" option.\\n2162: func filterProvisioners(configured []*configs.Provisioner, when configs.ProvisionerWhen) []*configs.Provisioner {\\n2163: \\tif len(configured) == 0 {\\n2164: \\t\\treturn nil\\n2165: \\t}\\n2166: \\n2167: \\tresult := make([]*configs.Provisioner, 0, len(configured))\\n2168: \\tfor _, p := range configured {\\n2169: \\t\\tif p.When == when {\\n2170: \\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, p)\\n2171: \\t\\t}\\n2172: \\t}\\n2173: \\n2174: \\treturn result\\n2175: }\\n2176: \\n2177: // applyProvisioners executes the provisioners for a resource.\\n2178: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) applyProvisioners(ctx EvalContext, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, when configs.ProvisionerWhen, provs []*configs.Provisioner) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\\n2179: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2180: \\n2181: \\t// this self is only used for destroy provisioner evaluation, and must\\n2182: \\t// refer to the last known value of the resource.\\n2183: \\tself := state.Value\\n2184: \\n2185: \\tvar evalScope func(EvalContext, hcl.Body, cty.Value, *configschema.Block) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics)\\n2186: \\tswitch when {\\n2187: \\tcase configs.ProvisionerWhenDestroy:\\n2188: \\t\\tevalScope = n.evalDestroyProvisionerConfig\\n2189: \\tdefault:\\n2190: \\t\\tevalScope = n.evalProvisionerConfig\\n2191: \\t}\\n2192: \\n2193: \\t// If there's a connection block defined directly inside the resource block\\n2194: \\t// then it'll serve as a base connection configuration for all of the\\n2195: \\t// provisioners.\\n2196: \\tvar baseConn hcl.Body\\n2197: \\tswitch {\\n2198: \\tcase n.Config != nil && n.Config.Managed != nil && n.Config.Managed.Connection != nil:\\n2199: \\t\\tbaseConn = n.Config.Managed.Connection.Config\\n2200: \\tcase n.RemovedConfig != nil && n.RemovedConfig.Managed != nil && n.RemovedConfig.Managed.Connection != nil:\\n2201: \\t\\tbaseConn = n.RemovedConfig.Managed.Connection.Config\\n2202: \\t}\\n2203: \\n2204: \\tfor _, prov := range provs {\\n2205: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] applyProvisioners: provisioning %s with %q\\\", n.Addr, prov.Type)\\n2206: \\n2207: \\t\\t// Get the provisioner\\n2208: \\t\\tprovisioner, err := ctx.Provisioner(prov.Type)\\n2209: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n2210: \\t\\t\\treturn diags.Append(err)\\n2211: \\t\\t}\\n2212: \\n2213: \\t\\tschema, err := ctx.ProvisionerSchema(prov.Type)\\n2214: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n2215: \\t\\t\\t// This error probably won't be a great diagnostic, but in practice\\n2216: \\t\\t\\t// we typically catch this problem long before we get here, so\\n2217: \\t\\t\\t// it should be rare to return via this codepath.\\n2218: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n2219: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n2220: \\t\\t}\\n2221: \\n2222: \\t\\tconfig, configDiags := evalScope(ctx, prov.Config, self, schema)\\n2223: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\\n2224: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n2225: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n2226: \\t\\t}\\n2227: \\n2228: \\t\\t// If the provisioner block contains a connection block of its own then\\n2229: \\t\\t// it can override the base connection configuration, if any.\\n2230: \\t\\tvar localConn hcl.Body\\n2231: \\t\\tif prov.Connection != nil {\\n2232: \\t\\t\\tlocalConn = prov.Connection.Config\\n2233: \\t\\t}\\n2234: \\n2235: \\t\\tvar connBody hcl.Body\\n2236: \\t\\tswitch {\\n2237: \\t\\tcase baseConn != nil && localConn != nil:\\n2238: \\t\\t\\t// Our standard merging logic applies here, similar to what we do\\n2239: \\t\\t\\t// with _override.tf configuration files: arguments from the\\n2240: \\t\\t\\t// base connection block will be masked by any arguments of the\\n2241: \\t\\t\\t// same name in the local connection block.\\n2242: \\t\\t\\tconnBody = configs.MergeBodies(baseConn, localConn)\\n2243: \\t\\tcase baseConn != nil:\\n2244: \\t\\t\\tconnBody = baseConn\\n2245: \\t\\tcase localConn != nil:\\n2246: \\t\\t\\tconnBody = localConn\\n2247: \\t\\t}\\n2248: \\n2249: \\t\\t// start with an empty connInfo\\n2250: \\t\\tconnInfo := cty.NullVal(connectionBlockSupersetSchema.ImpliedType())\\n2251: \\n2252: \\t\\tif connBody != nil {\\n2253: \\t\\t\\tvar connInfoDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2254: \\t\\t\\tconnInfo, connInfoDiags = evalScope(ctx, connBody, self, connectionBlockSupersetSchema)\\n2255: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(connInfoDiags)\\n2256: \\t\\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n2257: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n2258: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2259: \\t\\t}\\n2260: \\n2261: \\t\\t{\\n2262: \\t\\t\\t// Call pre hook\\n2263: \\t\\t\\terr := ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n2264: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn h.PreProvisionInstanceStep(n.HookResourceIdentity(), prov.Type)\\n2265: \\t\\t\\t})\\n2266: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n2267: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn diags.Append(err)\\n2268: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2269: \\t\\t}\\n2270: \\n2271: \\t\\t// The output function\\n2272: \\t\\toutputFn := func(msg string) {\\n2273: \\t\\t\\tctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n2274: \\t\\t\\t\\th.ProvisionOutput(n.HookResourceIdentity(), prov.Type, msg)\\n2275: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn HookActionContinue, nil\\n2276: \\t\\t\\t})\\n2277: \\t\\t}\\n2278: \\n2279: \\t\\t// If our config or connection info contains any marked values, ensure\\n2280: \\t\\t// those are stripped out before sending to the provisioner. Unlike\\n2281: \\t\\t// resources, we have no need to capture the marked paths and reapply\\n2282: \\t\\t// later.\\n2283: \\t\\tunmarkedConfig, configMarks := config.UnmarkDeep()\\n2284: \\t\\tunmarkedConnInfo, _ := connInfo.UnmarkDeep()\\n2285: \\n2286: \\t\\t// Marks on the config might result in leaking sensitive values through\\n2287: \\t\\t// provisioner logging, so we conservatively suppress all output in\\n2288: \\t\\t// this case. This should not apply to connection info values, which\\n2289: \\t\\t// provisioners ought not to be logging anyway.\\n2290: \\t\\tif len(configMarks) > 0 {\\n2291: \\t\\t\\toutputFn = func(msg string) {\\n2292: \\t\\t\\t\\tctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n2293: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\th.ProvisionOutput(n.HookResourceIdentity(), prov.Type, \\\"(output suppressed due to sensitive value in config)\\\")\\n2294: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn HookActionContinue, nil\\n2295: \\t\\t\\t\\t})\\n2296: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2297: \\t\\t}\\n2298: \\n2299: \\t\\toutput := CallbackUIOutput{OutputFn: outputFn}\\n2300: \\t\\tresp := provisioner.ProvisionResource(provisioners.ProvisionResourceRequest{\\n2301: \\t\\t\\tConfig:     unmarkedConfig,\\n2302: \\t\\t\\tConnection: unmarkedConnInfo,\\n2303: \\t\\t\\tUIOutput:   &output,\\n2304: \\t\\t})\\n2305: \\t\\tapplyDiags := resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(prov.Config, n.Addr.String())\\n2306: \\n2307: \\t\\t// Call post hook\\n2308: \\t\\thookErr := ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n2309: \\t\\t\\treturn h.PostProvisionInstanceStep(n.HookResourceIdentity(), prov.Type, applyDiags.Err())\\n2310: \\t\\t})\\n2311: \\n2312: \\t\\tswitch prov.OnFailure {\\n2313: \\t\\tcase configs.ProvisionerOnFailureContinue:\\n2314: \\t\\t\\tif applyDiags.HasErrors() {\\n2315: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[WARN] Errors while provisioning %s with %q, but continuing as requested in configuration\\\", n.Addr, prov.Type)\\n2316: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n2317: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Maybe there are warnings that we still want to see\\n2318: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(applyDiags)\\n2319: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2320: \\t\\tdefault:\\n2321: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(applyDiags)\\n2322: \\t\\t\\tif applyDiags.HasErrors() {\\n2323: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[WARN] Errors while provisioning %s with %q, so aborting\\\", n.Addr, prov.Type)\\n2324: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n2325: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2326: \\t\\t}\\n2327: \\n2328: \\t\\t// Deal with the hook\\n2329: \\t\\tif hookErr != nil {\\n2330: \\t\\t\\treturn diags.Append(hookErr)\\n2331: \\t\\t}\\n2332: \\t}\\n2333: \\n2334: \\treturn diags\\n2335: }\\n2336: \\n2337: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) evalProvisionerConfig(ctx EvalContext, body hcl.Body, self cty.Value, schema *configschema.Block) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n2338: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2339: \\n2340: \\tforEach, _, forEachDiags := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, false)\\n2341: \\tdiags = diags.Append(forEachDiags)\\n2342: \\n2343: \\tkeyData := EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, forEach)\\n2344: \\n2345: \\tconfig, _, configDiags := ctx.EvaluateBlock(body, schema, n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource, keyData)\\n2346: \\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\\n2347: \\n2348: \\treturn config, diags\\n2349: }\\n2350: \\n2351: // during destroy a provisioner can only evaluate within the scope of the parent resource\\n2352: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) evalDestroyProvisionerConfig(ctx EvalContext, body hcl.Body, self cty.Value, schema *configschema.Block) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n2353: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2354: \\n2355: \\t// For a destroy-time provisioner forEach is intentionally nil here,\\n2356: \\t// which EvalDataForInstanceKey responds to by not populating EachValue\\n2357: \\t// in its result. That's okay because each.value is prohibited for\\n2358: \\t// destroy-time provisioners.\\n2359: \\tkeyData := EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, nil)\\n2360: \\n2361: \\tevalScope := ctx.EvaluationScope(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource, nil, keyData)\\n2362: \\tconfig, evalDiags := evalScope.EvalSelfBlock(body, self, schema, keyData)\\n2363: \\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\\n2364: \\n2365: \\treturn config, diags\\n2366: }\\n2367: \\n2368: // apply accepts an applyConfig, instead of using n.Config, so destroy plans can\\n2369: // send a nil config. The keyData information can be empty if the config is\\n2370: // nil, since it is only used to evaluate the configuration.\\n2371: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) apply(\\n2372: \\tctx EvalContext,\\n2373: \\tstate *states.ResourceInstanceObject,\\n2374: \\tchange *plans.ResourceInstanceChange,\\n2375: \\tapplyConfig *configs.Resource,\\n2376: \\tkeyData instances.RepetitionData,\\n2377: \\tcreateBeforeDestroy bool) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n2378: \\n2379: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2380: \\tif state == nil {\\n2381: \\t\\tstate = &states.ResourceInstanceObject{}\\n2382: \\t}\\n2383: \\n2384: \\tif change.Action == plans.NoOp {\\n2385: \\t\\t// If this is a no-op change then we don't want to actually change\\n2386: \\t\\t// anything, so we'll just echo back the state we were given and\\n2387: \\t\\t// let our internal checks and updates proceed.\\n2388: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] NodeAbstractResourceInstance.apply: skipping %s because it has no planned action\\\", n.Addr)\\n2389: \\t\\treturn state, diags\\n2390: \\t}\\n2391: \\n2392: \\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n2393: \\tif err != nil {\\n2394: \\t\\treturn nil, diags.Append(err)\\n2395: \\t}\\n2396: \\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceType(n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode, n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type)\\n2397: \\tif schema == nil {\\n2398: \\t\\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\\n2399: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"provider does not support resource type %q\\\", n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type))\\n2400: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n2401: \\t}\\n2402: \\n2403: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[INFO] Starting apply for %s\\\", n.Addr)\\n2404: \\n2405: \\tconfigVal := cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType)\\n2406: \\tif applyConfig != nil {\\n2407: \\t\\tvar configDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2408: \\t\\tconfigVal, _, configDiags = ctx.EvaluateBlock(applyConfig.Config, schema, nil, keyData)\\n2409: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\\n2410: \\t\\tif configDiags.HasErrors() {\\n2411: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n2412: \\t\\t}\\n2413: \\t}\\n2414: \\n2415: \\tif !configVal.IsWhollyKnown() {\\n2416: \\t\\t// We don't have a pretty format function for a path, but since this is\\n2417: \\t\\t// such a rare error, we can just drop the raw GoString values in here\\n2418: \\t\\t// to make sure we have something to debug with.\\n2419: \\t\\tvar unknownPaths []string\\n2420: \\t\\tcty.Transform(configVal, func(p cty.Path, v cty.Value) (cty.Value, error) {\\n2421: \\t\\t\\tif !v.IsKnown() {\\n2422: \\t\\t\\t\\tunknownPaths = append(unknownPaths, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%#v\\\", p))\\n2423: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2424: \\t\\t\\treturn v, nil\\n2425: \\t\\t})\\n2426: \\n2427: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n2428: \\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n2429: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Configuration contains unknown value\\\",\\n2430: \\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"configuration for %s still contains unknown values during apply (this is a bug in Terraform; please report it!)\\\\n\\\"+\\n2431: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"The following paths in the resource configuration are unknown:\\\\n%s\\\",\\n2432: \\t\\t\\t\\tn.Addr,\\n2433: \\t\\t\\t\\tstrings.Join(unknownPaths, \\\"\\\\n\\\"),\\n2434: \\t\\t\\t),\\n2435: \\t\\t))\\n2436: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n2437: \\t}\\n2438: \\n2439: \\tmetaConfigVal, metaDiags := n.providerMetas(ctx)\\n2440: \\tdiags = diags.Append(metaDiags)\\n2441: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n2442: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n2443: \\t}\\n2444: \\n2445: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[DEBUG] %s: applying the planned %s change\\\", n.Addr, change.Action)\\n2446: \\n2447: \\t// If our config, Before or After value contain any marked values,\\n2448: \\t// ensure those are stripped out before sending\\n2449: \\t// this to the provider\\n2450: \\tunmarkedConfigVal, _ := configVal.UnmarkDeep()\\n2451: \\tunmarkedBefore, beforePaths := change.Before.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\\n2452: \\tunmarkedAfter, afterPaths := change.After.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\\n2453: \\n2454: \\t// If we have an Update action, our before and after values are equal,\\n2455: \\t// and only differ on their sensitivity, the newVal is the after val\\n2456: \\t// and we should not communicate with the provider. We do need to update\\n2457: \\t// the state with this new value, to ensure the sensitivity change is\\n2458: \\t// persisted.\\n2459: \\teqV := unmarkedBefore.Equals(unmarkedAfter)\\n2460: \\teq := eqV.IsKnown() && eqV.True()\\n2461: \\tif change.Action == plans.Update && eq && !marks.MarksEqual(beforePaths, afterPaths) {\\n2462: \\t\\t// Copy the previous state, changing only the value\\n2463: \\t\\tnewState := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\n2464: \\t\\t\\tCreateBeforeDestroy: state.CreateBeforeDestroy,\\n2465: \\t\\t\\tDependencies:        state.Dependencies,\\n2466: \\t\\t\\tPrivate:             state.Private,\\n2467: \\t\\t\\tStatus:              state.Status,\\n2468: \\t\\t\\tValue:               change.After,\\n2469: \\t\\t}\\n2470: \\t\\treturn newState, diags\\n2471: \\t}\\n2472: \\n2473: \\tvar resp providers.ApplyResourceChangeResponse\\n2474: \\tif n.override != nil {\\n2475: \\t\\t// As with the planning stage, we only need to worry about computed\\n2476: \\t\\t// values the first time the object is created. Otherwise, we're happy\\n2477: \\t\\t// to just apply whatever the user asked for.\\n2478: \\t\\tif change.Action == plans.Create {\\n2479: \\t\\t\\toverride, overrideDiags := mocking.ApplyComputedValuesForResource(unmarkedAfter, mocking.MockedData{\\n2480: \\t\\t\\t\\tValue: n.override.Values,\\n2481: \\t\\t\\t\\tRange: n.override.ValuesRange,\\n2482: \\t\\t\\t}, schema)\\n2483: \\t\\t\\tresp = providers.ApplyResourceChangeResponse{\\n2484: \\t\\t\\t\\tNewState:    override,\\n2485: \\t\\t\\t\\tDiagnostics: overrideDiags,\\n2486: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2487: \\t\\t} else {\\n2488: \\t\\t\\tresp = providers.ApplyResourceChangeResponse{\\n2489: \\t\\t\\t\\tNewState: unmarkedAfter,\\n2490: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2491: \\t\\t}\\n2492: \\t} else {\\n2493: \\t\\tresp = provider.ApplyResourceChange(providers.ApplyResourceChangeRequest{\\n2494: \\t\\t\\tTypeName:       n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\\n2495: \\t\\t\\tPriorState:     unmarkedBefore,\\n2496: \\t\\t\\tConfig:         unmarkedConfigVal,\\n2497: \\t\\t\\tPlannedState:   unmarkedAfter,\\n2498: \\t\\t\\tPlannedPrivate: change.Private,\\n2499: \\t\\t\\tProviderMeta:   metaConfigVal,\\n2500: \\t\\t})\\n2501: \\t}\\n2502: \\tapplyDiags := resp.Diagnostics\\n2503: \\tif applyConfig != nil {\\n2504: \\t\\tapplyDiags = applyDiags.InConfigBody(applyConfig.Config, n.Addr.String())\\n2505: \\t}\\n2506: \\tdiags = diags.Append(applyDiags)\\n2507: \\n2508: \\t// Even if there are errors in the returned diagnostics, the provider may\\n2509: \\t// have returned a _partial_ state for an object that already exists but\\n2510: \\t// failed to fully configure, and so the remaining code must always run\\n2511: \\t// to completion but must be defensive against the new value being\\n2512: \\t// incomplete.\\n2513: \\tnewVal := resp.NewState\\n2514: \\n2515: \\tif newVal == cty.NilVal {\\n2516: \\t\\t// Providers are supposed to return a partial new value even when errors\\n2517: \\t\\t// occur, but sometimes they don't and so in that case we'll patch that up\\n2518: \\t\\t// by just using the prior state, so we'll at least keep track of the\\n2519: \\t\\t// object for the user to retry.\\n2520: \\t\\tnewVal = change.Before\\n2521: \\n2522: \\t\\t// As a special case, we'll set the new value to null if it looks like\\n2523: \\t\\t// we were trying to execute a delete, because the provider in this case\\n2524: \\t\\t// probably left the newVal unset intending it to be interpreted as \\\"null\\\".\\n2525: \\t\\tif change.After.IsNull() {\\n2526: \\t\\t\\tnewVal = cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType())\\n2527: \\t\\t}\\n2528: \\n2529: \\t\\tif !diags.HasErrors() {\\n2530: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n2531: \\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n2532: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider produced invalid object\\\",\\n2533: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n2534: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider %q produced an invalid nil value after apply for %s.\\\\n\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\",\\n2535: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tn.ResolvedProvider.String(), n.Addr.String(),\\n2536: \\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n2537: \\t\\t\\t))\\n2538: \\t\\t}\\n2539: \\t}\\n2540: \\n2541: \\tvar conformDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2542: \\tfor _, err := range newVal.Type().TestConformance(schema.ImpliedType()) {\\n2543: \\t\\tconformDiags = conformDiags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n2544: \\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n2545: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider produced invalid object\\\",\\n2546: \\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n2547: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider %q produced an invalid value after apply for %s. The result cannot not be saved in the Terraform state.\\\\n\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\",\\n2548: \\t\\t\\t\\tn.ResolvedProvider.String(), tfdiags.FormatErrorPrefixed(err, n.Addr.String()),\\n2549: \\t\\t\\t),\\n2550: \\t\\t))\\n2551: \\t}\\n2552: \\tdiags = diags.Append(conformDiags)\\n2553: \\tif conformDiags.HasErrors() {\\n2554: \\t\\t// Bail early in this particular case, because an object that doesn't\\n2555: \\t\\t// conform to the schema can't be saved in the state anyway -- the\\n2556: \\t\\t// serializer will reject it.\\n2557: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n2558: \\t}\\n2559: \\n2560: \\t// After this point we have a type-conforming result object and so we\\n2561: \\t// must always run to completion to ensure it can be saved. If n.Error\\n2562: \\t// is set then we must not return a non-nil error, in order to allow\\n2563: \\t// evaluation to continue to a later point where our state object will\\n2564: \\t// be saved.\\n2565: \\n2566: \\t// By this point there must not be any unknown values remaining in our\\n2567: \\t// object, because we've applied the change and we can't save unknowns\\n2568: \\t// in our persistent state. If any are present then we will indicate an\\n2569: \\t// error (which is always a bug in the provider) but we will also replace\\n2570: \\t// them with nulls so that we can successfully save the portions of the\\n2571: \\t// returned value that are known.\\n2572: \\tif !newVal.IsWhollyKnown() {\\n2573: \\t\\t// To generate better error messages, we'll go for a walk through the\\n2574: \\t\\t// value and make a separate diagnostic for each unknown value we\\n2575: \\t\\t// find.\\n2576: \\t\\tcty.Walk(newVal, func(path cty.Path, val cty.Value) (bool, error) {\\n2577: \\t\\t\\tif !val.IsKnown() {\\n2578: \\t\\t\\t\\tpathStr := tfdiags.FormatCtyPath(path)\\n2579: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n2580: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n2581: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider returned invalid result object after apply\\\",\\n2582: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n2583: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"After the apply operation, the provider still indicated an unknown value for %s%s. All values must be known after apply, so this is always a bug in the provider and should be reported in the provider's own repository. Terraform will still save the other known object values in the state.\\\",\\n2584: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tn.Addr, pathStr,\\n2585: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n2586: \\t\\t\\t\\t))\\n2587: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2588: \\t\\t\\treturn true, nil\\n2589: \\t\\t})\\n2590: \\n2591: \\t\\t// NOTE: This operation can potentially be lossy if there are multiple\\n2592: \\t\\t// elements in a set that differ only by unknown values: after\\n2593: \\t\\t// replacing with null these will be merged together into a single set\\n2594: \\t\\t// element. Since we can only get here in the presence of a provider\\n2595: \\t\\t// bug, we accept this because storing a result here is always a\\n2596: \\t\\t// best-effort sort of thing.\\n2597: \\t\\tnewVal = cty.UnknownAsNull(newVal)\\n2598: \\t}\\n2599: \\n2600: \\t// If we have paths to mark, mark those on this new value we need to\\n2601: \\t// re-check the value against the schema, because nested computed values\\n2602: \\t// won't be included in afterPaths, which are only what was read from the\\n2603: \\t// After plan value.\\n2604: \\tnewVal = newVal.MarkWithPaths(afterPaths)\\n2605: \\tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(newVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\\n2606: \\t\\tnewVal = marks.MarkPaths(newVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\\n2607: \\t}\\n2608: \\n2609: \\tif change.Action != plans.Delete && !diags.HasErrors() {\\n2610: \\t\\t// Only values that were marked as unknown in the planned value are allowed\\n2611: \\t\\t// to change during the apply operation. (We do this after the unknown-ness\\n2612: \\t\\t// check above so that we also catch anything that became unknown after\\n2613: \\t\\t// being known during plan.)\\n2614: \\t\\t//\\n2615: \\t\\t// If we are returning other errors anyway then we'll give this\\n2616: \\t\\t// a pass since the other errors are usually the explanation for\\n2617: \\t\\t// this one and so it's more helpful to let the user focus on the\\n2618: \\t\\t// root cause rather than distract with this extra problem.\\n2619: \\t\\tif errs := objchange.AssertObjectCompatible(schema, change.After, newVal); len(errs) > 0 {\\n2620: \\t\\t\\tif resp.LegacyTypeSystem {\\n2621: \\t\\t\\t\\t// The shimming of the old type system in the legacy SDK is not precise\\n2622: \\t\\t\\t\\t// enough to pass this consistency check, so we'll give it a pass here,\\n2623: \\t\\t\\t\\t// but we will generate a warning about it so that we are more likely\\n2624: \\t\\t\\t\\t// to notice in the logs if an inconsistency beyond the type system\\n2625: \\t\\t\\t\\t// leads to a downstream provider failure.\\n2626: \\t\\t\\t\\tvar buf strings.Builder\\n2627: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \\\"[WARN] Provider %q produced an unexpected new value for %s, but we are tolerating it because it is using the legacy plugin SDK.\\\\n    The following problems may be the cause of any confusing errors from downstream operations:\\\", n.ResolvedProvider.String(), n.Addr)\\n2628: \\t\\t\\t\\tfor _, err := range errs {\\n2629: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \\\"\\\\n      - %s\\\", tfdiags.FormatError(err))\\n2630: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n2631: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Print(buf.String())\\n2632: \\n2633: \\t\\t\\t\\t// The sort of inconsistency we won't catch here is if a known value\\n2634: \\t\\t\\t\\t// in the plan is changed during apply. That can cause downstream\\n2635: \\t\\t\\t\\t// problems because a dependent resource would make its own plan based\\n2636: \\t\\t\\t\\t// on the planned value, and thus get a different result during the\\n2637: \\t\\t\\t\\t// apply phase. This will usually lead to a \\\"Provider produced invalid plan\\\"\\n2638: \\t\\t\\t\\t// error that incorrectly blames the downstream resource for the change.\\n2639: \\n2640: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n2641: \\t\\t\\t\\tfor _, err := range errs {\\n2642: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n2643: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n2644: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider produced inconsistent result after apply\\\",\\n2645: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n2646: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"When applying changes to %s, provider %q produced an unexpected new value: %s.\\\\n\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\",\\n2647: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tn.Addr, n.ResolvedProvider.String(), tfdiags.FormatError(err),\\n2648: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n2649: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t))\\n2650: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n2651: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2652: \\t\\t}\\n2653: \\t}\\n2654: \\n2655: \\t// If a provider returns a null or non-null object at the wrong time then\\n2656: \\t// we still want to save that but it often causes some confusing behaviors\\n2657: \\t// where it seems like Terraform is failing to take any action at all,\\n2658: \\t// so we'll generate some errors to draw attention to it.\\n2659: \\tif !diags.HasErrors() {\\n2660: \\t\\tif change.Action == plans.Delete && !newVal.IsNull() {\\n2661: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n2662: \\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n2663: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider returned invalid result object after apply\\\",\\n2664: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n2665: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"After applying a %s plan, the provider returned a non-null object for %s. Destroying should always produce a null value, so this is always a bug in the provider and should be reported in the provider's own repository. Terraform will still save this errant object in the state for debugging and recovery.\\\",\\n2666: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tchange.Action, n.Addr,\\n2667: \\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n2668: \\t\\t\\t))\\n2669: \\t\\t}\\n2670: \\t\\tif change.Action != plans.Delete && newVal.IsNull() {\\n2671: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n2672: \\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n2673: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider returned invalid result object after apply\\\",\\n2674: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n2675: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"After applying a %s plan, the provider returned a null object for %s. Only destroying should always produce a null value, so this is always a bug in the provider and should be reported in the provider's own repository.\\\",\\n2676: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tchange.Action, n.Addr,\\n2677: \\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n2678: \\t\\t\\t))\\n2679: \\t\\t}\\n2680: \\t}\\n2681: \\n2682: \\tswitch {\\n2683: \\tcase diags.HasErrors() && newVal.IsNull():\\n2684: \\t\\t// Sometimes providers return a null value when an operation fails for\\n2685: \\t\\t// some reason, but we'd rather keep the prior state so that the error\\n2686: \\t\\t// can be corrected on a subsequent run. We must only do this for null\\n2687: \\t\\t// new value though, or else we may discard partial updates the\\n2688: \\t\\t// provider was able to complete. Otherwise, we'll continue using the\\n2689: \\t\\t// prior state as the new value, making this effectively a no-op.  If\\n2690: \\t\\t// the item really _has_ been deleted then our next refresh will detect\\n2691: \\t\\t// that and fix it up.\\n2692: \\t\\treturn state.DeepCopy(), diags\\n2693: \\n2694: \\tcase diags.HasErrors() && !newVal.IsNull():\\n2695: \\t\\t// if we have an error, make sure we restore the object status in the new state\\n2696: \\t\\tnewState := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\n2697: \\t\\t\\tStatus:              state.Status,\\n2698: \\t\\t\\tValue:               newVal,\\n2699: \\t\\t\\tPrivate:             resp.Private,\\n2700: \\t\\t\\tCreateBeforeDestroy: createBeforeDestroy,\\n2701: \\t\\t}\\n2702: \\n2703: \\t\\t// if the resource was being deleted, the dependencies are not going to\\n2704: \\t\\t// be recalculated and we need to restore those as well.\\n2705: \\t\\tif change.Action == plans.Delete {\\n2706: \\t\\t\\tnewState.Dependencies = state.Dependencies\\n2707: \\t\\t}\\n2708: \\n2709: \\t\\treturn newState, diags\\n2710: \\n2711: \\tcase !newVal.IsNull():\\n2712: \\t\\t// Non error case with a new state\\n2713: \\t\\tnewState := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\n2714: \\t\\t\\tStatus:              states.ObjectReady,\\n2715: \\t\\t\\tValue:               newVal,\\n2716: \\t\\t\\tPrivate:             resp.Private,\\n2717: \\t\\t\\tCreateBeforeDestroy: createBeforeDestroy,\\n2718: \\t\\t}\\n2719: \\t\\treturn newState, diags\\n2720: \\n2721: \\tdefault:\\n2722: \\t\\t// Non error case, were the object was deleted\\n2723: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n2724: \\t}\\n2725: }\\n2726: \\n2727: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) prevRunAddr(ctx EvalContext) addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\\n2728: \\treturn resourceInstancePrevRunAddr(ctx, n.Addr)\\n2729: }\\n2730: \\n2731: func resourceInstancePrevRunAddr(ctx EvalContext, currentAddr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\\n2732: \\ttable := ctx.MoveResults()\\n2733: \\treturn table.OldAddr(currentAddr)\\n2734: }\\n2735: \\n2736: func getAction(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, priorVal, plannedNewVal cty.Value, createBeforeDestroy bool, forceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance, reqRep cty.PathSet) (action plans.Action, actionReason plans.ResourceInstanceChangeActionReason) {\\n2737: \\t// The user might also ask us to force replacing a particular resource\\n2738: \\t// instance, regardless of whether the provider thinks it needs replacing.\\n2739: \\t// For example, users typically do this if they learn a particular object\\n2740: \\t// has become degraded in an immutable infrastructure scenario and so\\n2741: \\t// replacing it with a new object is a viable repair path.\\n2742: \\tmatchedForceReplace := false\\n2743: \\tfor _, candidateAddr := range forceReplace {\\n2744: \\t\\tif candidateAddr.Equal(addr) {\\n2745: \\t\\t\\tmatchedForceReplace = true\\n2746: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n2747: \\t\\t}\\n2748: \\n2749: \\t\\t// For \\\"force replace\\\" purposes we require an exact resource instance\\n2750: \\t\\t// address to match. If a user forgets to include the instance key\\n2751: \\t\\t// for a multi-instance resource then it won't match here, but we\\n2752: \\t\\t// have an earlier check in NodePlannableResource.Execute that should\\n2753: \\t\\t// prevent us from getting here in that case.\\n2754: \\t}\\n2755: \\n2756: \\t// Unmark for this test for value equality.\\n2757: \\teqV := plannedNewVal.Equals(priorVal)\\n2758: \\teq := eqV.IsKnown() && eqV.True()\\n2759: \\n2760: \\tswitch {\\n2761: \\tcase priorVal.IsNull():\\n2762: \\t\\taction = plans.Create\\n2763: \\tcase eq && !matchedForceReplace:\\n2764: \\t\\taction = plans.NoOp\\n2765: \\tcase matchedForceReplace || !reqRep.Empty():\\n2766: \\t\\t// If the user \\\"forced replace\\\" of this instance of if there are any\\n2767: \\t\\t// \\\"requires replace\\\" paths left _after our filtering above_ then this\\n2768: \\t\\t// is a replace action.\\n2769: \\t\\tif createBeforeDestroy {\\n2770: \\t\\t\\taction = plans.CreateThenDelete\\n2771: \\t\\t} else {\\n2772: \\t\\t\\taction = plans.DeleteThenCreate\\n2773: \\t\\t}\\n2774: \\t\\tswitch {\\n2775: \\t\\tcase matchedForceReplace:\\n2776: \\t\\t\\tactionReason = plans.ResourceInstanceReplaceByRequest\\n2777: \\t\\tcase !reqRep.Empty():\\n2778: \\t\\t\\tactionReason = plans.ResourceInstanceReplaceBecauseCannotUpdate\\n2779: \\t\\t}\\n2780: \\tdefault:\\n2781: \\t\\taction = plans.Update\\n2782: \\t\\t// \\\"Delete\\\" is never chosen here, because deletion plans are always\\n2783: \\t\\t// created more directly elsewhere, such as in \\\"orphan\\\" handling.\\n2784: \\t}\\n2785: \\n2786: \\treturn\\n2787: }\\n2788: \\n2789: // getRequiredReplaces returns a list of paths to attributes whose changes mean\\n2790: // that we must replace rather than update an existing remote object.\\n2791: //\\n2792: // The provider produces a list of paths to attributes whose changes mean\\n2793: // that we must replace rather than update an existing remote object.\\n2794: // However, we only need to do that if the identified attributes _have_\\n2795: // actually changed -- particularly after we may have undone some of the\\n2796: // changes in processIgnoreChanges -- so now we'll filter that list to\\n2797: // include only where changes are detected.\\n2798: //\\n2799: // Both the priorVal and plannedNewVal should be unmarked before calling this\\n2800: // function. This function exposes nothing about the priorVal or plannedVal\\n2801: // except for the paths that require replacement which can be deduced from the\\n2802: // type with or without marks.\\n2803: func getRequiredReplaces(priorVal, plannedNewVal cty.Value, requiredReplaces []cty.Path, providerAddr tfaddr.Provider, addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) (cty.PathSet, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n2804: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2805: \\n2806: \\treqRep := cty.NewPathSet()\\n2807: \\tif len(requiredReplaces) > 0 {\\n2808: \\t\\tfor _, path := range requiredReplaces {\\n2809: \\t\\t\\tif priorVal.IsNull() {\\n2810: \\t\\t\\t\\t// If prior is null then we don't expect any RequiresReplace at all,\\n2811: \\t\\t\\t\\t// because this is a Create action.\\n2812: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n2813: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2814: \\n2815: \\t\\t\\tpriorChangedVal, priorPathDiags := hcl.ApplyPath(priorVal, path, nil)\\n2816: \\t\\t\\tplannedChangedVal, plannedPathDiags := hcl.ApplyPath(plannedNewVal, path, nil)\\n2817: \\t\\t\\tif plannedPathDiags.HasErrors() && priorPathDiags.HasErrors() {\\n2818: \\t\\t\\t\\t// This means the path was invalid in both the prior and new\\n2819: \\t\\t\\t\\t// values, which is an error with the provider itself.\\n2820: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n2821: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n2822: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider produced invalid plan\\\",\\n2823: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n2824: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider %q has indicated \\\\\\\"requires replacement\\\\\\\" on %s for a non-existent attribute path %#v.\\\\n\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\",\\n2825: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tproviderAddr, addr, path,\\n2826: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n2827: \\t\\t\\t\\t))\\n2828: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n2829: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2830: \\n2831: \\t\\t\\t// Make sure we have valid Values for both values.\\n2832: \\t\\t\\t// Note: if the opposing value was of the type\\n2833: \\t\\t\\t// cty.DynamicPseudoType, the type assigned here may not exactly\\n2834: \\t\\t\\t// match the schema. This is fine here, since we're only going to\\n2835: \\t\\t\\t// check for equality, but if the NullVal is to be used, we need to\\n2836: \\t\\t\\t// check the schema for th true type.\\n2837: \\t\\t\\tswitch {\\n2838: \\t\\t\\tcase priorChangedVal == cty.NilVal && plannedChangedVal == cty.NilVal:\\n2839: \\t\\t\\t\\t// this should never happen without ApplyPath errors above\\n2840: \\t\\t\\t\\tpanic(\\\"requires replace path returned 2 nil values\\\")\\n2841: \\t\\t\\tcase priorChangedVal == cty.NilVal:\\n2842: \\t\\t\\t\\tpriorChangedVal = cty.NullVal(plannedChangedVal.Type())\\n2843: \\t\\t\\tcase plannedChangedVal == cty.NilVal:\\n2844: \\t\\t\\t\\tplannedChangedVal = cty.NullVal(priorChangedVal.Type())\\n2845: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2846: \\n2847: \\t\\t\\teqV := plannedChangedVal.Equals(priorChangedVal)\\n2848: \\t\\t\\tif !eqV.IsKnown() || eqV.False() {\\n2849: \\t\\t\\t\\treqRep.Add(path)\\n2850: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2851: \\t\\t}\\n2852: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n2853: \\t\\t\\treturn reqRep, diags\\n2854: \\t\\t}\\n2855: \\t}\\n2856: \\n2857: \\treturn reqRep, diags\\n2858: }\\n2859: \", \"url\": \"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go\"}, \"isError\": false}"
