must also depend on the destruction of any of its\\n61: \\t// dependencies, since this may for example affect the outcome of\\n62: \\t// evaluating an entire list of resources with \\\"count\\\" set (by reducing\\n63: \\t// the count).\\n64: \\t//\\n65: \\t// However, we can't do this in create_before_destroy mode because that\\n66: \\t// would create a dependency cycle. We make a compromise here of requiring\\n67: \\t// changes to be updated across two applies in this case, since the first\\n68: \\t// plan will use the old values.\\n69: \\tif !n.CreateBeforeDestroy() {\\n70: \\t\\tfor _, ref := range ret {\\n71: \\t\\t\\tswitch tr := ref.Subject.(type) {\\n72: \\t\\t\\tcase addrs.ResourceInstance:\\n73: \\t\\t\\t\\tnewRef := *ref // shallow copy so we can mutate\\n74: \\t\\t\\t\\tnewRef.Subject = tr.Phase(addrs.ResourceInstancePhaseDestroy)\\n75: \\t\\t\\t\\tnewRef.Remaining = nil // can't access attributes of something being destroyed\\n76: \\t\\t\\t\\tret = append(ret, &newRef)\\n77: \\t\\t\\tcase addrs.Resource:\\n78: \\t\\t\\t\\tnewRef := *ref // shallow copy so we can mutate\\n79: \\t\\t\\t\\tnewRef.Subject = tr.Phase(addrs.ResourceInstancePhaseDestroy)\\n80: \\t\\t\\t\\tnewRef.Remaining = nil // can't access attributes of something being destroyed\\n81: \\t\\t\\t\\tret = append(ret, &newRef)\\n82: \\t\\t\\t}\\n83: \\t\\t}\\n84: \\t}\\n85: \\n86: \\treturn ret\\n87: }\\n88: \\n89: // GraphNodeAttachDependencies\\n90: func (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) AttachDependencies(deps []addrs.ConfigResource) {\\n91: \\tn.Dependencies = deps\\n92: }\\n93: \\n94: // GraphNodeExecutable\\n95: func (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n96: \\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\\n97: \\n98: \\tif n.Config == nil {\\n99: \\t\\t// If there is no config, and there is no change, then we have nothing\\n100: \\t\\t// to do and the change was left in the plan for informational\\n101: \", \"url\": \"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/node_resource_apply_instance.go%3FL55-100\"}, \"isError\": false}"

true\\\\n46: \\\\t}\\\\n47: \\\\treturn n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.ProvidedBy()\\\\n48: }\\\\n49: \\\\n50: // GraphNodeDestroyer\\\\n51: func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) DestroyAddr() *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) CreateBeforeDestroy() bool {\\\\n58: \\\\t// State takes precedence during destroy.\\\\n59: \\\\t// If the resource was removed, there is no config to check.\\\\n60: \\\\t// If CBD was forced from descendant, it should be saved in the state\\\\n61: \\\\t// already.\\\\n62: \\\\tif s := n.instanceState; s != nil {\\\\n63: \\\\t\\\\tif s.Current != nil {\\\\n64: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn s.Current.CreateBeforeDestroy\\\\n65: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n66: \\\\t}\\\\n67: \\\\n68: \\\\tif n.Config != nil \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 n.Config.Managed != nil {\\\\n69: \\\\t\\\\treturn n.Config.Managed.CreateBeforeDestroy\\\\n70: \\\\t}\\\\n71: \\\\n72: \\\\treturn false\\\\n73: }\\\\n74: \\\\n75: // GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\\\\n76: func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) ModifyCreateBeforeDestroy(v bool) error {\\\\n77: \\\\treturn nil\\\\n78: }\\\\n79: \\\\n80: // GraphNodeReferenceable, overriding NodeAbstractResource\\\\n81: func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) ReferenceableAddrs() []addrs.Referenceable {\\\\n82: \\\\tnormalAddrs := n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.ReferenceableAddrs()\\\\n83: \\\\tdestroyAddrs := make([]addrs.Referenceable, len(normalAddrs))\\\\n84: \\\\n85: \\\\tphaseType := addrs.ResourceInstancePhaseDestroy\\\\n86: \\\\tif n.CreateBeforeDestroy() {\\\\n87: \\\\t\\\\tphaseType = addrs.ResourceInstancePhaseDestroyCBD\\\\n88: \\\\t}\\\\n89: \\\\n90: \\\\tfor i, normalAddr := range normalAddrs {\\\\n91: \\\\t\\\\tswitch ta := normalAddr.(type) {\\\\n92: \\\\t\\\\tcase addrs.Resource:\\\\n93: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdestroyAddrs[i] = ta.Phase(phaseType)\\\\n94: \\\\t\\\\tcase addrs.ResourceInstance:\\\\n95: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdestroyAddrs[i] = ta.Phase(phaseType)\\\\n96: \\\\t\\\\tdefault:\\\\n97: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdestroyAddrs[i] = normalAddr\\\\n98: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n99: \\\\t}\\\\n100: \\\\n101: \\\\treturn destroyAddrs\\\\n102: }\\\\n103: \\\\n104: // \\\\tdefault:\\\\n140: \\\\t\\\\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"unsupported resource mode %s\\\\\\\", n.Config.Mode))\\\\n141: \\\\t}\\\\n142: }\\\\n143: \\\\n144: func (n *NodeDestroyResourceInstance) n.instanceState\\\\n149: \\\\tif is == nil {\\\\n150: \\\\t\\\\tlog.Printf(\\\\\\\"[WARN] NodeDestroyResourceInstance for %s with no state\\\\\\\", addr)\\\\n151: \\\\t}\\\\n152: \\\\n153: \\\\t// is\\n134: \\tswitch addr.Resource.Resource.Mode {\\n135: \\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\\n136: \\t\\treturn n.managedResourceExecute(ctx)\\n137: \\tcase addrs.DataResourceMode:\\n138: \\t\\treturn n.dataResourceExecute(ctx)\\n139: \\tdefault:\\n140: \\t\\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\\\"unsupported resource mode %s\\\", n.Config.Mode))\\n141: \\t}\\n142: }\\n143: \\n144: func (n n.instanceState\\n149: \\tif is == nil {\\n150: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[WARN] NodeDestroyResourceInstance for %s with no state\\\", addr)\\n151: \\t}\\n152: \\n153: \\t// These vars are updated through getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n158: \\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n159: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n160: \\t\\treturn diags\\n161: \\t}\\n162: \\n163: \\tchangeApply, err = n.readDiff(ctx, reducePlan(addr.Resource, changeApply, true)\\n170: \\t// reducePlan may have simplified our planned change\\n171: \\t// into a NoOp if it does not require destroying.\\n172: \\tif changeApply == nil || changeApply.Action == plans.NoOp {\\n173: \\t\\treturn diags\\n174: \\t}\\n175: \\n176: \\tstate, readDiags := n.readResourceInstanceState(ctx, addr)\\n177: \\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\\n178: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n179: \\t\\treturn diags\\n180: \\t}\\n181: \\n182: \\t// Exit early if the state object is null after reading the state\\n183: \\tif state == nil || state.Value.IsNull() {\\n184: \\t\\treturn diags\\n185: \\t}\\n186: \\n187: \\tdiags = diags.Append(n.preApplyHook(ctx, changeApply))\\n188: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n189: \\t\\treturn diags\\n190: \\t}\\n191: \\n192: \\t// Run destroy provisioners if not tainted\\n193: \\tif state.Status != states.ObjectTainted {\\n194: \\t\\tapplyProvisionersDiags := n.evalApplyProvisioners(ctx, \\n198: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n199: \\t\\t\\t// If we have a provisioning error, then we just call\\n200: \\t\\t\\t// the post-apply hook now.\\n201: \\t\\t\\tdiags = {
  "command": "create",
  "file_text": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2\"\n\ttfaddr \"github.com/hashicorp/terraform-registry-address\"\n\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/checks\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang/marks\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/deferring\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/objchange\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/provisioners\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// NodeResourceInstanceBase represents a resource instance with no\n// associated operations. It embeds NodeAbstractResource but additionally\n// contains an instance key, used to identify one of potentially many\n// instances that were created from a resource in configuration, e.g. using\n// the \"count\" or \"for_each\" arguments.\ntype NodeResourceInstanceBase struct {\n\tNodeAbstractResource\n\tAddr addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\n\t// These are set via the AttachState method.\n\tinstanceState *states.ResourceInstance\n\n\tDependencies []addrs.ConfigResource\n\n\tpreDestroyRefresh bool\n\n\t// During import we may generate configuration for a resource, which needs\n\t// to be stored in the final change.\n\tgeneratedConfigHCL string\n\n\t// override is set by the graph itself, just before this node executes.\n\toverride *configs.Override\n}\n\n// NewNodeResourceInstanceBase creates a resource instance base graph\n// node for the given absolute resource instance address.\nfunc NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) *NodeResourceInstanceBase {\n\t// Due to the fact that we embed NodeAbstractResource, the given address\n\t// actually ends up split between the resource address in the embedded\n\t// object and the InstanceKey field in our own struct. The\n\t// ResourceInstanceAddr method will stick these back together again on\n\t// request.\n\tr := NewNodeAbstractResource(addr.ContainingResource().Config())\n\treturn &NodeResourceInstanceBase{\n\t\tNodeAbstractResource: *r,\n\t\tAddr:                 addr,\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) Name() string {\n\treturn n.ResourceInstanceAddr().String()\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) Path() addrs.ModuleInstance {\n\treturn n.Addr.Module\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) HookResourceIdentity() HookResourceIdentity {\n\treturn HookResourceIdentity{\n\t\tAddr:         n.Addr,\n\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider.Provider,\n\t}\n}\n\n// GraphNodeReferenceable\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) ReferenceableAddrs() []addrs.Referenceable {\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\treturn []addrs.Referenceable{\n\t\taddr.Resource,\n\n\t\t// A resource instance can also be referenced by the address of its\n\t\t// containing resource, so that e.g. a reference to aws_instance.foo\n\t\t// would match both aws_instance.foo[0] and aws_instance.foo[1].\n\t\taddr.ContainingResource().Resource,\n\t}\n}\n\n// GraphNodeReferencer\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n\t// If we have a configuration attached then we'll delegate to our\n\t// embedded abstract resource, which knows how to extract dependencies\n\t// from configuration. If there is no config, then the dependencies will\n\t// be connected during destroy from those stored in the state.\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\tif n.Schema == nil {\n\t\t\t// We'll produce a log message about this out here so that\n\t\t\t// we can include the full instance address, since the equivalent\n\t\t\t// message in NodeAbstractResource.References cannot see it.\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] no schema is attached to %s, so config references cannot be detected\", n.Name())\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn n.NodeAbstractResource.References()\n\t}\n\n\t// If we have neither config nor state then we have no references.\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// StateDependencies returns the dependencies which will be saved in the state\n// for managed resources, or the most current dependencies for data resources.\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) StateDependencies() []addrs.ConfigResource {\n\t// Managed resources prefer the stored dependencies, to avoid possible\n\t// conflicts in ordering when refactoring configuration.\n\tif s := n.instanceState; s != nil {\n\t\tif s.Current != nil {\n\t\t\treturn s.Current.Dependencies\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If there are no stored dependencies, this is either a newly created\n\t// managed resource, or a data source, and we can use the most recently\n\t// calculated dependencies.\n\treturn n.Dependencies\n}\n\n// GraphNodeResourceInstance\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) ResourceInstanceAddr() addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n\treturn n.Addr\n}\n\n// GraphNodeAttachResourceState\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) AttachResourceState(s *states.Resource) {\n\tif s == nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] attaching nil state to %s\", n.Addr)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] NodeResourceInstanceBase.AttachResourceState for %s\", n.Addr)\n\tn.instanceState = s.Instance(n.Addr.Resource.Key)\n\tn.storedProviderConfig = s.ProviderConfig\n}\n\n// GraphNodeOverridable\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) ConfigProvider() addrs.AbsProviderConfig {\n\treturn n.ResolvedProvider\n}\n\n// GraphNodeOverridable\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) SetOverride(override *configs.Override) {\n\tn.override = override\n}\n\n// readDiff returns the planned change for a particular resource instance\n// object.\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) readDiff(ctx EvalContext, providerSchema providers.ProviderSchema) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, error) {\n\tchanges := ctx.Changes()\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\n\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(addr.Resource.Resource)\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"provider does not support resource type %q\", addr.Resource.Resource.Type)\n\t}\n\n\tchange := changes.GetResourceInstanceChange(addr, addrs.NotDeposed)\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readDiff: Read %s change from plan for %s\", change.Action, n.Addr)\n\n\treturn change, nil\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) checkPreventDestroy(change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tif change == nil || n.Config == nil || n.Config.Managed == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tpreventDestroy := n.Config.Managed.PreventDestroy\n\n\tif (change.Action == plans.Delete || change.Action.IsReplace()) && preventDestroy {\n\t\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n\t\t\tSummary:  \"Instance cannot be destroyed\",\n\t\t\tDetail: fmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\"Resource %s has lifecycle.prevent_destroy set, but the plan calls for this resource to be destroyed. To avoid this error and continue with the plan, either disable lifecycle.prevent_destroy or reduce the scope of the plan using the -target option.\",\n\t\t\t\tn.Addr.String(),\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t\tSubject: &n.Config.DeclRange,\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// preApplyHook calls the pre-Apply hook\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) preApplyHook(ctx EvalContext, change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tif change == nil {\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"preApplyHook for %s called with nil Change\", n.Addr))\n\t}\n\n\t// Only managed resources have user-visible apply actions.\n\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.ManagedResourceMode {\n\t\tpriorState := change.Before\n\t\tplannedNewState := change.After\n\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n\t\t\treturn h.PreApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), change.DeposedKey, change.Action, priorState, plannedNewState)\n\t\t}))\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// postApplyHook calls the post-Apply hook\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) postApplyHook(ctx EvalContext, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, err error) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\t// Only managed resources have user-visible apply actions.\n\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.ManagedResourceMode {\n\t\tvar newState cty.Value\n\t\tif state != nil {\n\t\t\tnewState = state.Value\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tnewState = cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType)\n\t\t}\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n\t\t\treturn h.PostApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, newState, err)\n\t\t}))\n\t}\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\ntype phaseState int\n\nconst (\n\tworkingState phaseState = iota\n\trefreshState\n\tprevRunState\n)\n\n//go:generate go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/stringer -type phaseState\n\n// writeResourceInstanceState saves the given object as the current object for\n// the selected resource instance.\n//\n// dependencies is a parameter, instead of those directly attacted to the\n// NodeResourceInstanceBase, because we don't write dependencies for\n// datasources.\n//\n// targetState determines which context state we're writing to during plan. The\n// default is the global working state.\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) writeResourceInstanceState(ctx EvalContext, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\n\treturn n.writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx, states.NotDeposed, obj, targetState)\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed(ctx EvalContext, deposedKey states.DeposedKey, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\n\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t// Bail out to avoid silently doing something other than what the\n\t\t// caller seems to have intended.\n\t\tpanic(\"trying to write current state object using writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed\")\n\t}\n\treturn n.writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx, deposedKey, obj, targetState)\n}\n\n// (this is the private common body of both writeResourceInstanceState and\n// writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed. Don't call it directly; instead, use\n// one of the two wrappers to be explicit about which of the instance's\n// objects you are intending to write.\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx EvalContext, deposedKey states.DeposedKey, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\n\tabsAddr := n.Addr\n\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tlogFuncName := \"NodeResourceInstanceBase.writeResourceInstanceState\"\n\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] %s to %s for %s\", logFuncName, targetState, absAddr)\n\t} else {\n\t\tlogFuncName = \"NodeResourceInstanceBase.writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed\"\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] %s to %s for %s (deposed key %s)\", logFuncName, targetState, absAddr, deposedKey)\n\t}\n\n\tvar state *states.SyncState\n\tswitch targetState {\n\tcase workingState:\n\t\tstate = ctx.State()\n\tcase refreshState:\n\t\tstate = ctx.RefreshState()\n\tcase prevRunState:\n\t\tstate = ctx.PrevRunState()\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"unsupported phaseState value %#v\", targetState))\n\t}\n\tif state == nil {\n\t\t// Should not happen, because we shouldn't ever try to write to\n\t\t// a state that isn't applicable to the current operation.\n\t\t// (We can also get in here for unit tests which are using\n\t\t// EvalContextMock but not populating PrevRunStateState with\n\t\t// a suitable state object.)\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"state of type %s is not applicable to the current operation; this is a bug in Terraform\", targetState)\n\t}\n\n\t// In spite of the name, this function also handles the non-deposed case\n\t// via the writeResourceInstanceState wrapper, by setting deposedKey to\n\t// the NotDeposed value (the zero value of DeposedKey).\n\tvar write func(src *states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc)\n\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\twrite = func(src *states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc) {\n\t\t\tstate.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(absAddr, src, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\twrite = func(src *states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc) {\n\t\t\tstate.SetResourceInstanceDeposed(absAddr, deposedKey, src, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif obj == nil {\n\t\t// No state at all yet, or we've been asked to remove the state.\n\t\twrite(nil)\n\t\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] %s: removing state object for %s\", logFuncName, absAddr)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] %s: removing state object for %s (deposed key %s)\", logFuncName, absAddr, deposedKey)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t_, providerSchema, err = getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tschema, currentVersion := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(absAddr.Resource.ContainingResource())\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// It shouldn't be possible to get this far in any real scenario\n\t\t// without a schema, but we might end up here in contrived tests that\n\t\t// fail to set up their world properly.\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no schema is attached to %s, so its state cannot be written\", n.Addr)\n\t}\n\n\tsrc, err := obj.Encode(schema.ImpliedType(), currentVersion)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode %s in state: %w\", n.Addr, err)\n\t}\n\n\twrite(src)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// planObjectDiff is a helper to compute a diff for a resource object.\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) planObjectDiff(ctx EvalContext, currentState *states.ResourceInstanceObject, plannedNewVal cty.Value, schema *configschema.Block, providerSchema providers.ProviderSchema) (cty.Value, *plans.ResourceInstanceChange, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\n\tvar priorVal cty.Value\n\tvar priorPrivate []byte\n\tvar priorPaths []cty.PathValueMarks\n\n\tif currentState != nil {\n\t\tpriorVal = currentState.Value\n\t\tpriorPrivate = currentState.Private\n\t\tpriorPaths = currentState.AttrSensitivePaths\n\t} else {\n\t\tpriorVal = cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType())\n\t}\n\n\tprovider, _, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, nil, diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\n\tunmarkedPriorVal, priorPaths2 := priorVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\n\tpriorPaths = append(priorPaths, priorPaths2...)\n\n\tunmarkedPlannedNewVal, plannedNewPaths := plannedNewVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\n\n\tresp := provider.PlanResourceChange(providers.PlanResourceChangeRequest{\n\t\tTypeName:         addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n\t\tConfig:           unmarkedPlannedNewVal,\n\t\tPriorState:       unmarkedPriorVal,\n\t\tProposedNewState: unmarkedPlannedNewVal,\n\t\tPriorPrivate:     priorPrivate,\n\t\tProviderMeta:     n.providerMeta(ctx),\n\t})\n\tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, addr.String()))\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tplannedNewVal = resp.PlannedState\n\tplannedPrivate := resp.PlannedPrivate\n\n\tif plannedNewVal == cty.NilVal {\n\t\t// Should never happen. Since real-world providers only implement the\n\t\t// temporary shimming protocol this is unlikely to occur, but we'll\n\t\t// check it anyway to give a diagnosable error rather than a panic.\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid plan\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned an invalid value for %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider, addr,\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t))\n\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tif errs := schema.InternalValidate(); len(errs) != 0 {\n\t\tfor _, err := range errs {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid plan\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q produced an invalid plan for %s, but we could not detect a reason for this from the provider's response; this is a bug in the provider.\\n\\n(%s)\",\n\t\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider, addr, tfdiags.FormatError(err),\n\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tplannedNewVal = plannedNewVal.MarkWithPaths(priorPaths)\n\tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(plannedNewVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\n\t\tplannedNewVal = marks.MarkPaths(plannedNewVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\n\t}\n\tplannedNewVal = plannedNewVal.MarkWithPaths(plannedNewPaths)\n\n\tforceReplace := n.forceReplace(ctx)\n\treqRep, reqRepDiags := getRequiredReplaces(unmarkedPriorVal, resp.PlannedState, resp.RequiresReplace, n.ResolvedProvider.Provider, addr)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(reqRepDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\taction, actionReason := getAction(addr, priorVal, plannedNewVal, n.CreateBeforeDestroy(), forceReplace, reqRep)\n\n\tchange := &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n\t\tAddr:         addr,\n\t\tPrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\n\t\tPrivate:      plannedPrivate,\n\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n\t\t\tAction: action,\n\t\t\tBefore: priorVal,\n\t\t\tAfter:  plannedNewVal,\n\n\t\t\t// BeforeValMarks and AfterValMarks are decided based on the\n\t\t\t// union of marks present on the original planned new value,\n\t\t\t// the prior value, and any additional marks discovered during\n\t\t\t// provider planning.\n\t\t\tBeforeValMarks: priorPaths,\n\t\t\tAfterValMarks:  plannedNewPaths,\n\t\t},\n\t\tActionReason:    actionReason,\n\t\tRequiredReplace: reqRep,\n\t}\n\n\treturn plannedNewVal, change, diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) forceReplace(ctx EvalContext) []addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// plan is the real implementation of both the planNewConfig and planUpdate\n// steps of a resource instance node's execution. It's the logic that actually\n// computes a plan.\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) plan(\n\tctx EvalContext,\n\tplannedChange *plans.ResourceInstanceChange,\n\tcurrentState *states.ResourceInstanceObject,\n\tcreateBeforeDestroy bool,\n\tforceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance,\n\tdeferred *deferring.Deferred,\n) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, *states.ResourceInstanceObject, instances.RepetitionData, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tvar keyData instances.RepetitionData\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\n\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, nil, keyData, diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\n\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(addr.Resource.Resource)\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"provider does not support resource type %q\", addr.Resource.Resource.Type))\n\t\treturn nil, nil, keyData, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// This is where we'll compute the planned new state for this instance.\n\t// We'll start by decoding the current state.\n\tvar priorState *states.ResourceInstanceObject\n\tvar priorVal cty.Value\n\tif currentState != nil {\n\t\tpriorState = currentState\n\t\tpriorVal = currentState.Value\n\t} else {\n\t\tpriorState = nil\n\t\tpriorVal = cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType())\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if there's already a change planned for this instance.\n\t// If so, we'll use the after value as the planned value for a refresh-only plan.\n\tif plannedChange != nil && plannedChange.Action == plans.NoOp {\n\t\tplannedChange = nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate the configuration value for this instance.\n\tvar configVal cty.Value\n\tvar configDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\tkeyData, configDiags = n.evaluateInstanceKeyData(ctx, addr)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn nil, priorState, keyData, diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tconfigVal, configDiags = n.evaluateResourceConfig(ctx, keyData)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn nil, priorState, keyData, diags\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tconfigVal = cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType)\n\t}\n\n\t// processIgnoreChanges handles the case where some attributes have been\n\t// excluded from future planned changes.\n\tvar ignoredChanges []cty.Path\n\tconfigVal, ignoredChanges, diags = processIgnoreChanges(priorVal, configVal, schema)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn nil, priorState, keyData, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Apply the proposed new state through the provider.\n\tpriorValForPlan, priorPathsForPlan := priorVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\n\tconfigValForPlan, configPathsForPlan := configVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\n\n\tproposedNewVal := objchange.ProposedNew(schema, priorValForPlan, configValForPlan)\n\n\t// The provider may need the PriorState and Config in the plan request\n\t// to produce a better plan.\n\tresp := provider.PlanResourceChange(providers.PlanResourceChangeRequest{\n\t\tTypeName:         addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n\t\tConfig:           configValForPlan,\n\t\tPriorState:       priorValForPlan,\n\t\tProposedNewState: proposedNewVal,\n\t\tPriorPrivate:     n.instancePrivate(priorState),\n\t\tProviderMeta:     n.providerMeta(ctx),\n\t})\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, addr.String()))\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn nil, priorState, keyData, diags\n\t}\n\n\tplannedNewVal := resp.PlannedState\n\tplannedPrivate := resp.PlannedPrivate\n\tif plannedNewVal == cty.NilVal {\n\t\t// Should never happen. Since real-world providers only implement the\n\t\t// temporary shimming protocol this is unlikely to occur, but we'll\n\t\t// check it anyway to give a diagnosable error rather than a panic.\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid plan\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned an invalid value for %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider, addr,\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t))\n\t\treturn nil, priorState, keyData, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Handling of required replaces\n\treqRep, reqRepDiags := getRequiredReplaces(priorValForPlan, plannedNewVal, resp.RequiresReplace, n.ResolvedProvider.Provider, addr)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(reqRepDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn nil, priorState, keyData, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Apply marks back to the planned values\n\tplannedNewVal = plannedNewVal.MarkWithPaths(priorPathsForPlan)\n\tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(plannedNewVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\n\t\tplannedNewVal = marks.MarkPaths(plannedNewVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\n\t}\n\tplannedNewVal = plannedNewVal.MarkWithPaths(configPathsForPlan)\n\n\tif errs := objchange.AssertPlanValid(schema, priorValForPlan, configValForPlan, plannedNewVal); len(errs) != 0 {\n\t\tif resp.LegacyTypeSystem {\n\t\t\t// The shimming of the old type system in the legacy SDK is not precise\n\t\t\t// enough to pass this consistency check, so we'll give it a pass here,\n\t\t\t// but we will generate a warning about it so that we are more likely\n\t\t\t// to notice in the logs if an inconsistency beyond the type system\n\t\t\t// leads to a downstream provider failure.\n\t\t\tvar buf strings.Builder\n\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \"[WARN] Provider %q produced an invalid plan for %s, but we are tolerating it because it is using the legacy plugin SDK.\\n    The following problems may be the cause of any confusing errors from downstream operations:\", n.ResolvedProvider.String(), addr)\n\t\t\tfor _, err := range errs {\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \"\\n      - %s\", tfdiags.FormatError(err))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tlog.Print(buf.String())\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tfor _, err := range errs {\n\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid plan\",\n\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned an invalid value for %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker. Please also refer to the documentation for the provider to check if there is guidance about this issue. Error: %s\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider, addr, tfdiags.FormatError(err),\n\t\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t\t))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil, priorState, keyData, diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// We need to combine the marks from the config and prior values because\n\t// the planned new value will reflect both.\n\tallPaths := append(priorPathsForPlan, configPathsForPlan...)\n\n\taction, actionReason := getAction(addr, priorVal, plannedNewVal, createBeforeDestroy, forceReplace, reqRep)\n\n\tchange := &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n\t\tAddr:         addr,\n\t\tPrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\n\t\tPrivate:      plannedPrivate,\n\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n\t\t\tAction:          action,\n\t\t\tBefore:          priorVal,\n\t\t\tAfter:           plannedNewVal,\n\t\t\tBeforeValMarks:  priorPathsForPlan,\n\t\t\tAfterValMarks:   allPaths,\n\t\t\tIgnoredChanges:  ignoredChanges,\n\t\t},\n\t\tActionReason:    actionReason,\n\t\tRequiredReplace: reqRep,\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if the provider is deferring this change\n\tif deferred != nil && resp.Deferred != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(deferred.DeferResourceInstanceChanges(addr, change, resp.Deferred.Reason))\n\t\treturn nil, priorState, keyData, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Also handle provider-initiated deferrals from the response\n\tif deferred != nil && deferred.DeferralAllowed() {\n\t\tif resp.Deferred != nil {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(deferred.DeferResourceInstanceChanges(addr, change, resp.Deferred.Reason))\n\t\t\treturn nil, priorState, keyData, diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn change, priorState, keyData, diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) instancePrivate(state *states.ResourceInstanceObject) []byte {\n\tif state == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn state.Private\n}\n\n// providerMeta returns the provider meta value for the provider used by this\n// resource, if there is one.\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) providerMeta(ctx EvalContext) cty.Value {\n\tif n.ProviderMetas != nil {\n\t\tif providerMeta, ok := n.ProviderMetas[n.ResolvedProvider.Provider]; ok {\n\t\t\tif n.Schema != nil {\n\t\t\t\tif metaVal, _, err := ctx.EvaluateExpr(providerMeta.Config, cty.DynamicPseudoType, nil); err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn metaVal\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn cty.NilVal\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) evaluateInstanceKeyData(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) (instances.RepetitionData, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\treturn instanceRepetitionData(ctx, addr)\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) evaluateResourceConfig(ctx EvalContext, keyData instances.RepetitionData) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tif n.Config == nil {\n\t\treturn cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType), diags\n\t}\n\n\tconfigVal, _, configDiags := ctx.EvaluateBlock(n.Config.Config, n.Schema, nil, keyData)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n\t}\n\treturn configVal, diags\n}\n\n// apply is the implementation of the apply operation for a resource instance.\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) apply(\n\tctx EvalContext,\n\tstate *states.ResourceInstanceObject,\n\tchange *plans.ResourceInstanceChange,\n\tconfig *configs.Resource,\n\tkeyData instances.RepetitionData,\n\tcreateBeforeDestroy bool,\n) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\n\tif state == nil {\n\t\tstate = &states.ResourceInstanceObject{}\n\t}\n\n\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(addr.Resource.Resource)\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"provider does not support resource type %q\", addr.Resource.Resource.Type))\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tunmarkedBefore, beforePaths := change.Before.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\n\tunmarkedAfter, afterPaths := change.After.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\n\n\t// If our prior value was tainted then we actually want to make the\n\t// temporary prior value be null, reflecting the fact that there is\n\t// effectively no prior value for a tainted resource.\n\tif state.Status == states.ObjectTainted {\n\t\tunmarkedBefore = cty.NullVal(unmarkedBefore.Type())\n\t}\n\n\tresp := provider.ApplyResourceChange(providers.ApplyResourceChangeRequest{\n\t\tTypeName:       addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n\t\tPriorState:     unmarkedBefore,\n\t\tConfig:         unmarkedAfter,\n\t\tPlannedState:   unmarkedAfter,\n\t\tPlannedPrivate: change.Private,\n\t\tProviderMeta:   n.providerMeta(ctx),\n\t})\n\tapplyDiags := resp.Diagnostics\n\tif config != nil {\n\t\tapplyDiags = applyDiags.InConfigBody(config.Config, addr.String())\n\t}\n\tdiags = diags.Append(applyDiags)\n\n\t// Even if there are errors in the response, we'll still try to extract\n\t// a new state from it.\n\tnewVal := resp.NewState\n\tif newVal == cty.NilVal {\n\t\t// If we get cty.NilVal here then it seems like the provider is not\n\t\t// behaving correctly in producing a result value from apply. We'll\n\t\t// treat this like a null result.\n\t\tnewVal = cty.NullVal(change.After.Type())\n\t\tif !diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Provider produced null object\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\"After the apply operation, the provider still returned a null object for %s. Only destroying should always produce a null value, so this is always a bug in the provider and should be reported in the provider's own repository.\",\n\t\t\t\t\taddr,\n\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// We need to check the output again after applying marks since the\n\t// marks are not transported through the provider protocol. If the\n\t// value after apply is known then we check to make sure the planned\n\t// values match.\n\tif newVal.IsWhollyKnown() {\n\t\tif errs := objchange.AssertObjectCompatible(schema, change.After, newVal); len(errs) > 0 {\n\t\t\tif resp.LegacyTypeSystem {\n\t\t\t\t// The shimming of the old type system in the legacy SDK is not precise\n\t\t\t\t// enough to pass this consistency check, so we'll give it a pass here,\n\t\t\t\t// but we will generate a warning about it so that we are more likely\n\t\t\t\t// to notice in the logs if an inconsistency beyond the type system\n\t\t\t\t// leads to a downstream provider failure.\n\t\t\t\tvar buf strings.Builder\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \"[WARN] Provider %q produced an unexpected new value for %s, but we are tolerating it because it is using the legacy plugin SDK.\\n    The following problems may be the cause of any confusing errors from downstream operations:\", n.ResolvedProvider.String(), n.Addr)\n\t\t\t\tfor _, err := range errs {\n\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \"\\n      - %s\", tfdiags.FormatError(err))\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tlog.Print(buf.String())\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tfor _, err := range errs {\n\t\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Provider produced inconsistent result after apply\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"When applying changes to %s, provider %q produced an unexpected new value: %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\taddr, n.ResolvedProvider.String(), tfdiags.FormatError(err),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t\t\t))\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If the value after apply still has unknown values, that's always a bug\n\t// in the provider.\n\tif !newVal.IsWhollyKnown() {\n\t\tnewVal.WalkWithTransform(func(path cty.Path, val cty.Value) (cty.Value, error) {\n\t\t\tif !val.IsKnown() {\n\t\t\t\tpathStr := tfdiags.FormatCtyPath(path)\n\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\t\"Provider returned invalid result object after apply\",\n\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"After the apply operation, the provider still indicated an unknown value for %s%s. All values must be known after apply, so this is always a bug in the provider and should be reported in the provider's own repository. Terraform will still save the other known object values in the state.\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn.Addr, pathStr,\n\t\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t\t))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn true, nil\n\t\t})\n\n\t\t// NOTE: This operation can potentially be lossy if there are multiple\n\t\t// elements in a set that differ only by unknown values: after\n\t\t// replacing with null these will be merged together into a single set\n\t\t// element. Since we can only get here in the presence of a provider\n\t\t// bug, we accept this because storing a result here is always a\n\t\t// best-effort sort of thing.\n\t\tnewVal = cty.UnknownAsNull(newVal)\n\t}\n\n\t// If we have paths to mark, mark those on this new value we need to\n\t// re-check the value against the schema, because nested computed values\n\t// won't be included in afterPaths, which are only what was read from the\n\t// After plan value.\n\tnewVal = newVal.MarkWithPaths(afterPaths)\n\tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(newVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\n\t\tnewVal = marks.MarkPaths(newVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\n\t}\n\n\tif change.Action != plans.Delete && !diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t// Only values that were marked as unknown in the planned value are allowed\n\t\t// to change during the apply operation. (We do this after the unknown-ness\n\t\t// check above so that we also catch anything that became unknown after\n\t\t// being known during plan.)\n\t\t//\n\t\t// If we are returning other errors anyway then we'll give this\n\t\t// a pass since the other errors are usually the explanation for\n\t\t// this one and so it's more helpful to let the user focus on the\n\t\t// root cause rather than distract with this extra problem.\n\t\tif errs := objchange.AssertObjectCompatible(schema, change.After, newVal); len(errs) > 0 {\n\t\t\tif resp.LegacyTypeSystem {\n\t\t\t\t// The shimming of the old type system in the legacy SDK is not precise\n\t\t\t\t// enough to pass this consistency check, so we'll give it a pass here,\n\t\t\t\t// but we will generate a warning about it so that we are more likely\n\t\t\t\t// to notice in the logs if an inconsistency beyond the type system\n\t\t\t\t// leads to a downstream provider failure.\n\t\t\t\tvar buf strings.Builder\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \"[WARN] Provider %q produced an unexpected new value for %s, but we are tolerating it because it is using the legacy plugin SDK.\\n    The following problems may be the cause of any confusing errors from downstream operations:\", n.ResolvedProvider.String(), n.Addr)\n\t\t\t\tfor _, err := range errs {\n\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \"\\n      - %s\", tfdiags.FormatError(err))\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tlog.Print(buf.String())\n\n\t\t\t\t// The sort of inconsistency we won't catch here is if a known value\n\t\t\t\t// in the plan is changed during apply. That can cause downstream\n\t\t\t\t// problems because a dependent resource would make its own plan based\n\t\t\t\t// on the planned value, and thus get a different result during the\n\t\t\t\t// apply phase. This will usually lead to a \"Provider produced invalid plan\"\n\t\t\t\t// error that incorrectly blames the downstream resource for the change.\n\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tfor _, err := range errs {\n\t\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Provider produced inconsistent result after apply\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"When applying changes to %s, provider %q produced an unexpected new value: %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tn.Addr, n.ResolvedProvider.String(), tfdiags.FormatError(err),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t\t\t))\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If a provider returns a null or non-null object at the wrong time then\n\t// we still want to save that but it often causes some confusing behaviors\n\t// where it seems like Terraform is failing to take any action at all,\n\t// so we'll generate some errors to draw attention to it.\n\tif !diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\tif change.Action == plans.Delete && !newVal.IsNull() {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Provider returned invalid result object after apply\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\"After applying a %s plan, the provider returned a non-null object for %s. Destroying should always produce a null value, so this is always a bug in the provider and should be reported in the provider's own repository. Terraform will still save this errant object in the state for debugging and recovery.\",\n\t\t\t\t\tchange.Action, n.Addr,\n\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif change.Action != plans.Delete && newVal.IsNull() {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Provider returned invalid result object after apply\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\"After applying a %s plan, the provider returned a null object for %s. Only destroying should always produce a null value, so this is always a bug in the provider and should be reported in the provider's own repository.\",\n\t\t\t\t\tchange.Action, n.Addr,\n\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tswitch {\n\tcase diags.HasErrors() && newVal.IsNull():\n\t\t// Sometimes providers return a null value when an operation fails for\n\t\t// some reason, but we'd rather keep the prior state so that the error\n\t\t// can be corrected on a subsequent run. We must only do this for null\n\t\t// new value though, or else we may discard partial updates the\n\t\t// provider was able to complete. Otherwise, we'll continue using the\n\t\t// prior state as the new value, making this effectively a no-op.  If\n\t\t// the item really _has_ been deleted then our next refresh will detect\n\t\t// that and fix it up.\n\t\treturn state.DeepCopy(), diags\n\n\tcase diags.HasErrors() && !newVal.IsNull():\n\t\t// if we have an error, make sure we restore the object status in the new state\n\t\tnewState := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\n\t\t\tStatus:              state.Status,\n\t\t\tValue:               newVal,\n\t\t\tPrivate:             resp.Private,\n\t\t\tCreateBeforeDestroy: createBeforeDestroy,\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// if the resource was being deleted, the dependencies are not going to\n\t\t// be recalculated and we need to restore those as well.\n\t\tif change.Action == plans.Delete {\n\t\t\tnewState.Dependencies = state.Dependencies\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn newState, diags\n\n\tcase !newVal.IsNull():\n\t\t// Non error case with a new state\n\t\tnewState := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\n\t\t\tStatus:              states.ObjectReady,\n\t\t\tValue:               newVal,\n\t\t\tPrivate:             resp.Private,\n\t\t\tCreateBeforeDestroy: createBeforeDestroy,\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn newState, diags\n\n\tdefault:\n\t\t// Non error case, were the object was deleted\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) prevRunAddr(ctx EvalContext) addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n\treturn resourceInstancePrevRunAddr(ctx, n.Addr)\n}\n\nfunc resourceInstancePrevRunAddr(ctx EvalContext, currentAddr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n\ttable := ctx.MoveResults()\n\treturn table.OldAddr(currentAddr)\n}\n\nfunc getAction(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, priorVal, plannedNewVal cty.Value, createBeforeDestroy bool, forceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance, reqRep cty.PathSet) (action plans.Action, actionReason plans.ResourceInstanceChangeActionReason) {\n\t// The user might also ask us to force replacing a particular resource\n\t// instance, regardless of whether the provider thinks it needs replacing.\n\t// For example, users typically do this if they learn a particular object\n\t// has become degraded in an immutable infrastructure scenario and so\n\t// replacing it with a new object is a viable repair path.\n\tmatchedForceReplace := false\n\tfor _, candidateAddr := range forceReplace {\n\t\tif candidateAddr.Equal(addr) {\n\t\t\tmatchedForceReplace = true\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// For \"force replace\" purposes we require an exact resource instance\n\t\t// address to match. If a user forgets to include the instance key\n\t\t// for a multi-instance resource then it won't match here, but we\n\t\t// have an earlier check in NodePlannableResource.Execute that should\n\t\t// prevent us from getting here in that case.\n\t}\n\n\t// Unmark for this test for value equality.\n\teqV := plannedNewVal.Equals(priorVal)\n\teq := eqV.IsKnown() && eqV.True()\n\n\tswitch {\n\tcase priorVal.IsNull():\n\t\taction = plans.Create\n\tcase eq && !matchedForceReplace:\n\t\taction = plans.NoOp\n\tcase matchedForceReplace || !reqRep.Empty():\n\t\t// If the user \"forced replace\" of this instance of if there are any\n\t\t// \"requires replace\" paths left _after our filtering above_ then this\n\t\t// is a replace action.\n\t\tif createBeforeDestroy {\n\t\t\taction = plans.CreateThenDelete\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\taction = plans.DeleteThenCreate\n\t\t}\n\t\tswitch {\n\t\tcase matchedForceReplace:\n\t\t\tactionReason = plans.ResourceInstanceReplaceByRequest\n\t\tcase !reqRep.Empty():\n\t\t\tactionReason = plans.ResourceInstanceReplaceBecauseCannotUpdate\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\taction = plans.Update\n\t\t// \"Delete\" is never chosen here, because deletion plans are always\n\t\t// created more directly elsewhere, such as in \"orphan\" handling.\n\t}\n\n\treturn\n}\n\n// getRequiredReplaces returns a list of paths to attributes whose changes mean\n// that we must replace rather than update an existing remote object.\n//\n// The provider produces a list of paths to attributes whose changes mean\n// that we must replace rather than update an existing remote object.\n// However, we only need to do that if the identified attributes _have_\n// actually changed -- particularly after we may have undone some of the\n// changes in processIgnoreChanges -- so now we'll filter that list to\n// include only where changes are detected.\n//\n// Both the priorVal and plannedNewVal should be unmarked before calling this\n// function. This function exposes nothing about the priorVal or plannedVal\n// except for the paths that require replacement which can be deduced from the\n// type with or without marks.\nfunc getRequiredReplaces(priorVal, plannedNewVal cty.Value, requiredReplaces []cty.Path, providerAddr tfaddr.Provider, addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) (cty.PathSet, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\treqRep := cty.NewPathSet()\n\tif len(requiredReplaces) > 0 {\n\t\tfor _, path := range requiredReplaces {\n\t\t\tif priorVal.IsNull() {\n\t\t\t\t// If prior is null then we don't expect any RequiresReplace at all,\n\t\t\t\t// because this is a Create action.\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tpriorChangedVal, priorPathDiags := hcl.ApplyPath(priorVal, path, nil)\n\t\t\tplannedChangedVal, plannedPathDiags := hcl.ApplyPath(plannedNewVal, path, nil)\n\t\t\tif plannedPathDiags.HasErrors() && priorPathDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\t// This means the path was invalid in both the prior and new\n\t\t\t\t// values, which is an error with the provider itself.\n\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid plan\",\n\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q has indicated \\\"requires replacement\\\" on %s for a non-existent attribute path %#v.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tproviderAddr, addr, path,\n\t\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t\t))\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Make sure we have valid Values for both values.\n\t\t\t// Note: if the opposing value was of the type\n\t\t\t// cty.DynamicPseudoType, the type assigned here may not exactly\n\t\t\t// match the schema. This is fine here, since we're only going to\n\t\t\t// check for equality, but if the NullVal is to be used, we need to\n\t\t\t// check the schema for th true type.\n\t\t\tswitch {\n\t\t\tcase priorChangedVal == cty.NilVal && plannedChangedVal == cty.NilVal:\n\t\t\t\t// this should never happen without ApplyPath errors above\n\t\t\t\tpanic(\"requires replace path returned 2 nil values\")\n\t\t\tcase priorChangedVal == cty.NilVal:\n\t\t\t\tpriorChangedVal = cty.NullVal(plannedChangedVal.Type())\n\t\t\tcase plannedChangedVal == cty.NilVal:\n\t\t\t\tplannedChangedVal = cty.NullVal(priorChangedVal.Type())\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\teqV := plannedChangedVal.Equals(priorChangedVal)\n\t\t\tif !eqV.IsKnown() || eqV.False() {\n\t\t\t\treqRep.Add(path)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn reqRep, diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn reqRep, diags\n}\n\n// Ensure unused imports don't cause compile errors\nvar _ = checks.StatusUnknown\nvar _ = mocking.PackageIsUsed\nvar _ = deferring.PackageIsUsed\nvar _ = provisioners.ConnTimeout\nvar _ = configschema.NestingList\nvar _ = objchange.AllBlockAttributesNull\n",

h.PostApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, newState, err)\\\\n243: \\\\t\\\\t}))\\\\n244: \\\\t}\\\\n245: \\\\n246: \\\\treturn diags\\\\n247: }\\\\n248: \\\\n249: type phaseState int\\\\n250: \\\\n251: const (\\\\n252: \\\\tworkingState phaseState = iota\\\\n253: \\\\trefreshState\\\\n254: \\\\tprevRunState\\\\n255: )\\\\n256: \\\\n257: //go:generate go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/stringer -type phaseState\\\\n258: \\\\n259: // writeResourceInstanceState saves the given object as the current object for\\\\n260: // the selected resource instance.\\\\n261: //\\\\n262: // dependencies is a parameter, instead of those directly attacted to the\\\\n263: // NodeAbstractResourceInstance, because we don't write dependencies for\\\\n264: // datasources.\\\\n265: //\\\\n266: // targetState determines which context state we're writing to during plan. The\\\\n267: // default is the global working state.\\\\n268: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) writeResourceInstanceState(ctx EvalContext, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\\\\n269: \\\\treturn n.writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx, states.NotDeposed, obj, targetState)\\\\n270: }\\\\n271: \\\\n272: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed(ctx EvalContext, deposedKey states.DeposedKey, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\\\\n273: \\\\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\\\\n274: \\\\t\\\\t// Bail out to avoid silently doing something other than what the\\\\n275: \\\\t\\\\t// caller seems to have intended.\\\\n276: \\\\t\\\\tpanic(\\\\\\\"trying to write current state object using writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed\\\\\\\")\\\\n277: \\\\t}\\\\n278: \\\\treturn n.writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx, deposedKey, obj, targetState)\\\\n279: }\\\\n280: \\\\n281: // (this is the private common body of both writeResourceInstanceState and\\\\n282: // writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed. Don't call it directly; instead, use\\\\n283: // one of the two wrappers to be explicit about which of the instance's\\\\n284: // objects you are intending to write.\\\\n285: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx EvalContext, deposedKey states.DeposedKey, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\\\\n286: \\\\tabsAddr := n.Addr\\\\n287: \\\\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\\\n288: \\\\tif err != nil {\\\\n289: \\\\t\\\\treturn err\\\\n290: \\\\t}\\\\n291: \\\\tlogFuncName := \\\\\\\"NodeAbstractResouceInstance.writeResourceInstanceState\\\\\\\"\\\\n292: \\\\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\\\\n293: \\\\t\\\\tlog.Printf(\\\\\\\"[TRACE] %s to %s for %s\\\\\\\", logFuncName, targetState, absAddr)\\\\n294: \\\\t} else {\\\\n295: \\\\t\\\\tlogFuncName = \\\\\\\"NodeAbstractResouceInstance.writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed\\\\\\\"\\\\n296: \\\\t\\\\tlog.Printf(\\\\\\\"[TRACE] %s to %s for %s (deposed key %s)\\\\\\\", logFuncName, targetState, absAddr, deposedKey)\\\\n297: \\\\t}\\\\n298: \\\\n299: \\\\tvar state *states.SyncState\\\\n300: \\\\tswitch targetState {\\\\n301: \\\\tcase workingState:\\\\n302: \\\\t\\\\tstate = ctx.State()\\\\n303: \\\\tcase refreshState:\\\\n304: \\\\t\\\\tstate = ctx.RefreshState()\\\\n305: \\\\tcase prevRunState:\\\\n306: \\\\t\\\\tstate = ctx.PrevRunState()\\\\n307: \\\\tdefault:\\\\n308: \\\\t\\\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\\\\\"unsupported phaseState value %#v\\\\\\\", targetState))\\\\n309: \\\\t}\\\\n310: \\\\tif state == nil {\\\\n311: \\\\t\\\\t// Should not happen, because we shouldn't ever try to write to\\\\n312: \\\\t\\\\t// a state that isn't applicable to the current operation.\\\\n313: \\\\t\\\\t// (We can also get in here for unit tests which are using\\\\n314: \\\\t\\\\t// EvalContextMock but not populating PrevRunStateState with\\\\n315: \\\\t\\\\t// a suitable state object.)\\\\n316: \\\\t\\\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"state of type %s is not applicable to the current operation; this is a bug in Terraform\\\\\\\", targetState)\\\\n317: \\\\t}\\\\n318: \\\\n319: \\\\t// In spite of the name, this function also handles the non-deposed case\\\\n320: \\\\t// via the writeResourceInstanceState wrapper, by setting deposedKey to\\\\n321: \\\\t// the NotDeposed value (the zero value of DeposedKey).\\\\n322: \\\\tvar write func(src *states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc)\\\\n323: \\\\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\\\\n324: \\\\t\\\\twrite = func(src *states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc) {\\\\n325: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tstate.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(absAddr, src, n.ResolvedProvider)\\\\n326: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n327: \\\\t} else {\\\\n328: \\\\t\\\\twrite = func(src *states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc) {\\\\n329: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tstate.SetResourceInstanceDeposed(absAddr, deposedKey, src, n.ResolvedProvider)\\\\n330: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n331: \\\\t}\\\\n332: \\\\n333: \\\\tif obj == nil || obj.Value.IsNull() {\\\\n334: \\\\t\\\\t// No need to encode anything: we'll just write it directly.\\\\n335: \\\\t\\\\twrite(nil)\\\\n336: \\\\t\\\\tlog.Printf(\\\\\\\"[TRACE] %s: removing state object for %s\\\\\\\", logFuncName, absAddr)\\\\n337: \\\\t\\\\treturn nil\\\\n338: \\\\t}\\\\n339: \\\\n340: \\\\tlog.Printf(\\\\\\\"[TRACE] %s: writing state object for %s\\\\\\\", logFuncName, absAddr)\\\\n341: \\\\n342: \\\\tschema, currentVersion := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(absAddr.ContainingResource().Resource)\\\\n343: \\\\tif schema == nil {\\\\n344: \\\\t\\\\t// It shouldn't be possible to get this far in any real scenario\\\\n345: \\\\t\\\\t// without a schema, but we might end up here in contrived tests that\\\\n346: \\\\t\\\\t// fail to set up their world properly.\\\\n347: \\\\t\\\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"failed to encode %s in state: no resource type schema available\\\\\\\", absAddr)\\\\n348: \\\\t}\\\\n349: \\\\n350: \\\\tsrc, err := obj.Encode(schema.ImpliedType(), currentVersion)\\\\n351: \\\\tif err != nil {\\\\n352: \\\\t\\\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"failed to encode %s in state: %s\\\\\\\", absAddr, err)\\\\n353: \\\\t}\\\\n354: \\\\n355: \\\\twrite(src)\\\\n356: \\\\treturn nil\\\\n357: }\\\\n358: \\\\n359: // planDestroy returns a plain destroy diff.\\\\n360: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) planDestroy(ctx EvalContext, currentState *states.ResourceInstanceObject, deposedKey states.DeposedKey) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, *providers.Deferred, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\\\n361: \\\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\\\n362: \\\\tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\\\\n363: \\\\tvar plan *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\\\\n364: \\\\n365: \\\\tabsAddr := n.Addr\\\\n366: \\\\tdeferralAllowed := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\\\\n367: \\\\n368: \\\\tif n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.Type == \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\" {\\\\n369: \\\\t\\\\tif deposedKey == \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\" {\\\\n370: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\\\\\"planDestroy for %s does not have ProviderAddr set\\\\\\\", absAddr))\\\\n371: \\\\t\\\\t} else {\\\\n372: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\\\\\"planDestroy for %s (deposed %s) does not have ProviderAddr set\\\\\\\", absAddr, deposedKey))\\\\n373: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n374: \\\\t}\\\\n375: \\\\n376: \\\\t// If there is no state or our attributes object is null then we're already\\\\n377: \\\\t// destroyed.\\\\n378: \\\\tif currentState == nil || currentState.Value.IsNull() {\\\\n379: \\\\t\\\\t// We still need to generate a NoOp change, because that allows\\\\n380: \\\\t\\\\t// outside consumers of the plan to distinguish between us affirming\\\\n381: \\\\t\\\\t// that we checked something and concluded no changes were needed\\\\n382: \\\\t\\\\t// vs. that something being entirely excluded e.g. due to -target.\\\\n383: \\\\t\\\\tnoop := \\\\u0026plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\\\\n384: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tAddr:        absAddr,\\\\n385: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tPrevRunAddr: n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\\\\n386: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tDeposedKey:  deposedKey,\\\\n387: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tChange: plans.Change{\\\\n388: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tAction: plans.NoOp,\\\\n389: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tBefore: cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType),\\\\n390: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tAfter:  cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType),\\\\n391: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t},\\\\n392: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\\\\n393: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n394: \\\\t\\\\treturn noop, deferred, nil\\\\n395: \\\\t}\\\\n396: \\\\n397: \\\\t// If we are in a context where we forget instead of destroying, we can\\\\n398: \\\\t// just return the forget change without consulting the provider.\\\\n399: \\\\tif ctx.Forget() {\\\\n400: \\\\t\\\\tforget, diags := n.planForget(ctx, currentState, deposedKey)\\\\n401: \\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go%3FL1-400\\\"}\", \"annotations\": null, \"meta\": null}], \"structuredContent\": {\"content\": \"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\\n2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\\n3: \\n4: package terraform\\n5: \\n6: import (\\n7: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n8: \\t\\\"log\\\"\\n9: \\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n10: \\n11: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2\\\"\\n12: \\ttfaddr \\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform-registry-address\\\"\\n13: \\t\\\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\\\"\\n14: \\n15: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\\\"\\n16: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/checks\\\"\\n17: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\\\"\\n18: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema\\\"\\n19: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\\\"\\n20: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang/marks\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/deferring\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/objchange\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\\\"\\n26: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/provisioners\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\\\"\\n28: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\\\"\\n29: )\\n30: \\n31: // NodeAbstractResourceInstance represents a resource instance with no\\n32: // associated operations. It embeds NodeAbstractResource but additionally\\n33: // contains an instance key, used to identify one of potentially many\\n34: // instances that were created from a resource in configuration, e.g. using\\n35: // the \\\"count\\\" or \\\"for_each\\\" arguments.\\n36: type NodeAbstractResourceInstance struct {\\n37: \\tNodeAbstractResource\\n38: \\tAddr addrs.AbsResourceInstance\\n39: \\n40: \\t// These are set via the AttachState method.\\n41: \\tinstanceState *states.ResourceInstance\\n42: \\n43: \\tDependencies []addrs.ConfigResource\\n44: \\n45: \\tpreDestroyRefresh bool\\n46: \\n47: \\t// During import we may generate configuration for a resource, which needs\\n48: \\t// to be stored in the final change.\\n49: \\tgeneratedConfigHCL string\\n50: \\n51: \\t// override is set by the graph itself, just before this node executes.\\n52: \\toverride *configs.Override\\n53: }\\n54: \\n55: // NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance creates an abstract resource instance graph\\n56: // node for the given absolute resource instance address.\\n57: func NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) *NodeAbstractResourceInstance {\\n58: \\t// Due to the fact that we embed NodeAbstractResource, the given address\\n59: \\t// actually ends up split between the resource address in the embedded\\n60: \\t// object and the InstanceKey field in our own struct. The\\n61: \\t// ResourceInstanceAddr method will stick these back together again on\\n62: \\t// request.\\n63: \\tr := NewNodeAbstractResource(addr.ContainingResource().Config())\\n64: \\treturn &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\\n65: \\t\\tNodeAbstractResource: *r,\\n66: \\t\\tAddr:                 addr,\\n67: \\t}\\n68: }\\n69: \\n70: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) Name() string {\\n71: \\treturn n.ResourceInstanceAddr().String()\\n72: }\\n73: \\n74: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) Path() addrs.ModuleInstance {\\n75: \\treturn n.Addr.Module\\n76: }\\n77: \\n78: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) HookResourceIdentity() HookResourceIdentity {\\n79: \\treturn HookResourceIdentity{\\n80: \\t\\tAddr:         n.Addr,\\n81: \\t\\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider.Provider,\\n82: \\t}\\n83: }\\n84: \\n85: // GraphNodeReferenceable\\n86: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) ReferenceableAddrs() []addrs.Referenceable {\\n87: \\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\\n88: \\treturn []addrs.Referenceable{\\n89: \\t\\taddr.Resource,\\n90: \\n91: \\t\\t// A resource instance can also be referenced by the address of its\\n92: \\t\\t// containing resource, so that e.g. a reference to aws_instance.foo\\n93: \\t\\t// would match both aws_instance.foo[0] and aws_instance.foo[1].\\n94: \\t\\taddr.ContainingResource().Resource,\\n95: \\t}\\n96: }\\n97: \\n98: // GraphNodeReferencer\\n99: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) References() []*addrs.Reference {\\n100: \\t// If we have a configuration attached then we'll delegate to our\\n101: \\t// embedded abstract resource, which knows how to extract dependencies\\n102: \\t// from configuration. If there is no config, then the dependencies will\\n103: \\t// be connected during destroy from those stored in the state.\\n104: \\tif n.Config != nil {\\n105: \\t\\tif n.Schema == nil {\\n106: \\t\\t\\t// We'll produce a log message about this out here so that\\n107: \\t\\t\\t// we can include the full instance address, since the equivalent\\n108: \\t\\t\\t// message in NodeAbstractResource.References cannot see it.\\n109: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[WARN] no schema is attached to %s, so config references cannot be detected\\\", n.Name())\\n110: \\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n111: \\t\\t}\\n112: \\t\\treturn n.NodeAbstractResource.References()\\n113: \\t}\\n114: \\n115: \\t// If we have neither config nor state then we have no references.\\n116: \\treturn nil\\n117: }\\n118: \\n119: // StateDependencies returns the dependencies which will be saved in the state\\n120: // for managed resources, or the most current dependencies for data resources.\\n121: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) StateDependencies() []addrs.ConfigResource {\\n122: \\t// Managed resources prefer the stored dependencies, to avoid possible\\n123: \\t// conflicts in ordering when refactoring configuration.\\n124: \\tif s := n.instanceState; s != nil {\\n125: \\t\\tif s.Current != nil {\\n126: \\t\\t\\treturn s.Current.Dependencies\\n127: \\t\\t}\\n128: \\t}\\n129: \\n130: \\t// If there are no stored dependencies, this is either a newly created\\n131: \\t// managed resource, or a data source, and we can use the most recently\\n132: \\t// calculated dependencies.\\n133: \\treturn n.Dependencies\\n134: }\\n135: \\n136: // GraphNodeResourceInstance\\n137: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) ResourceInstanceAddr() addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\\n138: \\treturn n.Addr\\n139: }\\n140: \\n141: // GraphNodeAttachResourceState\\n142: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) AttachResourceState(s *states.Resource) {\\n143: \\tif s == nil {\\n144: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[WARN] attaching nil state to %s\\\", n.Addr)\\n145: \\t\\treturn\\n146: \\t}\\n147: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] NodeAbstractResourceInstance.AttachResourceState for %s\\\", n.Addr)\\n148: \\tn.instanceState = s.Instance(n.Addr.Resource.Key)\\n149: \\tn.storedProviderConfig = s.ProviderConfig\\n150: }\\n151: \\n152: // GraphNodeOverridable\\n153: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) ConfigProvider() addrs.AbsProviderConfig {\\n154: \\treturn n.ResolvedProvider\\n155: }\\n156: \\n157: // GraphNodeOverridable\\n158: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) SetOverride(override *configs.Override) {\\n159: \\tn.override = override\\n160: }\\n161: \\n162: // readDiff returns the planned change for a particular resource instance\\n163: // object.\\n164: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) readDiff(ctx EvalContext, providerSchema providers.ProviderSchema) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, error) {\\n165: \\tchanges := ctx.Changes()\\n166: \\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\\n167: \\n168: \\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(addr.Resource.Resource)\\n169: \\tif schema == nil {\\n170: \\t\\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\\n171: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"provider does not support resource type %q\\\", addr.Resource.Resource.Type)\\n172: \\t}\\n173: \\n174: \\tchange := changes.GetResourceInstanceChange(addr, addrs.NotDeposed)\\n175: \\n176: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] readDiff: Read %s change from plan for %s\\\", change.Action, n.Addr)\\n177: \\n178: \\treturn change, nil\\n179: }\\n180: \\n181: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) checkPreventDestroy(change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\\n182: \\tif change == nil || n.Config == nil || n.Config.Managed == nil {\\n183: \\t\\treturn nil\\n184: \\t}\\n185: \\n186: \\tpreventDestroy := n.Config.Managed.PreventDestroy\\n187: \\n188: \\tif (change.Action == plans.Delete || change.Action.IsReplace()) && preventDestroy {\\n189: \\t\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n190: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n191: \\t\\t\\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\\n192: \\t\\t\\tSummary:  \\\"Instance cannot be destroyed\\\",\\n193: \\t\\t\\tDetail: fmt.Sprintf(\\n194: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Resource %s has lifecycle.prevent_destroy set, but the plan calls for this resource to be destroyed. To avoid this error and continue with the plan, either disable lifecycle.prevent_destroy or reduce the scope of the plan using the -target option.\\\",\\n195: \\t\\t\\t\\tn.Addr.String(),\\n196: \\t\\t\\t),\\n197: \\t\\t\\tSubject: &n.Config.DeclRange,\\n198: \\t\\t})\\n199: \\t\\treturn diags\\n200: \\t}\\n201: \\n202: \\treturn nil\\n203: }\\n204: \\n205: // preApplyHook calls the pre-Apply hook\\n206: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) preApplyHook(ctx EvalContext, change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\\n207: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n208: \\n209: \\tif change == nil {\\n210: \\t\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"preApplyHook for %s called with nil Change\\\", n.Addr))\\n211: \\t}\\n212: \\n213: \\t// Only managed resources have user-visible apply actions.\\n214: \\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.ManagedResourceMode {\\n215: \\t\\tpriorState := change.Before\\n216: \\t\\tplannedNewState := change.After\\n217: \\n218: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n219: \\t\\t\\treturn h.PreApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), change.DeposedKey, change.Action, priorState, plannedNewState)\\n220: \\t\\t}))\\n221: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n222: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n223: \\t\\t}\\n224: \\t}\\n225: \\n226: \\treturn nil\\n227: }\\n228: \\n229: // postApplyHook calls the post-Apply hook\\n230: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) postApplyHook(ctx EvalContext, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, err error) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\\n231: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n232: \\n233: \\t// Only managed resources have user-visible apply actions.\\n234: \\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.ManagedResourceMode {\\n235: \\t\\tvar newState cty.Value\\n236: \\t\\tif state != nil {\\n237: \\t\\t\\tnewState = state.Value\\n238: \\t\\t} else {\\n239: \\t\\t\\tnewState = cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType)\\n240: \\t\\t}\\n241: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n242: \\t\\t\\treturn h.PostApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, newState, err)\\n243: \\t\\t}))\\n244: \\t}\\n245: \\n246: \\treturn diags\\n247: }\\n248: \\n249: type phaseState int\\n250: \\n251: const (\\n252: \\tworkingState phaseState = iota\\n253: \\trefreshState\\n254: \\tprevRunState\\n255: )\\n256: \\n257: //go:generate go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/stringer -type phaseState\\n258: \\n259: // writeResourceInstanceState saves the given object as the current object for\\n260: // the selected resource instance.\\n261: //\\n262: // dependencies is a parameter, instead of those directly attacted to the\\n263: // NodeAbstractResourceInstance, because we don't write dependencies for\\n264: // datasources.\\n265: //\\n266: // targetState determines which context state we're writing to during plan. The\\n267: // default is the global working state.\\n268: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) writeResourceInstanceState(ctx EvalContext, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\\n269: \\treturn n.writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx, states.NotDeposed, obj, targetState)\\n270: }\\n271: \\n272: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed(ctx EvalContext, deposedKey states.DeposedKey, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\\n273: \\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\\n274: \\t\\t// Bail out to avoid silently doing something other than what the\\n275: \\t\\t// caller seems to have intended.\\n276: \\t\\tpanic(\\\"trying to write current state object using writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed\\\")\\n277: \\t}\\n278: \\treturn n.writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx, deposedKey, obj, targetState)\\n279: }\\n280: \\n281: // (this is the private common body of both writeResourceInstanceState and\\n282: // writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed. Don't call it directly; instead, use\\n283: // one of the two wrappers to be explicit about which of the instance's\\n284: // objects you are intending to write.\\n285: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx EvalContext, deposedKey states.DeposedKey, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\\n286: \\tabsAddr := n.Addr\\n287: \\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n288: \\tif err != nil {\\n289: \\t\\treturn err\\n290: \\t}\\n291: \\tlogFuncName := \\\"NodeAbstractResouceInstance.writeResourceInstanceState\\\"\\n292: \\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\\n293: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] %s to %s for %s\\\", logFuncName, targetState, absAddr)\\n294: \\t} else {\\n295: \\t\\tlogFuncName = \\\"NodeAbstractResouceInstance.writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed\\\"\\n296: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] %s to %s for %s (deposed key %s)\\\", logFuncName, targetState, absAddr, deposedKey)\\n297: \\t}\\n298: \\n299: \\tvar state *states.SyncState\\n300: \\tswitch targetState {\\n301: \\tcase workingState:\\n302: \\t\\tstate = ctx.State()\\n303: \\tcase refreshState:\\n304: \\t\\tstate = ctx.RefreshState()\\n305: \\tcase prevRunState:\\n306: \\t\\tstate = ctx.PrevRunState()\\n307: \\tdefault:\\n308: \\t\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"unsupported phaseState value %#v\\\", targetState))\\n309: \\t}\\n310: \\tif state == nil {\\n311: \\t\\t// Should not happen, because we shouldn't ever try to write to\\n312: \\t\\t// a state that isn't applicable to the current operation.\\n313: \\t\\t// (We can also get in here for unit tests which are using\\n314: \\t\\t// EvalContextMock but not populating PrevRunStateState with\\n315: \\t\\t// a suitable state object.)\\n316: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"state of type %s is not applicable to the current operation; this is a bug in Terraform\\\", targetState)\\n317: \\t}\\n318: \\n319: \\t// In spite of the name, this function also handles the non-deposed case\\n320: \\t// via the writeResourceInstanceState wrapper, by setting deposedKey to\\n321: \\t// the NotDeposed value (the zero value of DeposedKey).\\n322: \\tvar write func(src *states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc)\\n323: \\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\\n324: \\t\\twrite = func(src *states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc) {\\n325: \\t\\t\\tstate.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(absAddr, src, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n326: \\t\\t}\\n327: \\t} else {\\n328: \\t\\twrite = func(src *states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc) {\\n329: \\t\\t\\tstate.SetResourceInstanceDeposed(absAddr, deposedKey, src, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n330: \\t\\t}\\n331: \\t}\\n332: \\n333: \\tif obj == nil || obj.Value.IsNull() {\\n334: \\t\\t// No need to encode anything: we'll just write it directly.\\n335: \\t\\twrite(nil)\\n336: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] %s: removing state object for %s\\\", logFuncName, absAddr)\\n337: \\t\\treturn nil\\n338: \\t}\\n339: \\n340: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] %s: writing state object for %s\\\", logFuncName, absAddr)\\n341: \\n342: \\tschema, currentVersion := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(absAddr.ContainingResource().Resource)\\n343: \\tif schema == nil {\\n344: \\t\\t// It shouldn't be possible to get this far in any real scenario\\n345: \\t\\t// without a schema, but we might end up here in contrived tests that\\n346: \\t\\t// fail to set up their world properly.\\n347: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to encode %s in state: no resource type schema available\\\", absAddr)\\n348: \\t}\\n349: \\n350: \\tsrc, err := obj.Encode(schema.ImpliedType(), currentVersion)\\n351: \\tif err != nil {\\n352: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to encode %s in state: %s\\\", absAddr, err)\\n353: \\t}\\n354: \\n355: \\twrite(src)\\n356: \\treturn nil\\n357: }\\n358: \\n359: // planDestroy returns a plain destroy diff.\\n360: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) planDestroy(ctx EvalContext, currentState *states.ResourceInstanceObject, deposedKey states.DeposedKey) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, *providers.Deferred, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n361: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n362: \\tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\\n363: \\tvar plan *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\\n364: \\n365: \\tabsAddr := n.Addr\\n366: \\tdeferralAllowed := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\\n367: \\n368: \\tif n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.Type == \\\"\\\" {\\n369: \\t\\tif deposedKey == \\\"\\\" {\\n370: \\t\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"planDestroy for %s does not have ProviderAddr set\\\", absAddr))\\n371: \\t\\t} else {\\n372: \\t\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"planDestroy for %s (deposed %s) does not \\tif currentState == nil || currentState.Value.IsNull() {\\n379: \\t\\t// We still need to generate a NoOp change, because that allows\\n380: \\t\\t// outside consumers of the plan to distinguish between us affirming\\n381: \\t\\t// that we checked something and concluded no changes were needed\\n382: \\t\\t// vs. that something being entirely excluded e.g. due to -target.\\n383: \\t\\tnoop := \\t\\t\\t},\\n392: \\t\\t\\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\\n393: \\t\\t}\\n394: \\t\\treturn noop, deferred, nil\\n395: \\t}\\n396: \\n397: \\t// If we are in a context where we forget instead of destroying, we can\\n398: \\t// just return the forget change without consulting the provider.\\n399: \\tif ctx.Forget() {\\n400: \\t\\tforget, diags := n.planForget(ctx, currentState, deposedKey)\\n401: \", \"url\": cty.NilVal\\\\\\\")\\\\n669: \\\\t}\\\\n670: \\\\n671: \\\\t// If we have deferred the refresh, we expect the new state not to be wholly known\\\\n672: \\\\t// and callers should be prepared to handle
