value.\\\\n1390: \\\\t\\\\tvar configMap map[string]cty.Value\\\\n1391: \\\\t\\\\tswitch {\\\\n1392: \\\\t\\\\tcase v.IsNull() || v.LengthInt() == 0:\\\\n1393: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tconfigMap = map[string]cty.Value{}\\\\n1394: \\\\t\\\\tdefault:\\\\n1395: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tconfigMap = v.AsValueMap()\\\\n1396: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1397: \\\\n1398: \\\\t\\\\tfor _, ignored := range ignoredValues {\\\\n1399: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif !path.Equals(ignored.path) {\\\\n1400: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tcontinue\\\\n1401: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1402: \\\\n1403: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif ignored.key.IsNull() {\\\\n1404: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// The map address is confirmed to match at this point,\\\\n1405: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// so if there is no key, we want the entire map and can\\\\n1406: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// stop accumulating values.\\\\n1407: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn ignored.value, nil\\\\n1408: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1409: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// Now we know we are ignoring a specific index of this map, so get\\\\n1410: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// the config map and modify, add, or remove the desired key.\\\\n1411: \\\\n1412: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// We also need to create a prior map, so we can check for\\\\n1413: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// existence while getting the value, because Value.Index will\\\\n1414: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// return null for a key with a null value and for a non-existent\\\\n1415: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// key.\\\\n1416: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tvar priorMap map[string]cty.Value\\\\n1417: \\\\n1418: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// We need to drop the marks from the ignored map for handling. We\\\\n1419: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// don't need to store these, as we now know the ignored value is\\\\n1420: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// only within the map, not the map itself.\\\\n1421: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tignoredVal, _ := ignored.value.Unmark()\\\\n1422: \\\\n1423: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tswitch {\\\\n1424: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tcase ignored.value.IsNull() || ignoredVal.LengthInt() == 0:\\\\n1425: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tpriorMap = map[string]cty.Value{}\\\\n1426: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdefault:\\\\n1427: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tpriorMap = ignoredVal.AsValueMap()\\\\n1428: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1429: \\\\n1430: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tkey := ignored.key.AsString()\\\\n1431: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tpriorElem, keep := priorMap[key]\\\\n1432: \\\\n1433: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tswitch {\\\\n1434: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tcase !keep:\\\\n1435: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// this didn't exist in the old map value, so we're keeping the\\\\n1436: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// \\\\\\\"absence\\\\\\\" of the key by removing it from the config\\\\n1437: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdelete(configMap, key)\\\\n1438: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdefault:\\\\n1439: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tconfigMap[key] = priorElem\\\\n1440: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1441: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1442: \\\\n1443: \\\\t\\\\tvar newVal cty.Value\\\\n1444: \\\\t\\\\tswitch {\\\\n1445: \\\\t\\\\tcase len(configMap) \\\\u003e 0:\\\\n1446: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tnewVal = cty.MapVal(configMap)\\\\n1447: \\\\t\\\\tcase v.IsNull():\\\\n1448: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// if the config value was null, and no values remain in the map,\\\\n1449: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// reset the value to null.\\\\n1450: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tnewVal = v\\\\n1451: \\\\t\\\\tdefault:\\\\n1452: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tnewVal = cty.MapValEmpty(v.Type().ElementType())\\\\n1453: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1454: \\\\n1455: \\\\t\\\\tif len(vMarks) \\\\u003e 0 {\\\\n1456: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tnewVal = newVal.WithMarks(vMarks)\\\\n1457: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1458: \\\\n1459: \\\\t\\\\treturn newVal, nil\\\\n1460: \\\\t})\\\\n1461: \\\\treturn ret, nil\\\\n1462: }\\\\n1463: \\\\n1464: // readDataSource handles everything needed to call ReadDataSource on the provider.\\\\n1465: // A previously evaluated configVal can be passed in, or a new one is generated\\\\n1466: // from the resource configuration.\\\\n1467: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) readDataSource(ctx EvalContext, configVal cty.Value) (cty.Value, *providers.Deferred, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\\\n1468: \\\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\\\n1469: \\\\tvar newVal cty.Value\\\\n1470: \\\\tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\\\\n1471: \\\\n1472: \\\\tconfig := *n.Config\\\\n1473: \\\\tdeferralAllowed := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\\\\n1474: \\\\n1475: \\\\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\\\n1476: \\\\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\\\n1477: \\\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\\\n1478: \\\\t\\\\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\\\\n1479: \\\\t}\\\\n1480: \\\\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource)\\\\n1481: \\\\tif schema == nil {\\\\n1482: \\\\t\\\\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\\\\n1483: \\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"provider %q does not support data source %q\\\\\\\", n.ResolvedProvider, n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource.Type))\\\\n1484: \\\\t\\\\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\\\\n1485: \\\\t}\\\\n1486: \\\\n1487: \\\\tmetaConfigVal, metaDiags := n.providerMetas(ctx)\\\\n1488: \\\\tdiags = diags.Append(metaDiags)\\\\n1489: \\\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\\\n1490: \\\\t\\\\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\\\\n1491: \\\\t}\\\\n1492: \\\\n1493: \\\\t// Unmark before sending to provider, will re-mark before returning\\\\n1494: \\\\tvar pvm []cty.PathValueMarks\\\\n1495: \\\\tconfigVal, pvm = configVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\\\\n1496: \\\\n1497: \\\\tlog.Printf(\\\\\\\"[TRACE] readDataSource: Re-validating config for %s\\\\\\\", n.Addr)\\\\n1498: \\\\tvalidateResp := provider.ValidateDataResourceConfig(\\\\n1499: \\\\t\\\\tproviders.ValidateDataResourceConfigRequest{\\\\n1500: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tTypeName: n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource.Type,\\\\n1501: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tConfig:   configVal,\\\\n1502: \\\\t\\\\t},\\\\n1503: \\\\t)\\\\n1504: \\\\tdiags = diags.Append(validateResp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(config.Config, n.Addr.String()))\\\\n1505: \\\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\\\n1506: \\\\t\\\\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\\\\n1507: \\\\t}\\\\n1508: \\\\n1509: \\\\t// If we get down here then our configuration is complete and we're read\\\\n1510: \\\\t// to actually call the provider to read the data.\\\\n1511: \\\\tlog.Printf(\\\\\\\"[TRACE] readDataSource: %s configuration is complete, so reading from provider\\\\\\\", n.Addr)\\\\n1512: \\\\n1513: \\\\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\\\n1514: \\\\t\\\\treturn h.PreApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, plans.Read, cty.NullVal(configVal.Type()), configVal)\\\\n1515: \\\\t}))\\\\n1516: \\\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\\\n1517: \\\\t\\\\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\\\\n1518: \\\\t}\\\\n1519: \\\\n1520: \\\\tvar resp providers.ReadDataSourceResponse\\\\n1521: \\\\tif n.override != nil {\\\\n1522: \\\\t\\\\toverride, overrideDiags := mocking.ComputedValuesForDataSource(configVal, mocking.MockedData{\\\\n1523: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tValue: n.override.Values,\\\\n1524: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tRange: n.override.ValuesRange,\\\\n1525: \\\\t\\\\t}, schema)\\\\n1526: \\\\t\\\\tresp = providers.ReadDataSourceResponse{\\\\n1527: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tState:       override,\\\\n1528: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tDiagnostics: overrideDiags,\\\\n1529: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1530: \\\\t} else {\\\\n1531: \\\\t\\\\tresp = provider.ReadDataSource(providers.ReadDataSourceRequest{\\\\n1532: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tTypeName:     n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource.Type,\\\\n1533: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tConfig:       configVal,\\\\n1534: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tProviderMeta: metaConfigVal,\\\\n1535: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\\\\n1536: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tDeferralAllowed: deferralAllowed,\\\\n1537: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t},\\\\n1538: \\\\t\\\\t})\\\\n1539: \\\\n1540: \\\\t\\\\t// If we don't support deferrals, but the provider reports a deferral and does not\\\\n1541: \\\\t\\\\t// emit any error level diagnostics, we should emit an error.\\\\n1542: \\\\t\\\\tif resp.Deferred != nil \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 !deferralAllowed \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 !resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {\\\\n1543: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(deferring.UnexpectedProviderDeferralDiagnostic(n.Addr))\\\\n1544: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1545: \\\\n1546: \\\\t\\\\tif resp.Deferred != nil {\\\\n1547: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdeferred = resp.Deferred\\\\n1548: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1549: \\\\t}\\\\n1550: \\\\tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(config.Config, n.Addr.String()))\\\\n1551: \\\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\\\n1552: \\\\t\\\\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\\\\n1553: \\\\t}\\\\n1554: \\\\tnewVal = resp.State\\\\n1555: \\\\tif newVal == cty.NilVal {\\\\n1556: \\\\t\\\\t// This can happen with incompletely-configured mocks. We'll allow it\\\\n1557: \\\\t\\\\t// and treat it as an alias for a properly-typed null value.\\\\n1558: \\\\t\\\\tnewVal = cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType())\\\\n1559: \\\\t}\\\\n1560: \\\\n1561: \\\\t// We don't want to run the checks if the data source read is deferred\\\\n1562: \\\\tif deferred == nil {\\\\n1563: \\\\t\\\\tfor _, err := range newVal.Type().TestConformance(schema.ImpliedType()) {\\\\n1564: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\\\n1565: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\ttfdiags.Error,\\\\n1566: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"Provider produced invalid object\\\\\\\",\\\\n1567: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\\n1568: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"Provider %q produced an invalid value for %s.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\\\\\",\\\\n1569: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tn.ResolvedProvider, tfdiags.FormatErrorPrefixed(err, n.Addr.String()),\\\\n1570: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t),\\\\n1571: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t))\\\\n1572: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1573: \\\\t\\\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\\\n1574: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\\\\n1575: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1576: \\\\n1577: \\\\t\\\\tif newVal.IsNull() {\\\\n1578: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\\\n1579: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\ttfdiags.Error,\\\\n1580: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"Provider produced null object\\\\\\\",\\\\n1581: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\\n1582: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"Provider %q produced a null value for %s.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\\\\\",\\\\n1583: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tn.ResolvedProvider, n.Addr,\\\\n1584: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t),\\\\n1585: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t))\\\\n1586: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1587: \\\\n1588: \\\\t\\\\tif !newVal.IsNull() \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 !newVal.IsWhollyKnown() {\\\\n1589: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\\\n1590: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\ttfdiags.Error,\\\\n1591: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"Provider produced invalid object\\\\\\\",\\\\n1592: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\\n1593: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"Provider %q produced a value for %s that is not wholly known.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\\\\\",\\\\n1594: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tn.ResolvedProvider, n.Addr,\\\\n1595: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t),\\\\n1596: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t))\\\\n1597: \\\\n1598: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// We'll still save the object, but we need to eliminate any unknown\\\\n1599: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// values first because we can't serialize them in the state file.\\\\n1600: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// Note that this may cause set elements to be coalesced if they\\\\n1601: \\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go%3FL1200-1600\\\"}\", \"annotations\": null, \"meta\": null}], \"structuredContent\": {\"content\": \"1200: \\t\\t\\t// Pass the marked planned value through in our change\\n1201: \\t\\t\\t// to propogate through evaluation.\\n1202: \\t\\t\\t// Marks will be removed when encoding.\\n1203: \\t\\t\\tAfter:           plannedNewVal,\\n1204: \\t\\t\\tGeneratedConfig: n.generatedConfigHCL,\\n1205: \\t\\t},\\n1206: \\t\\tActionReason:    actionReason,\\n1207: \\t\\tRequiredReplace: reqRep,\\n1208: \\t}\\n1209: \\n1210: \\t// Update our return state\\n1211: \\tstate := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\n1212: \\t\\t// We use the special \\\"planned\\\" status here to note that this\\n1213: \\t\\t// object's value is not yet complete. Objects with this status\\n1214: \\t\\t// cannot be used during expression evaluation, so the caller\\n1215: \\t\\t// must _also_ record the returned change in the active plan,\\n1216: \\t\\t// which the expression evaluator will use in preference to this\\n1217: \\t\\t// incomplete value recorded in the state.\\n1218: \\t\\tStatus:  states.ObjectPlanned,\\n1219: \\t\\tValue:   plannedNewVal,\\n1220: \\t\\tPrivate: plannedPrivate,\\n1221: \\t}\\n1222: \\n1223: \\treturn plan, state, deferred, keyData, diags\\n1224: }\\n1225: \\n1226: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) processIgnoreChanges(prior, config cty.Value, schema *configschema.Block) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n1227: \\t// ignore_changes only applies when an object already exists, since we\\n1228: \\t// can't ignore changes to a thing we've not created yet.\\n1229: \\tif prior.IsNull() {\\n1230: \\t\\treturn config, nil\\n1231: \\t}\\n1232: \\n1233: \\tignoreChanges := traversalsToPaths(n.Config.Managed.IgnoreChanges)\\n1234: \\tignoreAll := n.Config.Managed.IgnoreAllChanges\\n1235: \\n1236: \\tif len(ignoreChanges) == 0 && !ignoreAll {\\n1237: \\t\\treturn config, nil\\n1238: \\t}\\n1239: \\n1240: \\tif ignoreAll {\\n1241: \\t\\t// Legacy providers need up to clean up their invalid plans and ensure\\n1242: \\t\\t// no changes are passed though, but that also means making an invalid\\n1243: \\t\\t// config with computed values. In that case we just don't supply a\\n1244: \\t\\t// schema and return the prior val directly.\\n1245: \\t\\tif schema == nil {\\n1246: \\t\\t\\treturn prior, nil\\n1247: \\t\\t}\\n1248: \\n1249: \\t\\t// If we are trying to ignore all attribute changes, we must filter\\n1250: \\t\\t// computed attributes out from the prior state to avoid sending them\\n1251: \\t\\t// to the provider as if they were included in the configuration.\\n1252: \\t\\tret, _ := cty.Transform(prior, func(path cty.Path, v cty.Value) (cty.Value, error) {\\n1253: \\t\\t\\tattr := schema.AttributeByPath(path)\\n1254: \\t\\t\\tif attr != nil && attr.Computed && !attr.Optional {\\n1255: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn cty.NullVal(v.Type()), nil\\n1256: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1257: \\n1258: \\t\\t\\treturn v, nil\\n1259: \\t\\t})\\n1260: \\n1261: \\t\\treturn ret, nil\\n1262: \\t}\\n1263: \\n1264: \\tif prior.IsNull() || config.IsNull() {\\n1265: \\t\\t// Ignore changes doesn't apply when we're creating for the first time.\\n1266: \\t\\t// Proposed should never be null here, but if it is then we'll just let it be.\\n1267: \\t\\treturn config, nil\\n1268: \\t}\\n1269: \\n1270: \\tret, diags := processIgnoreChangesIndividual(prior, config, ignoreChanges)\\n1271: \\n1272: \\treturn ret, diags\\n1273: }\\n1274: \\n1275: // Convert the hcl.Traversal values we get form the configuration to the\\n1276: // cty.Path values we need to operate on the cty.Values\\n1277: func traversalsToPaths(traversals []hcl.Traversal) []cty.Path {\\n1278: \\tpaths := make([]cty.Path, len(traversals))\\n1279: \\tfor i, traversal := range traversals {\\n1280: \\t\\tpath := traversalToPath(traversal)\\n1281: \\t\\tpaths[i] = path\\n1282: \\t}\\n1283: \\treturn paths\\n1284: }\\n1285: \\n1286: func traversalToPath(traversal hcl.Traversal) cty.Path {\\n1287: \\tpath := make(cty.Path, len(traversal))\\n1288: \\tfor si, step := range traversal {\\n1289: \\t\\tswitch ts := step.(type) {\\n1290: \\t\\tcase hcl.TraverseRoot:\\n1291: \\t\\t\\tpath[si] = cty.GetAttrStep{\\n1292: \\t\\t\\t\\tName: ts.Name,\\n1293: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1294: \\t\\tcase hcl.TraverseAttr:\\n1295: \\t\\t\\tpath[si] = cty.GetAttrStep{\\n1296: \\t\\t\\t\\tName: ts.Name,\\n1297: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1298: \\t\\tcase hcl.TraverseIndex:\\n1299: \\t\\t\\tpath[si] = cty.IndexStep{\\n1300: \\t\\t\\t\\tKey: ts.Key,\\n1301: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1302: \\t\\tdefault:\\n1303: \\t\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"unsupported traversal step %#v\\\", step))\\n1304: \\t\\t}\\n1305: \\t}\\n1306: \\treturn path\\n1307: }\\n1308: \\n1309: func processIgnoreChangesIndividual(prior, config cty.Value, ignoreChangesPath []cty.Path) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n1310: \\ttype ignoreChange struct {\\n1311: \\t\\t// Path is the full path, minus any trailing map index\\n1312: \\t\\tpath cty.Path\\n1313: \\t\\t// Value is the value we are to retain at the above path. If there is a\\n1314: \\t\\t// key value, this must be a map and the desired value will be at the\\n1315: \\t\\t// key index.\\n1316: \\t\\tvalue cty.Value\\n1317: \\t\\t// Key is the index key if the ignored path ends in a map index.\\n1318: \\t\\tkey cty.Value\\n1319: \\t}\\n1320: \\tvar ignoredValues []ignoreChange\\n1321: \\n1322: \\t// Find the actual changes first and store them in the ignoreChange struct.\\n1323: \\t// If the change was to a map value, and the key doesn't exist in the\\n1324: \\t// config, it would never be visited in the transform walk.\\n1325: \\tfor _, icPath := range ignoreChangesPath {\\n1326: \\t\\tkey := cty.NullVal(cty.String)\\n1327: \\t\\t// check for a map index, since maps are the only structure where we\\n1328: \\t\\t// could have invalid path steps.\\n1329: \\t\\tlast, ok := icPath[len(icPath)-1].(cty.IndexStep)\\n1330: \\t\\tif ok {\\n1331: \\t\\t\\tif last.Key.Type() == cty.String {\\n1332: \\t\\t\\t\\ticPath = icPath[:len(icPath)-1]\\n1333: \\t\\t\\t\\tkey = last.Key\\n1334: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1335: \\t\\t}\\n1336: \\n1337: \\t\\t// The structure should have been validated already, and we already\\n1338: \\t\\t// trimmed the trailing map index. Any other intermediate index error\\n1339: \\t\\t// means we wouldn't be able to apply the value below, so no need to\\n1340: \\t\\t// record this.\\n1341: \\t\\tp, err := icPath.Apply(prior)\\n1342: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n1343: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n1344: \\t\\t}\\n1345: \\t\\tc, err := icPath.Apply(config)\\n1346: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n1347: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n1348: \\t\\t}\\n1349: \\n1350: \\t\\t// If this is a map, it is checking the entire map value for equality\\n1351: \\t\\t// rather than the individual key. This means that the change is stored\\n1352: \\t\\t// here even if our ignored key doesn't change. That is OK since it\\n1353: \\t\\t// won't cause any changes in the transformation, but allows us to skip\\n1354: \\t\\t// breaking up the maps and checking for key existence here too.\\n1355: \\t\\tif !p.RawEquals(c) {\\n1356: \\t\\t\\t// there a change to ignore at this path, store the prior value\\n1357: \\t\\t\\tignoredValues = append(ignoredValues, ignoreChange{icPath, p, key})\\n1358: \\t\\t}\\n1359: \\t}\\n1360: \\n1361: \\tif len(ignoredValues) == 0 {\\n1362: \\t\\treturn config, nil\\n1363: \\t}\\n1364: \\n1365: \\tret, _ := cty.Transform(config, func(path cty.Path, v cty.Value) (cty.Value, error) {\\n1366: \\t\\t// Easy path for when we are only matching the entire value. The only\\n1367: \\t\\t// values we break up for inspection are maps.\\n1368: \\t\\tif !v.Type().IsMapType() {\\n1369: \\t\\t\\tfor _, ignored := range ignoredValues {\\n1370: \\t\\t\\t\\tif path.Equals(ignored.path) {\\n1371: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn ignored.value, nil\\n1372: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1373: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1374: \\t\\t\\treturn v, nil\\n1375: \\t\\t}\\n1376: \\t\\t// We now know this must be a map, so we need to accumulate the values\\n1377: \\t\\t// key-by-key.\\n1378: \\n1379: \\t\\tif !v.IsNull() && !v.IsKnown() {\\n1380: \\t\\t\\t// since v is not known, we cannot ignore individual keys\\n1381: \\t\\t\\treturn v, nil\\n1382: \\t\\t}\\n1383: \\n1384: \\t\\t// The map values will remain as cty values, so we only need to store\\n1385: \\t\\t// the marks from the outer map itself\\n1386: \\t\\tv, vMarks := v.Unmark()\\n1387: \\n1388: \\t\\t// The configMap is the current configuration value, which we will\\n1389: \\t\\t// mutate based on the ignored paths and the prior map value.\\n1390: \\t\\tvar configMap map[string]cty.Value\\n1391: \\t\\tswitch {\\n1392: \\t\\tcase v.IsNull() || v.LengthInt() == 0:\\n1393: \\t\\t\\tconfigMap = map[string]cty.Value{}\\n1394: \\t\\tdefault:\\n1395: \\t\\t\\tconfigMap = v.AsValueMap()\\n1396: \\t\\t}\\n1397: \\n1398: \\t\\tfor _, ignored := range ignoredValues {\\n1399: \\t\\t\\tif !path.Equals(ignored.path) {\\n1400: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n1401: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1402: \\n1403: \\t\\t\\tif ignored.key.IsNull() {\\n1404: \\t\\t\\t\\t// The map address is confirmed to match at this point,\\n1405: \\t\\t\\t\\t// so if there is no key, we want the entire map and can\\n1406: \\t\\t\\t\\t// stop accumulating values.\\n1407: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn ignored.value, nil\\n1408: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1409: \\t\\t\\t// Now we know we are ignoring a specific index of this map, so get\\n1410: \\t\\t\\t// the config map and modify, add, or remove the desired key.\\n1411: \\n1412: \\t\\t\\t// We also need to create a prior map, so we can check for\\n1413: \\t\\t\\t// existence while getting the value, because Value.Index will\\n1414: \\t\\t\\t// return null for a key with a null value and for a non-existent\\n1415: \\t\\t\\t// key.\\n1416: \\t\\t\\tvar priorMap map[string]cty.Value\\n1417: \\n1418: \\t\\t\\t// We need to drop the marks from the ignored map for handling. We\\n1419: \\t\\t\\t// don't need to store these, as we now know the ignored value is\\n1420: \\t\\t\\t// only within the map, not the map itself.\\n1421: \\t\\t\\tignoredVal, _ := ignored.value.Unmark()\\n1422: \\n1423: \\t\\t\\tswitch {\\n1424: \\t\\t\\tcase ignored.value.IsNull() || ignoredVal.LengthInt() == 0:\\n1425: \\t\\t\\t\\tpriorMap = map[string]cty.Value{}\\n1426: \\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n1427: \\t\\t\\t\\tpriorMap = ignoredVal.AsValueMap()\\n1428: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1429: \\n1430: \\t\\t\\tkey := ignored.key.AsString()\\n1431: \\t\\t\\tpriorElem, keep := priorMap[key]\\n1432: \\n1433: \\t\\t\\tswitch {\\n1434: \\t\\t\\tcase !keep:\\n1435: \\t\\t\\t\\t// this didn't exist in the old map value, so we're keeping the\\n1436: \\t\\t\\t\\t// \\\"absence\\\" newVal, nil\\n1460: \\t})\\n1461: \\treturn ret, nil\\n1462: }\\n1463: \\n1464: // readDataSource handles everything needed to call ReadDataSource on the provider.\\n1465: // A previously evaluated configVal can be passed in, or a new one is generated\\n1466: // from the resource configuration.\\n1467: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) readDataSource(ctx EvalContext, configVal cty.Value) (cty.Value, *providers.Deferred, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n1468: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n1469: \\tvar newVal cty.Value\\n1470: \\tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\\n1471: \\n1472: \\tconfig := *n.Config\\n1473: \\tdeferralAllowed := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\\n1474: \\n1475: \\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n1476: \\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n1477: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n1478: \\t\\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\\n1479: \\t}\\n1480: \\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource)\\n1481: \\tif schema == nil {\\n1482: \\t\\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\\n1483: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"provider %q does not support data source %q\\\", n.ResolvedProvider, n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource.Type))\\n1484: \\t\\treturn newVal, newVal, deferred, diags\\n1491: \\t}\\n1492: \\n1493: \\t// Unmark before sending to provider, will re-mark before returning\\n1494: \\tvar pvm []cty.PathValueMarks\\n1495: \\tconfigVal, pvm = {\\n1514: \\t\\treturn h.PreApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, plans.Read, cty.NullVal(configVal.Type()), configVal)\\n1515: \\t}))\\n1516: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n1517: While data sources are read-only, we need to start with the prior\\\\n1652: // state to determine if we have a change or not.  If we needed to read a new\\\\n1653: // value, but it still matches the previous state, then we can record a NoNop\\\\n1654: // change. If the states don't match then we record a Read change so that the\\\\n1655: // new value is applied to the state.\\\\n1656: //\\\\n1657: // The cases where a data source will generate a planned change instead\\\\n1658: // of finishing during the plan are:\\\\n1659: //\\\\n1660: //   - Its config has unknown values or it depends on a resource with pending changes.\\\\n1661: //     (Note that every data source that is DeferredPrereq should also fit this description.)\\\\n1662: //   - We attempted a read request, but the provider says we're deferred.\\\\n1663: //   - It's nested in a check block, and should always read again during apply.\\\\n1664: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) planDataSource(ctx EvalContext, checkRuleSeverity tfdiags.Severity, skipPlanChanges, dependencyDeferred bool) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, *states.ResourceInstanceObject, *providers.Deferred, instances.RepetitionData, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\\\n1665: \\\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\\\n1666: \\\\tvar keyData instances.RepetitionData\\\\n1667: \\\\tvar configVal cty.Value\\\\n1668: \\\\n1669: \\\\tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\\\\n1670: \\\\tif dependencyDeferred {\\\\n1671: \\\\t\\\\t// If a dependency of this data source was deferred, then we're going\\\\n1672: \\\\t\\\\t// to end up deferring this whatever happens. So, our default status\\\\n1673: \\\\t\\\\t// is deferred. If the provider indicates this resource should be\\\\n1674: \\\\t\\\\t// deferred for another reason, that reason should take priority over\\\\n1675: \\\\t\\\\t// this one.\\\\n1676: \\\\t\\\\tdeferred = \\\\u0026providers.Deferred{\\\\n1677: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tReason: providers.DeferredReasonDeferredPrereq,\\\\n1678: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1679: \\\\t}\\\\n1680: \\\\n1681: \\\\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\\\n1682: \\\\tif err != nil {\\\\n1683: \\\\t\\\\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags.Append(err)\\\\n1684: \\\\t}\\\\n1685: \\\\n1686: \\\\tconfig := *n.Config\\\\n1687: \\\\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource)\\\\n1688: \\\\tif schema == nil {\\\\n1689: \\\\t\\\\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\\\\n1690: \\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"provider %q does not support data source %q\\\\\\\", n.ResolvedProvider, n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource.Type))\\\\n1691: \\\\t\\\\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\\\\n1692: \\\\t}\\\\n1693: \\\\n1694: \\\\tobjTy := schema.ImpliedType()\\\\n1695: \\\\tpriorVal := cty.NullVal(objTy)\\\\n1696: \\\\n1697: \\\\tforEach, _, _ := evaluateForEachExpression(config.ForEach, ctx, false)\\\\n1698: \\\\tkeyData = EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, forEach)\\\\n1699: \\\\n1700: \\\\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\\\\n1701: \\\\t\\\\taddrs.ResourcePrecondition,\\\\n1702: \\\\t\\\\tn.Config.Preconditions,\\\\n1703: \\\\t\\\\tctx, n.Addr, keyData,\\\\n1704: \\\\t\\\\tcheckRuleSeverity,\\\\n1705: \\\\t)\\\\n1706: \\\\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\\\\n1707: \\\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\\\n1708: \\\\t\\\\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags // failed preconditions prevent further evaluation\\\\n1709: \\\\t}\\\\n1710: \\\\n1711: \\\\tvar configDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\\\n1712: \\\\tconfigVal, _, configDiags = ctx.EvaluateBlock(config.Config, schema, nil, keyData)\\\\n1713: \\\\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\\\\n1714: \\\\tdiags = diags.Append(\\\\n1715: \\\\t\\\\tvalidateResourceForbiddenEphemeralValues(ctx, configVal, schema).InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, n.Addr.String()),\\\\n1716: \\\\t)\\\\n1717: \\\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\\\n1718: \\\\t\\\\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\\\\n1719: \\\\t}\\\\n1720: \\\\tunmarkedConfigVal, unmarkedPaths := configVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\\\\n1721: \\\\n1722: \\\\tcheck, nested := n.nestedInCheckBlock()\\\\n1723: \\\\tif nested {\\\\n1724: \\\\t\\\\t// Going forward from this point, the only reason we will fail is\\\\n1725: \\\\t\\\\t// that the data source fails to load its data. Normally, this would\\\\n1726: \\\\t\\\\t// cancel the entire plan and this error message would bubble its way\\\\n1727: \\\\t\\\\t// back up to the user.\\\\n1728: \\\\t\\\\t//\\\\n1729: \\\\t\\\\t// But, if we are in a check block then we don't want this data block to\\\\n1730: \\\\t\\\\t// cause the plan to fail. We also need to report a status on the data\\\\n1731: \\\\t\\\\t// block so the check processing later on knows whether to attempt to\\\\n1732: \\\\t\\\\t// process the checks. Either we'll report the data block as failed\\\\n1733: \\\\t\\\\t// if/when we load the data block later, or we want to report it as a\\\\n1734: \\\\t\\\\t// success overall.\\\\n1735: \\\\t\\\\t//\\\\n1736: \\\\t\\\\t// Therefore, we create a deferred function here that will check if the\\\\n1737: \\\\t\\\\t// status for the check has been updated yet, and if not we will set it\\\\n1738: \\\\t\\\\t// to be StatusPass. The rest of this function will only update the\\\\n1739: \\\\t\\\\t// status if it should be StatusFail.\\\\n1740: \\\\t\\\\tdefer func() {\\\\n1741: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tstatus := ctx.Checks().ObjectCheckStatus(check.Addr().Absolute(n.Addr.Module))\\\\n1742: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif status == checks.StatusUnknown {\\\\n1743: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tctx.Checks().ReportCheckResult(check.Addr().Absolute(n.Addr.Module), addrs.CheckDataResource, 0, checks.StatusPass)\\\\n1744: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1745: \\\\t\\\\t}()\\\\n1746: \\\\t}\\\\n1747: \\\\n1748: \\\\tconfigKnown := configVal.IsWhollyKnown()\\\\n1749: \\\\tdepsPending := n.dependenciesHavePendingChanges(ctx)\\\\n1750: \\\\t// If our configuration contains any unknown values, or we depend on any\\\\n1751: \\\\t// unknown values then we must defer the read to the apply phase by\\\\n1752: \\\\t// producing a \\\\\\\"Read\\\\\\\" change for this resource, and a placeholder value for\\\\n1753: \\\\t// it in the state.\\\\n1754: \\\\tif depsPending || !configKnown {\\\\n1755: \\\\t\\\\t// We can't plan any changes if we're only refreshing, so the only\\\\n1756: \\\\t\\\\t// value we can set here is whatever was in state previously.\\\\n1757: \\\\t\\\\tif skipPlanChanges {\\\\n1758: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tplannedNewState := \\\\u0026states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\\\n1759: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tValue:  priorVal,\\\\n1760: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\\\\n1761: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1762: \\\\n1763: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn nil, plannedNewState, deferred, keyData, diags\\\\n1764: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1765: \\\\n1766: \\\\t\\\\tvar reason plans.ResourceInstanceChangeActionReason\\\\n1767: \\\\t\\\\tswitch {\\\\n1768: \\\\t\\\\tcase !configKnown:\\\\n1769: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tlog.Printf(\\\\\\\"[TRACE] planDataSource: %s configuration not fully known yet, so deferring to apply phase\\\\\\\", n.Addr)\\\\n1770: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treason = plans.ResourceInstanceReadBecauseConfigUnknown\\\\n1771: \\\\t\\\\tcase depsPending:\\\\n1772: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// NOTE: depsPending can be true at the same time as configKnown\\\\n1773: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// is false; configKnown takes precedence because it's more\\\\n1774: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// specific.\\\\n1775: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tlog.Printf(\\\\\\\"[TRACE] planDataSource: %s configuration is fully known, at least one dependency has changes pending\\\\\\\", n.Addr)\\\\n1776: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treason = plans.ResourceInstanceReadBecauseDependencyPending\\\\n1777: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1778: \\\\n1779: \\\\t\\\\tproposedNewVal := objchange.PlannedDataResourceObject(schema, unmarkedConfigVal)\\\\n1780: \\\\n1781: \\\\t\\\\t// even though we are only returning the config value because we can't\\\\n1782: \\\\t\\\\t// yet read the data source, we need to incorporate the schema marks so\\\\n1783: \\\\t\\\\t// that downstream consumers can detect them when planning.\\\\n1784: \\\\t\\\\tproposedNewVal = proposedNewVal.MarkWithPaths(unmarkedPaths)\\\\n1785: \\\\t\\\\tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(proposedNewVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\\\\n1786: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tproposedNewVal = marks.MarkPaths(proposedNewVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\\\\n1787: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1788: \\\\n1789: \\\\t\\\\t// Apply detects that the data source will need to be read by the After\\\\n1790: \\\\t\\\\t// value containing unknowns from PlanDataResourceObject.\\\\n1791: \\\\t\\\\tplannedChange := \\\\u0026plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\\\\n1792: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tAddr:         n.Addr,\\\\n1793: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tPrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\\\\n1794: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\\\\n1795: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tChange: plans.Change{\\\\n1796: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tAction: plans.Read,\\\\n1797: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tBefore: priorVal,\\\\n1798: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tAfter:  proposedNewVal,\\\\n1799: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t},\\\\n1800: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tActionReason: reason,\\\\n1801: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1802: \\\\n1803: \\\\t\\\\tplannedNewState := \\\\u0026states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\\\n1804: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tValue:  proposedNewVal,\\\\n1805: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tStatus: states.ObjectPlanned,\\\\n1806: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1807: \\\\n1808: \\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\\\n1809: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn h.PostDiff(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, plans.Read, priorVal, proposedNewVal)\\\\n1810: \\\\t\\\\t}))\\\\n1811: \\\\n1812: \\\\t\\\\treturn plannedChange, plannedNewState, deferred, keyData, diags\\\\n1813: \\\\t}\\\\n1814: \\\\n1815: \\\\t// We have a complete configuration with no dependencies to wait on, so we\\\\n1816: \\\\t// can read the data source into the state.\\\\n1817: \\\\t// newVal is fully marked by the readDataSource method.\\\\n1818: \\\\tnewVal, readDeferred, readDiags := n.readDataSource(ctx, configVal)\\\\n1819: \\\\n1820: \\\\tif readDeferred != nil {\\\\n1821: \\\\t\\\\t// This will either be null or a value that indicates we're deferred\\\\n1822: \\\\t\\\\t// because of a dependency. In both cases we're happy to just overwrite\\\\n1823: \\\\t\\\\t// that with the more relevant information directly from the provider.\\\\n1824: \\\\t\\\\tdeferred = readDeferred\\\\n1825: \\\\t}\\\\n1826: \\\\n1827: \\\\t// Now we've loaded the data, and diags tells us whether we were successful\\\\n1828: \\\\t// or not, we are going to create our plannedChange and our\\\\n1829: \\\\t// proposedNewState.\\\\n1830: \\\\tvar plannedChange *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\\\\n1831: \\\\tvar plannedNewState *states.ResourceInstanceObject\\\\n1832: \\\\n1833: \\\\t// If we are a nested block, then we want to create a plannedChange that\\\\n1834: \\\\t// tells Terraform to reload the data block during the apply stage even if\\\\n1835: \\\\t// we managed to get the data now.\\\\n1836: \\\\t// Another consideration is that if we failed to load the data, we need to\\\\n1837: \\\\t// disguise that for a nested block. Nested blocks will report the overall\\\\n1838: \\\\t// check as failed but won't affect the rest of the plan operation or block\\\\n1839: \\\\t// an apply operation.\\\\n1840: \\\\n1841: \\\\tif nested {\\\\n1842: \\\\t\\\\taddr := check.Addr().Absolute(n.Addr.Module)\\\\n1843: \\\\n1844: \\\\t\\\\t// Let's fix things up for a nested data block.\\\\n1845: \\\\t\\\\t//\\\\n1846: \\\\t\\\\t// A nested data block doesn't error, and creates a planned change. So,\\\\n1847: \\\\t\\\\t// if we encountered an error we'll tidy up newVal so it makes sense\\\\n1848: \\\\t\\\\t// and handle the error. We'll also create the plannedChange if\\\\n1849: \\\\t\\\\t// appropriate.\\\\n1850: \\\\n1851: \\\\t\\\\tif readDiags.HasErrors() {\\\\n1852: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// If we had errors, then we can cover that up by marking the new\\\\n1853: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// state as unknown.\\\\n1854: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tnewVal = objchange.PlannedDataResourceObject(schema, unmarkedConfigVal)\\\\n1855: \\\\n1856: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// not only do we want to ensure this synthetic value has the marks,\\\\n1857: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// but since this is the value being returned from the data source\\\\n1858: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// we need to ensure the schema marks are added as well.\\\\n1859: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tnewVal = newVal.MarkWithPaths(unmarkedPaths)\\\\n1860: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(newVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\\\\n1861: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tnewVal = marks.MarkPaths(newVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\\\\n1862: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1863: \\\\n1864: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// We still want to report the check as failed even if we are still\\\\n1865: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// letting it run again during the apply stage.\\\\n1866: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tctx.Checks().ReportCheckFailure(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0, readDiags.Err().Error())\\\\n1867: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1868: \\\\n1869: \\\\t\\\\t// Any warning or error diagnostics we'll wrap with some special checks\\\\n1870: \\\\t\\\\t// diagnostics. This is so we can identify them later, and so they'll\\\\n1871: \\\\t\\\\t// only report as warnings.\\\\n1872: \\\\t\\\\treadDiags = tfdiags.OverrideAll(readDiags, tfdiags.Warning, func() tfdiags.DiagnosticExtraWrapper {\\\\n1873: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn \\\\u0026addrs.CheckRuleDiagnosticExtra{\\\\n1874: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tCheckRule: addrs.NewCheckRule(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0),\\\\n1875: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1876: \\\\t\\\\t})\\\\n1877: \\\\n1878: \\\\t\\\\t// refreshOnly plans cannot produce planned changes, so we only do\\\\n1879: \\\\t\\\\t// this if skipPlanChanges is false. Conversely, provider-deferred data\\\\n1880: \\\\t\\\\t// sources always generate a planned change with a different ActionReason.\\\\n1881: \\\\t\\\\tif !skipPlanChanges \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 deferred == nil {\\\\n1882: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tplannedChange = \\\\u0026plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\\\\n1883: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tAddr:         n.Addr,\\\\n1884: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tPrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\\\\n1885: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\\\\n1886: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tChange: plans.Change{\\\\n1887: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tAction: plans.Read,\\\\n1888: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tBefore: priorVal,\\\\n1889: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tAfter:  newVal,\\\\n1890: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t},\\\\n1891: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tActionReason: plans.ResourceInstanceReadBecauseCheckNested,\\\\n1892: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1893: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1894: \\\\t}\\\\n1895: \\\\n1896: \\\\t// Provider-deferred data sources always generate a planned change.\\\\n1897: \\\\tif deferred != nil {\\\\n1898: \\\\t\\\\tplannedChange = \\\\u0026plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\\\\n1899: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tAddr:         n.Addr,\\\\n1900: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tPrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\\\\n1901: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\\\\n1902: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tChange: plans.Change{\\\\n1903: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tAction: plans.Read,\\\\n1904: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tBefore: priorVal,\\\\n1905: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tAfter:  newVal,\\\\n1906: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t},\\\\n1907: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// The caller should be more interested in the deferral reason, but this\\\\n1908: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// action reason is a reasonable description of what's happening.\\\\n1909: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tActionReason: plans.ResourceInstanceReadBecauseDependencyPending,\\\\n1910: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1911: \\\\n1912: \\\\t\\\\tplannedNewState = \\\\u0026states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\\\n1913: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tValue:  newVal,\\\\n1914: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tStatus: states.ObjectPlanned,\\\\n1915: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1916: \\\\t}\\\\n1917: \\\\n1918: \\\\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\\\\n1919: \\\\tif !diags.HasErrors() \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 deferred == nil {\\\\n1920: \\\\t\\\\t// Finally, let's make our new state.\\\\n1921: \\\\t\\\\tplannedNewState = \\\\u0026states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\\\n1922: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tValue:  newVal,\\\\n1923: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\\\\n1924: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1925: \\\\t}\\\\n1926: \\\\n1927: \\\\treturn plannedChange, plannedNewState, deferred, keyData, diags\\\\n1928: }\\\\n1929: \\\\n1930: // nestedInCheckBlock determines if this resource is nested in a Check config\\\\n1931: // block. If so, this resource will be loaded during both plan and apply\\\\n1932: // operations to make sure the check is always giving the latest information.\\\\n1933: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) nestedInCheckBlock() (*configs.Check, bool) {\\\\n1934: \\\\tif n.Config.Container != nil {\\\\n1935: \\\\t\\\\tcheck, ok := n.Config.Container.(*configs.Check)\\\\n1936: \\\\t\\\\treturn check, ok\\\\n1937: \\\\t}\\\\n1938: \\\\treturn nil, false\\\\n1939: }\\\\n1940: \\\\n1941: // dependenciesHavePendingChanges determines whether any managed resource the\\\\n1942: // receiver depends on has a change pending in the plan, in which case we'd\\\\n1943: // need to override the usual behavior of immediately reading from the data\\\\n1944: // source where possible, and instead defer the read until the apply step.\\\\n1945: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dependenciesHavePendingChanges(ctx EvalContext) bool {\\\\n1946: \\\\tnModInst := n.Addr.Module\\\\n1947: \\\\tnMod := nModInst.Module()\\\\n1948: \\\\n1949: \\\\t// Check and see if any depends_on dependencies have\\\\n1950: \\\\t// changes, since they won't show up as changes in the\\\\n1951: \\\\t// configuration.\\\\n1952: \\\\tchanges := ctx.Changes()\\\\n1953: \\\\n1954: \\\\tdepsToUse := n.dependsOn\\\\n1955: \\\\n1956: \\\\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.DataResourceMode {\\\\n1957: \\\\t\\\\tif n.Config.HasCustomConditions() {\\\\n1958: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// For a data resource with custom conditions we need to look at\\\\n1959: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// the full set of resource dependencies -- both direct and\\\\n1960: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// indirect -- because an upstream update might be what's needed\\\\n1961: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// in order to make a condition pass.\\\\n1962: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdepsToUse = n.Dependencies\\\\n1963: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1964: \\\\t}\\\\n1965: \\\\n1966: \\\\tfor _, d := range depsToUse {\\\\n1967: \\\\t\\\\tif d.Resource.Mode == addrs.DataResourceMode {\\\\n1968: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// Data sources have no external side effects, so they pose a need\\\\n1969: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// to delay this read. If they do have a change planned, it must be\\\\n1970: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// because of a dependency on a managed resource, in which case\\\\n1971: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// we'll also encounter it in this list of dependencies.\\\\n1972: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tcontinue\\\\n1973: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1974: \\\\n1975: \\\\t\\\\tfor _, change := range changes.GetChangesForConfigResource(d) {\\\\n1976: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tchangeModInst := change.Addr.Module\\\\n1977: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tchangeMod := changeModInst.Module()\\\\n1978: \\\\n1979: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif changeMod.Equal(nMod) \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 !changeModInst.Equal(nModInst) {\\\\n1980: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// Dependencies are tracked by configuration address, which\\\\n1981: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// means we may have changes from other instances of parent\\\\n1982: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// modules. The actual reference can only take effect within\\\\n1983: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// the same module instance, so skip any that aren't an exact\\\\n1984: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// match\\\\n1985: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tcontinue\\\\n1986: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1987: \\\\n1988: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif change != nil \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 change.Action != plans.NoOp {\\\\n1989: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn true\\\\n1990: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1991: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n1992: \\\\t}\\\\n1993: \\\\treturn false\\\\n1994: }\\\\n1995: \\\\n1996: // apply deals with the main part of the data resource lifecycle: either\\\\n1997: // actually reading from the data source or generating a plan to do so.\\\\n1998: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) applyDataSource(ctx EvalContext, planned *plans.ResourceInstanceChange) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, instances.RepetitionData, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\\\n1999: \\\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\\\n2000: \\\\tvar keyData instances.RepetitionData\\\\n2001: \\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go%3FL1600-2000\\\"}\", \"annotations\": null, \"meta\": null}], \"structuredContent\": {\"content\": \"1600: \\t\\t\\t// Note that this may cause set elements to be coalesced if they\\n1601: \\t\\t\\t// differed only by having unknown values, but we don't worry about\\n1602: \\t\\t\\t// that here because we're saving the value only for inspection\\n1603: \\t\\t\\t// purposes; the error we added above will halt the graph walk.\\n1604: \\t\\t\\tnewVal = cty.UnknownAsNull(newVal)\\n1605: \\t\\t}\\n1606: \\t}\\n1607: \\tnewVal = newVal.MarkWithPaths(pvm)\\n1608: \\tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(newVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\\n1609: \\t\\tnewVal = marks.MarkPaths(newVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\\n1610: \\t}\\n1611: \\n1612: \\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n1613: \\t\\treturn h.PostApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, newVal, diags.Err())\\n1614: \\t}))\\n1615: \\n1616: \\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\\n1617: }\\n1618: \\n1619: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) providerMetas(ctx EvalContext) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n1620: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n1621: \\tmetaConfigVal := cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType)\\n1622: \\n1623: \\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n1624: \\tif err != nil {\\n1625: \\t\\treturn metaConfigVal, diags.Append(err)\\n1626: \\t}\\n1627: \\tif n.ProviderMetas != nil {\\n1628: \\t\\tif m, ok := n.ProviderMetas[n.ResolvedProvider.Provider]; ok && m != nil {\\n1629: \\t\\t\\t// if the provider doesn't support this feature, throw an error\\n1630: \\t\\t\\tif providerSchema.ProviderMeta.Block == nil {\\n1631: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n1632: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\\n1633: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSummary:  fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Provider %s doesn't support provider_meta\\\", n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.String()),\\n1634: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(\\\"The resource %s belongs to a provider that doesn't support provider_meta blocks\\\", n.Addr.Resource),\\n1635: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSubject:  &m.ProviderRange,\\n1636: \\t\\t\\t\\t})\\n1637: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n1638: \\t\\t\\t\\tvar configDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n1639: \\t\\t\\t\\tmetaConfigVal, _, configDiags = ctx.EvaluateBlock(m.Config, providerSchema.ProviderMeta.Block, nil, EvalDataForNoInstanceKey)\\n1640: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\\n1641: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1642: \\t\\t}\\n1643: \\t}\\n1644: \\treturn metaConfigVal, diags\\n1645: }\\n1646: \\n1647: // planDataSource deals with the main part of the data resource lifecycle:\\n1648: // either actually reading from the data source or generating a plan to do so.\\n1649: //\\n1650: // currentState is the current state for the data source, and the new state is\\n1651: // returned. While data sources are read-only, we need to start with the prior\\n1652: // state to determine if we have a change or not.  If we needed to read a new\\n1653: // value, but it still matches the previous state, then we can record a NoNop\\n1654: // change. If the states don't match then we record a Read change so that the\\n1655: // new value is applied to the state.\\n1656: //\\n1657: // The cases where a data source will generate a planned change instead\\n1658: // of finishing during the plan are:\\n1659: //\\n1660: //   - Its config has unknown values or it depends on a resource with pending changes.\\n1661: //     (Note that every data source that is DeferredPrereq should also fit this description.)\\n1662: //   - We attempted a read request, but the provider says we're deferred.\\n1663: //   - It's nested in a check block, and should always read again during apply.\\n1664: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) planDataSource(ctx EvalContext, checkRuleSeverity tfdiags.Severity, skipPlanChanges, dependencyDeferred bool) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, *states.ResourceInstanceObject, *providers.Deferred, instances.RepetitionData, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n1665: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n1666: \\tvar keyData instances.RepetitionData\\n1667: \\tvar configVal cty.Value\\n1668: \\n1669: \\tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\\n1670: \\tif dependencyDeferred {\\n1671: \\t\\t// If a dependency of this data source was deferred, then we're going\\n1672: \\t\\t// to end up deferring this whatever happens. So, our default status\\n1673: \\t\\t// is deferred. If the provider indicates this resource should be\\n1674: \\t\\t// deferred for another reason, that reason should take priority over\\n1675: \\t\\t// this one.\\n1676: \\t\\tdeferred = &providers.Deferred{\\n1677: \\t\\t\\tReason: providers.DeferredReasonDeferredPrereq,\\n1678: \\t\\t}\\n1679: \\t}\\n1680: \\n1681: \\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n1682: \\tif err != nil {\\n1683: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags.Append(err)\\n1684: \\t}\\n1685: \\n1686: \\tconfig := *n.Config\\n1687: \\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource)\\n1688: \\tif schema == nil {\\n1689: \\t\\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\\n1690: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"provider %q does not support data source %q\\\", n.ResolvedProvider, n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource.Type))\\n1691: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\\n1692: \\t}\\n1693: \\n1694: \\tobjTy := schema.ImpliedType()\\n1695: \\tpriorVal := cty.NullVal(objTy)\\n1696: \\n1697: \\tforEach, _, _ := evaluateForEachExpression(config.ForEach, ctx, false)\\n1698: \\tkeyData = EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, forEach)\\n1699: \\n1700: \\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\\n1701: \\t\\taddrs.ResourcePrecondition,\\n1702: \\t\\tn.Config.Preconditions,\\n1703: \\t\\tctx, n.Addr, keyData,\\n1704: \\t\\tcheckRuleSeverity,\\n1705: \\t)\\n1706: \\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\\n1707: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n1708: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags // failed preconditions prevent further evaluation\\n1709: \\t}\\n1710: \\n1711: \\tvar configDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n1712: \\tconfigVal, _, configDiags = ctx.EvaluateBlock(config.Config, schema, nil, keyData)\\n1713: \\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\\n1714: \\tdiags = diags.Append(\\n1715: \\t\\tvalidateResourceForbiddenEphemeralValues(ctx, configVal, schema).InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, n.Addr.String()),\\n1716: \\t)\\n1717: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n1718: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\\n1719: \\t}\\n1720: \\tunmarkedConfigVal, unmarkedPaths := configVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\\n1721: \\n1722: \\tcheck, nested := n.nestedInCheckBlock()\\n1723: \\tif nested {\\n1724: \\t\\t// Going forward from this point, the only reason we will fail is\\n1725: \\t\\t// that the data source fails to load its data. Normally, this would\\n1726: \\t\\t// cancel the entire plan and this error message would bubble its way\\n1727: \\t\\t// back up to the user.\\n1728: \\t\\t//\\n1729: \\t\\t// But, if we are in a check block then we don't want this data block to\\n1730: \\t\\t// cause the plan to fail. We also need to report a status on the data\\n1731: \\t\\t// block so the check processing later on knows whether to attempt to\\n1732: \\t\\t// process the checks. Either we'll report the data block as failed\\n1733: \\t\\t// if/when we load the data block later, or we want to report it as a\\n1734: \\t\\t// success overall.\\n1735: \\t\\t//\\n1736: \\t\\t// Therefore, we create a deferred function here that will check if the\\n1737: \\t\\t// status for the check has been updated yet, and if not we will set it\\n1738: \\t\\t// to be StatusPass. The rest of this function will only update the\\n1739: \\t\\t// status if it should be StatusFail.\\n1740: \\t\\tdefer func() {\\n1741: \\t\\t\\tstatus := ctx.Checks().ObjectCheckStatus(check.Addr().Absolute(n.Addr.Module))\\n1742: \\t\\t\\tif status == checks.StatusUnknown {\\n1743: \\t\\t\\t\\tctx.Checks().ReportCheckResult(check.Addr().Absolute(n.Addr.Module), addrs.CheckDataResource, 0, checks.StatusPass)\\n1744: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1745: \\t\\t}()\\n1746: \\t}\\n1747: \\n1748: \\tconfigKnown := configVal.IsWhollyKnown()\\n1749: \\tdepsPending := n.dependenciesHavePendingChanges(ctx)\\n1750: \\t// If our configuration contains any unknown values, or we depend on any\\n1751: \\t// unknown values then we must defer the read to the apply phase by\\n1752: \\t// producing a \\\"Read\\\" change for this resource, and a placeholder value for\\n1753: \\t// it in the state.\\n1754: \\tif depsPending || !configKnown {\\n1755: \\t\\t// We can't plan any changes if we're only refreshing, so the only\\n1756: \\t\\t// value we can set here is whatever was in state previously.\\n1757: \\t\\tif skipPlanChanges {\\n1758: \\t\\t\\tplannedNewState := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\n1759: \\t\\t\\t\\tValue:  priorVal,\\n1760: \\t\\t\\t\\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\\n1761: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1762: \\n1763: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, plannedNewState, deferred, keyData, diags\\n1764: \\t\\t}\\n1765: \\n1766: \\t\\tvar reason plans.ResourceInstanceChangeActionReason\\n1767: \\t\\tswitch {\\n1768: \\t\\tcase !configKnown:\\n1769: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] planDataSource: %s configuration not fully known yet, so deferring to apply phase\\\", n.Addr)\\n1770: \\t\\t\\treason = plans.ResourceInstanceReadBecauseConfigUnknown\\n1771: \\t\\tcase depsPending:\\n1772: \\t\\t\\t// NOTE: depsPending can be true at the same time as configKnown\\n1773: \\t\\t\\t// is false; configKnown takes precedence because it's more\\n1774: \\t\\t\\t// specific.\\n1775: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] planDataSource: %s configuration is fully known, at least one dependency has changes pending\\\", n.Addr)\\n1776: \\t\\t\\treason = plans.ResourceInstanceReadBecauseDependencyPending\\n1777: \\t\\t}\\n1778: \\n1779: \\t\\tproposedNewVal := objchange.PlannedDataResourceObject(schema, unmarkedConfigVal)\\n1780: \\n1781: \\t\\t// even though we are only returning the config value because we can't\\n1782: \\t\\t// yet read the data source, we need to incorporate the schema marks so\\n1783: \\t\\t// that downstream consumers can detect them when planning.\\n1784: \\t\\tproposedNewVal = proposedNewVal.MarkWithPaths(unmarkedPaths)\\n1785: \\t\\tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(proposedNewVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\\n1786: \\t\\t\\tproposedNewVal = marks.MarkPaths(proposedNewVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\\n1787: \\t\\t}\\n1788: \\n1789: \\t\\t// Apply detects that the data source will need to be read by the After\\n1790: \\t\\t// value containing unknowns from PlanDataResourceObject.\\n1791: \\t\\tplannedChange := &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\\n1792: \\t\\t\\tAddr:         n.Addr,\\n1793: \\t\\t\\tPrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\\n1794: \\t\\t\\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\\n1795: \\t\\t\\tChange: plans.Change{\\n1796: \\t\\t\\t\\tAction: plans.Read,\\n1797: \\t\\t\\t\\tBefore: priorVal,\\n1798: \\t\\t\\t\\tAfter:  proposedNewVal,\\n1799: \\t\\t\\t},\\n1800: \\t\\t\\tActionReason: reason,\\n1801: \\t\\t}\\n1802: \\n1803: \\t\\tplannedNewState := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\n1804: \\t\\t\\tValue:  proposedNewVal,\\n1805: \\t\\t\\tStatus: states.ObjectPlanned,\\n1806: \\t\\t}\\n1807: \\n1808: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n1809: \\t\\t\\treturn h.PostDiff(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, plans.Read, priorVal, proposedNewVal)\\n1810: \\t\\t}))\\n1811: \\n1812: \\t\\treturn plannedChange, plannedNewState, deferred, keyData, diags\\n1813: \\t}\\n1814: \\n1815: \\t// We have a complete configuration with no dependencies to wait on, so we\\n1816: \\t// can read the data source into the state.\\n1817: \\t// newVal is fully marked by the readDataSource method.\\n1818: \\tnewVal, readDeferred, readDiags := n.readDataSource(ctx, configVal)\\n1819: \\n1820: \\tif readDeferred != nil {\\n1821: \\t\\t// This will either be null or a value that indicates we're deferred\\n1822: \\t\\t// because of a dependency. In both cases we're happy to just overwrite\\n1823: \\t\\t// that with the more relevant information directly from the provider.\\n1824: \\t\\tdeferred = readDeferred\\n1825: \\t}\\n1826: \\n1827: \\t// Now we've loaded the data, and diags tells us whether we were successful\\n1828: \\t// or not, we are going to create our plannedChange and our\\n1829: \\t// proposedNewState.\\n1830: \\tvar plannedChange *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\\n1831: \\tvar plannedNewState *states.ResourceInstanceObject\\n1832: \\n1833: \\t// If we are a nested block, then we want to create a plannedChange that\\n1834: \\t// tells Terraform to reload the data block during the apply stage even if\\n1835: \\t// we managed to get the data now.\\n1836: \\t// Another consideration is that if we failed to load the data, we need to\\n1837: \\t// disguise that for a nested block. Nested blocks will report the overall\\n1838: \\t// check as failed but won't affect the rest of the plan operation or block\\n1839: \\t// an apply operation.\\n1840: \\n1841: \\tif nested {\\n1842: \\t\\taddr := check.Addr().Absolute(n.Addr.Module)\\n1843: \\n1844: \\t\\t// Let's fix things up for a nested data block.\\n1845: \\t\\t//\\n1846: \\t\\t// A nested data block doesn't error, and creates a planned change. So,\\n1847: \\t\\t// if we encountered an error we'll tidy up newVal so it makes sense\\n1848: \\t\\t// and handle the error. We'll also create the plannedChange if\\n1849: \\t\\t// appropriate.\\n1850: \\n1851: \\t\\tif readDiags.HasErrors() {\\n1852: \\t\\t\\t// If we had errors, then we can cover that up by marking the new\\n1853: \\t\\t\\t// state as unknown.\\n1854: \\t\\t\\tnewVal = objchange.PlannedDataResourceObject(schema, unmarkedConfigVal)\\n1855: \\n1856: \\t\\t\\t// not only do we want to ensure this synthetic value has the marks,\\n1857: \\t\\t\\t// but since this is the value being returned from the data source\\n1858: \\t\\t\\t// we need to ensure the schema marks are added as well.\\n1859: \\t\\t\\tnewVal = newVal.MarkWithPaths(unmarkedPaths)\\n1860: \\t\\t\\tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(newVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\\n1861: \\t\\t\\t\\tnewVal = marks.MarkPaths(newVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\\n1862: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1863: \\n1864: \\t\\t\\t// We still want to report the check as failed even if we are still\\n1865: \\t\\t\\t// letting it run again during the apply stage.\\n1866: \\t\\t\\tctx.Checks().ReportCheckFailure(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0, readDiags.Err().Error())\\n1867: \\t\\t}\\n1868: \\n1869: \\t\\t// Any warning or error diagnostics we'll wrap with some special checks\\n1870: \\t\\t// diagnostics. This is so we can identify them later, and so they'll\\n1871: \\t\\t// only report as warnings.\\n1872: \\t\\treadDiags = tfdiags.OverrideAll(readDiags, tfdiags.Warning, func() tfdiags.DiagnosticExtraWrapper {\\n1873: \\t\\t\\treturn &addrs.CheckRuleDiagnosticExtra{\\n1874: \\t\\t\\t\\tCheckRule: addrs.NewCheckRule(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0),\\n1875: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1876: \\t\\t})\\n1877: \\n1878: \\t\\t// refreshOnly plans cannot produce planned changes, so we only do\\n1879: \\t\\t// this if skipPlanChanges is false. Conversely, provider-deferred data\\n1880: \\t\\t// sources always generate a planned change with a different ActionReason.\\n1881: \\t\\tif !skipPlanChanges && deferred == nil {\\n1882: \\t\\t\\tplannedChange = &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\\n1883: \\t\\t\\t\\tAddr:         n.Addr,\\n1884: \\t\\t\\t\\tPrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\\n1885: \\t\\t\\t\\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\\n1886: \\t\\t\\t\\tChange: plans.Change{\\n1887: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tAction: plans.Read,\\n1888: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tBefore: priorVal,\\n1889: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tAfter:  newVal,\\n1890: \\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n1891: \\t\\t\\t\\tActionReason: plans.ResourceInstanceReadBecauseCheckNested,\\n1892: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1893: \\t\\t}\\n1894: \\t}\\n1895: \\n1896: \\t// Provider-deferred data sources always generate a planned change.\\n1897: \\tif deferred != nil {\\n1898: \\t\\tplannedChange = &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\\n1899: \\t\\t\\tAddr:         n.Addr,\\n1900: \\t\\t\\tPrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\\n1901: \\t\\t\\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\\n1902: \\t\\t\\tChange: plans.Change{\\n1903: \\t\\t\\t\\tAction: plans.Read,\\n1904: \\t\\t\\t\\tBefore: priorVal,\\n1905: \\t\\t\\t\\tAfter:  newVal,\\n1906: \\t\\t\\t},\\n1907: \\t\\t\\t// The caller should be more interested in the deferral reason, but this\\n1908: \\t\\t\\t// action reason is a reasonable description of what's happening.\\n1909: \\t\\t\\tActionReason: plans.ResourceInstanceReadBecauseDependencyPending,\\n1910: \\t\\t}\\n1911: \\n1912: \\t\\tplannedNewState = &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\n1913: \\t\\t\\tValue:  newVal,\\n1914: \\t\\t\\tStatus: states.ObjectPlanned,\\n1915: \\t\\t}\\n1916: \\t}\\n1917: \\n1918: \\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\\n1919: \\tif !diags.HasErrors() && deferred == nil {\\n1920: \\t\\t// Finally, let's make our new state.\\n1921: \\t\\tplannedNewState = &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\n1922: \\t\\t\\tValue:  newVal,\\n1923: \\t\\t\\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\\n1924: \\t\\t}\\n1925: \\t}\\n1926: \\n1927: \\treturn plannedChange, plannedNewState, deferred, keyData, diags\\n1928: }\\n1929: \\n1930: // nestedInCheckBlock determines if this resource is nested in a Check config\\n1931: // block. If so, this resource will be loaded during both plan and apply\\n1932: // operations to make sure the check is always giving the latest information.\\n1933: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) nestedInCheckBlock() (*configs.Check, bool) {\\n1934: \\tif n.Config.Container != nil {\\n1935: \\t\\tcheck, ok := n.Config.Container.(*configs.Check)\\n1936: \\t\\treturn check, ok\\n1937: \\t}\\n1938: \\treturn nil, false\\n1939: }\\n1940: \\n1941: // dependenciesHavePendingChanges determines whether any managed resource the\\n1942: // receiver depends on has a change pending in the plan, in which case we'd\\n1943: // need to override the usual behavior of immediately reading from the data\\n1944: // source where possible, and instead defer the read until the apply step.\\n1945: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dependenciesHavePendingChanges(ctx EvalContext) bool {\\n1946: \\tnModInst := n.Addr.Module\\n1947: \\tnMod := nModInst.Module()\\n1948: \\n1949: \\t// Check and see if any depends_on dependencies have\\n1950: \\t// changes, since they won't show up as changes in the\\n1951: \\t// configuration.\\n1952: \\tchanges := ctx.Changes()\\n1953: \\n1954: \\tdepsToUse := n.dependsOn\\n1955: \\n1956: \\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.DataResourceMode {\\n1957: \\t\\tif n.Config.HasCustomConditions() {\\n1958: \\t\\t\\t// For a data resource with custom conditions we need to look at\\n1959: \\t\\t\\t// the full set of resource dependencies -- both direct and\\n1960: \\t\\t\\t// indirect -- because an upstream update might be what's needed\\n1961: \\t\\t\\t// in order to make a condition pass.\\n1962: \\t\\t\\tdepsToUse = n.Dependencies\\n1963: \\t\\t}\\n1964: \\t}\\n1965: \\n1966: \\tfor _, d := range depsToUse {\\n1967: \\t\\tif d.Resource.Mode == addrs.DataResourceMode {\\n1968: \\t\\t\\t// Data sources have no external side effects, so they pose a need\\n1969: \\t\\t\\t// to delay this read. If they do have a change planned, it must be\\n1970: \\t\\t\\t// because of a dependency on a managed resource, in which case\\n1971: \\t\\t\\t// we'll also encounter it in this list of dependencies.\\n1972: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n1973: \\t\\t}\\n1974: \\n1975: \\t\\tfor _, change := range changes.GetChangesForConfigResource(d) {\\n1976: \\t\\t\\tchangeModInst := change.Addr.Module\\n1977: \\t\\t\\tchangeMod := changeModInst.Module()\\n1978: \\n1979: \\t\\t\\tif changeMod.Equal(nMod) && !changeModInst.Equal(nModInst) {\\n1980: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Dependencies are tracked by configuration address, which\\n1981: \\t\\t\\t\\t// means we may have changes from other instances of parent\\n1982: \\t\\t\\t\\t// modules. The actual reference can only take effect within\\n1983: \\t\\t\\t\\t// the same module instance, so skip any that aren't an exact\\n1984: \\t\\t\\t\\t// match\\n1985: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n1986: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1987: \\n1988: \\t\\t\\tif change != nil && change.Action != plans.NoOp {\\n1989: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n1990: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1991: \\t\\t}\\n1992: \\t}\\n1993: \\treturn false\\n1994: }\\n1995: \\n1996: // apply deals with the main part of the data resource lifecycle: either\\n1997: // actually reading from the data source or generating a plan to do so.\\n1998: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) applyDataSource(ctx EvalContext, planned *plans.ResourceInstanceChange) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, instances.RepetitionData, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n1999: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2000: \\tvar keyData instances.RepetitionData\\n2001: \", \"url\": \"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go%3FL1600-2000\"}, \"isError\": false}"
