nested {\\\\n2056: \\\\t\\\\taddr := check.Addr().Absolute(n.Addr.Module)\\\\n2057: \\\\n2058: \\\\t\\\\t// We're just going to jump in here and hide away any errors for nested\\\\n2059: \\\\t\\\\t// data blocks.\\\\n2060: \\\\t\\\\tif readDiags.HasErrors() {\\\\n2061: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tctx.Checks().ReportCheckFailure(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0, readDiags.Err().Error())\\\\n2062: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.OverrideAll(readDiags, tfdiags.Warning, func() tfdiags.DiagnosticExtraWrapper {\\\\n2063: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn \\\\u0026addrs.CheckRuleDiagnosticExtra{\\\\n2064: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tCheckRule: addrs.NewCheckRule(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0),\\\\n2065: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2066: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}))\\\\n2067: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn nil, keyData, diags\\\\n2068: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2069: \\\\n2070: \\\\t\\\\t// Even though we know there are no errors here, we still want to\\\\n2071: \\\\t\\\\t// identify these diags has having been generated from a check block.\\\\n2072: \\\\t\\\\treadDiags = tfdiags.OverrideAll(readDiags, tfdiags.Warning, func() tfdiags.DiagnosticExtraWrapper {\\\\n2073: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn \\\\u0026addrs.CheckRuleDiagnosticExtra{\\\\n2074: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tCheckRule: addrs.NewCheckRule(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0),\\\\n2075: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2076: \\\\t\\\\t})\\\\n2077: \\\\n2078: \\\\t\\\\t// If no errors, just remember to report this as a success and continue\\\\n2079: \\\\t\\\\t// as normal.\\\\n2080: \\\\t\\\\tctx.Checks().ReportCheckResult(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0, checks.StatusPass)\\\\n2081: \\\\t}\\\\n2082: \\\\n2083: \\\\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\\\\n2084: \\\\tif readDiags.HasErrors() {\\\\n2085: \\\\t\\\\treturn nil, keyData, diags\\\\n2086: \\\\t}\\\\n2087: \\\\n2088: \\\\tif readDeferred != nil {\\\\n2089: \\\\t\\\\t// Just skip data sources that are being deferred. Nothing, that\\\\n2090: \\\\t\\\\t// references them should be calling them.\\\\n2091: \\\\t\\\\treturn nil, keyData, diags\\\\n2092: \\\\t}\\\\n2093: \\\\n2094: \\\\tstate := \\\\u0026states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\\\n2095: \\\\t\\\\tValue:  newVal,\\\\n2096: \\\\t\\\\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\\\\n2097: \\\\t}\\\\n2098: \\\\n2099: \\\\treturn state, keyData, diags\\\\n2100: }\\\\n2101: \\\\n2102: // evalApplyProvisioners determines if provisioners need to be run, and if so\\\\n2103: // executes the provisioners for a resource and returns an updated error if\\\\n2104: // provisioning fails.\\\\n2105: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) evalApplyProvisioners(ctx EvalContext, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, createNew bool, when configs.ProvisionerWhen) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\\\\n2106: \\\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\\\n2107: \\\\n2108: \\\\tif state == nil {\\\\n2109: \\\\t\\\\tlog.Printf(\\\\\\\"[TRACE] evalApplyProvisioners: %s has no state, so skipping provisioners\\\\\\\", n.Addr)\\\\n2110: \\\\t\\\\treturn nil\\\\n2111: \\\\t}\\\\n2112: \\\\tif when == configs.ProvisionerWhenCreate \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 !createNew {\\\\n2113: \\\\t\\\\t// If we're not creating a new resource, then don't run provisioners\\\\n2114: \\\\t\\\\tlog.Printf(\\\\\\\"[TRACE] evalApplyProvisioners: %s is not freshly-created, so no provisioning is required\\\\\\\", n.Addr)\\\\n2115: \\\\t\\\\treturn nil\\\\n2116: \\\\t}\\\\n2117: \\\\tif state.Status == states.ObjectTainted {\\\\n2118: \\\\t\\\\t// No point in provisioning an object that is already tainted, since\\\\n2119: \\\\t\\\\t// it's going to get recreated on the next apply anyway.\\\\n2120: \\\\t\\\\tlog.Printf(\\\\\\\"[TRACE] evalApplyProvisioners: %s is tainted, so skipping provisioning\\\\\\\", n.Addr)\\\\n2121: \\\\t\\\\treturn nil\\\\n2122: \\\\t}\\\\n2123: \\\\n2124: \\\\tvar allProvs []*configs.Provisioner\\\\n2125: \\\\tswitch {\\\\n2126: \\\\tcase n.Config != nil \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 n.Config.Managed != nil:\\\\n2127: \\\\t\\\\tallProvs = n.Config.Managed.Provisioners\\\\n2128: \\\\tcase n.RemovedConfig != nil \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 n.RemovedConfig.Managed != nil:\\\\n2129: \\\\t\\\\tallProvs = n.RemovedConfig.Managed.Provisioners\\\\n2130: \\\\t}\\\\n2131: \\\\n2132: \\\\tprovs := filterProvisioners(allProvs, when)\\\\n2133: \\\\tif len(provs) == 0 {\\\\n2134: \\\\t\\\\t// We have no provisioners, so don't do anything\\\\n2135: \\\\t\\\\treturn nil\\\\n2136: \\\\t}\\\\n2137: \\\\n2138: \\\\t// Call pre hook\\\\n2139: \\\\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\\\n2140: \\\\t\\\\treturn h.PreProvisionInstance(n.HookResourceIdentity(), state.Value)\\\\n2141: \\\\t}))\\\\n2142: \\\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\\\n2143: \\\\t\\\\treturn diags\\\\n2144: \\\\t}\\\\n2145: \\\\n2146: \\\\t// If there are no errors, then we append it to our output error\\\\n2147: \\\\t// if we have one, otherwise we just output it.\\\\n2148: \\\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.applyProvisioners(ctx, state, when, provs))\\\\n2149: \\\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\\\n2150: \\\\t\\\\tlog.Printf(\\\\\\\"[TRACE] evalApplyProvisioners: %s provisioning failed, but we will continue anyway at the caller's request\\\\\\\", n.Addr)\\\\n2151: \\\\t\\\\treturn diags\\\\n2152: \\\\t}\\\\n2153: \\\\n2154: \\\\t// Call post hook\\\\n2155: \\\\treturn diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\\\n2156: \\\\t\\\\treturn h.PostProvisionInstance(n.HookResourceIdentity(), state.Value)\\\\n2157: \\\\t}))\\\\n2158: }\\\\n2159: \\\\n2160: // filterProvisioners filters the provisioners on the resource to only\\\\n2161: // the provisioners specified by the \\\\\\\"when\\\\\\\" option.\\\\n2162: func filterProvisioners(configured []*configs.Provisioner, when configs.ProvisionerWhen) []*configs.Provisioner {\\\\n2163: \\\\tif len(configured) == 0 {\\\\n2164: \\\\t\\\\treturn nil\\\\n2165: \\\\t}\\\\n2166: \\\\n2167: \\\\tresult := make([]*configs.Provisioner, 0, len(configured))\\\\n2168: \\\\tfor _, p := range configured {\\\\n2169: \\\\t\\\\tif p.When == when {\\\\n2170: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tresult = append(result, p)\\\\n2171: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2172: \\\\t}\\\\n2173: \\\\n2174: \\\\treturn result\\\\n2175: }\\\\n2176: \\\\n2177: // applyProvisioners executes the provisioners for a resource.\\\\n2178: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) applyProvisioners(ctx EvalContext, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, when configs.ProvisionerWhen, provs []*configs.Provisioner) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\\\\n2179: \\\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\\\n2180: \\\\n2181: \\\\t// this self is only used for destroy provisioner evaluation, and must\\\\n2182: \\\\t// refer to the last known value of the resource.\\\\n2183: \\\\tself := state.Value\\\\n2184: \\\\n2185: \\\\tvar evalScope func(EvalContext, hcl.Body, cty.Value, *configschema.Block) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics)\\\\n2186: \\\\tswitch when {\\\\n2187: \\\\tcase configs.ProvisionerWhenDestroy:\\\\n2188: \\\\t\\\\tevalScope = n.evalDestroyProvisionerConfig\\\\n2189: \\\\tdefault:\\\\n2190: \\\\t\\\\tevalScope = n.evalProvisionerConfig\\\\n2191: \\\\t}\\\\n2192: \\\\n2193: \\\\t// If there's a connection block defined directly inside the resource block\\\\n2194: \\\\t// then it'll serve as a base connection configuration for all of the\\\\n2195: \\\\t// provisioners.\\\\n2196: \\\\tvar baseConn hcl.Body\\\\n2197: \\\\tswitch {\\\\n2198: \\\\tcase n.Config != nil \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 n.Config.Managed != nil \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 n.Config.Managed.Connection != nil:\\\\n2199: \\\\t\\\\tbaseConn = n.Config.Managed.Connection.Config\\\\n2200: \\\\tcase n.RemovedConfig != nil \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 n.RemovedConfig.Managed != nil \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 n.RemovedConfig.Managed.Connection != nil:\\\\n2201: \\\\t\\\\tbaseConn = n.RemovedConfig.Managed.Connection.Config\\\\n2202: \\\\t}\\\\n2203: \\\\n2204: \\\\tfor _, prov := range provs {\\\\n2205: \\\\t\\\\tlog.Printf(\\\\\\\"[TRACE] applyProvisioners: provisioning %s with %q\\\\\\\", n.Addr, prov.Type)\\\\n2206: \\\\n2207: \\\\t\\\\t// Get the provisioner\\\\n2208: \\\\t\\\\tprovisioner, err := ctx.Provisioner(prov.Type)\\\\n2209: \\\\t\\\\tif err != nil {\\\\n2210: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn diags.Append(err)\\\\n2211: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2212: \\\\n2213: \\\\t\\\\tschema, err := ctx.ProvisionerSchema(prov.Type)\\\\n2214: \\\\t\\\\tif err != nil {\\\\n2215: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// This error probably won't be a great diagnostic, but in practice\\\\n2216: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// we typically catch this problem long before we get here, so\\\\n2217: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// it should be rare to return via this codepath.\\\\n2218: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\\\n2219: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn diags\\\\n2220: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2221: \\\\n2222: \\\\t\\\\tconfig, configDiags := evalScope(ctx, prov.Config, self, schema)\\\\n2223: \\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\\\\n2224: \\\\t\\\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\\\n2225: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn diags\\\\n2226: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2227: \\\\n2228: \\\\t\\\\t// If the provisioner block contains a connection block of its own then\\\\n2229: \\\\t\\\\t// it can override the base connection configuration, if any.\\\\n2230: \\\\t\\\\tvar localConn hcl.Body\\\\n2231: \\\\t\\\\tif prov.Connection != nil {\\\\n2232: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tlocalConn = prov.Connection.Config\\\\n2233: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2234: \\\\n2235: \\\\t\\\\tvar connBody hcl.Body\\\\n2236: \\\\t\\\\tswitch {\\\\n2237: \\\\t\\\\tcase baseConn != nil \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 localConn != nil:\\\\n2238: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// Our standard merging logic applies here, similar to what we do\\\\n2239: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// with _override.tf configuration files: arguments from the\\\\n2240: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// base connection block will be masked by any arguments of the\\\\n2241: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// same name in the local connection block.\\\\n2242: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tconnBody = configs.MergeBodies(baseConn, localConn)\\\\n2243: \\\\t\\\\tcase baseConn != nil:\\\\n2244: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tconnBody = baseConn\\\\n2245: \\\\t\\\\tcase localConn != nil:\\\\n2246: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tconnBody = localConn\\\\n2247: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2248: \\\\n2249: \\\\t\\\\t// start with an empty connInfo\\\\n2250: \\\\t\\\\tconnInfo := cty.NullVal(connectionBlockSupersetSchema.ImpliedType())\\\\n2251: \\\\n2252: \\\\t\\\\tif connBody != nil {\\\\n2253: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tvar connInfoDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\\\n2254: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tconnInfo, connInfoDiags = evalScope(ctx, connBody, self, connectionBlockSupersetSchema)\\\\n2255: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(connInfoDiags)\\\\n2256: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\\\n2257: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn diags\\\\n2258: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2259: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2260: \\\\n2261: \\\\t\\\\t{\\\\n2262: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// Call pre hook\\\\n2263: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\terr := ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\\\n2264: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn h.PreProvisionInstanceStep(n.HookResourceIdentity(), prov.Type)\\\\n2265: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t})\\\\n2266: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif err != nil {\\\\n2267: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn diags.Append(err)\\\\n2268: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2269: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2270: \\\\n2271: \\\\t\\\\t// The output function\\\\n2272: \\\\t\\\\toutputFn := func(msg string) {\\\\n2273: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\\\n2274: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\th.ProvisionOutput(n.HookResourceIdentity(), prov.Type, msg)\\\\n2275: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn HookActionContinue, nil\\\\n2276: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t})\\\\n2277: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2278: \\\\n2279: \\\\t\\\\t// If our config or connection info contains any marked values, ensure\\\\n2280: \\\\t\\\\t// those are stripped out before sending to the provisioner. Unlike\\\\n2281: \\\\t\\\\t// resources, we have no need to capture the marked paths and reapply\\\\n2282: \\\\t\\\\t// later.\\\\n2283: \\\\t\\\\tunmarkedConfig, configMarks := config.UnmarkDeep()\\\\n2284: \\\\t\\\\tunmarkedConnInfo, _ := connInfo.UnmarkDeep()\\\\n2285: \\\\n2286: \\\\t\\\\t// Marks on the config might result in leaking sensitive values through\\\\n2287: \\\\t\\\\t// provisioner logging, so we conservatively suppress all output in\\\\n2288: \\\\t\\\\t// this case. This should not apply to connection info values, which\\\\n2289: \\\\t\\\\t// provisioners ought not to be logging anyway.\\\\n2290: \\\\t\\\\tif len(configMarks) \\\\u003e 0 {\\\\n2291: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\toutputFn = func(msg string) {\\\\n2292: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\\\n2293: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\th.ProvisionOutput(n.HookResourceIdentity(), prov.Type, \\\\\\\"(output suppressed due to sensitive value in config)\\\\\\\")\\\\n2294: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn HookActionContinue, nil\\\\n2295: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t})\\\\n2296: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2297: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2298: \\\\n2299: \\\\t\\\\toutput := CallbackUIOutput{OutputFn: outputFn}\\\\n2300: \\\\t\\\\tresp := provisioner.ProvisionResource(provisioners.ProvisionResourceRequest{\\\\n2301: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tConfig:     unmarkedConfig,\\\\n2302: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tConnection: unmarkedConnInfo,\\\\n2303: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tUIOutput:   \\\\u0026output,\\\\n2304: \\\\t\\\\t})\\\\n2305: \\\\t\\\\tapplyDiags := resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(prov.Config, n.Addr.String())\\\\n2306: \\\\n2307: \\\\t\\\\t// Call post hook\\\\n2308: \\\\t\\\\thookErr := ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\\\n2309: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn h.PostProvisionInstanceStep(n.HookResourceIdentity(), prov.Type, applyDiags.Err())\\\\n2310: \\\\t\\\\t})\\\\n2311: \\\\n2312: \\\\t\\\\tswitch prov.OnFailure {\\\\n2313: \\\\t\\\\tcase configs.ProvisionerOnFailureContinue:\\\\n2314: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif applyDiags.HasErrors() {\\\\n2315: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tlog.Printf(\\\\\\\"[WARN] Errors while provisioning %s with %q, but continuing as requested in configuration\\\\\\\", n.Addr, prov.Type)\\\\n2316: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t} else {\\\\n2317: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// Maybe there are warnings that we still want to see\\\\n2318: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(applyDiags)\\\\n2319: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2320: \\\\t\\\\tdefault:\\\\n2321: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(applyDiags)\\\\n2322: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif applyDiags.HasErrors() {\\\\n2323: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tlog.Printf(\\\\\\\"[WARN] Errors while provisioning %s with %q, so aborting\\\\\\\", n.Addr, prov.Type)\\\\n2324: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn diags\\\\n2325: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2326: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2327: \\\\n2328: \\\\t\\\\t// Deal with the hook\\\\n2329: \\\\t\\\\tif hookErr != nil {\\\\n2330: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn diags.Append(hookErr)\\\\n2331: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2332: \\\\t}\\\\n2333: \\\\n2334: \\\\treturn diags\\\\n2335: }\\\\n2336: \\\\n2337: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) evalProvisionerConfig(ctx EvalContext, body hcl.Body, self cty.Value, schema *configschema.Block) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\\\n2338: \\\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\\\n2339: \\\\n2340: \\\\tforEach, _, forEachDiags := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, false)\\\\n2341: \\\\tdiags = diags.Append(forEachDiags)\\\\n2342: \\\\n2343: \\\\tkeyData := EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, forEach)\\\\n2344: \\\\n2345: \\\\tconfig, _, configDiags := ctx.EvaluateBlock(body, schema, n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource, keyData)\\\\n2346: \\\\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\\\\n2347: \\\\n2348: \\\\treturn config, diags\\\\n2349: }\\\\n2350: \\\\n2351: // during destroy a provisioner can only evaluate within the scope of the parent resource\\\\n2352: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) evalDestroyProvisionerConfig(ctx EvalContext, body hcl.Body, self cty.Value, schema *configschema.Block) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\\\n2353: \\\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\\\n2354: \\\\n2355: \\\\t// For a destroy-time provisioner forEach is intentionally nil here,\\\\n2356: \\\\t// which EvalDataForInstanceKey responds to by not populating EachValue\\\\n2357: \\\\t// in its result. That's okay because each.value is prohibited for\\\\n2358: \\\\t// destroy-time provisioners.\\\\n2359: \\\\tkeyData := EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, nil)\\\\n2360: \\\\n2361: \\\\tevalScope := ctx.EvaluationScope(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource, nil, keyData)\\\\n2362: \\\\tconfig, evalDiags := evalScope.EvalSelfBlock(body, self, schema, keyData)\\\\n2363: \\\\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\\\\n2364: \\\\n2365: \\\\treturn config, diags\\\\n2366: }\\\\n2367: \\\\n2368: // apply accepts an applyConfig, instead of using n.Config, so destroy plans can\\\\n2369: // send a nil config. The keyData information can be empty if the config is\\\\n2370: // nil, since it is only used to evaluate the configuration.\\\\n2371: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) apply(\\\\n2372: \\\\tctx EvalContext,\\\\n2373: \\\\tstate *states.ResourceInstanceObject,\\\\n2374: \\\\tchange *plans.ResourceInstanceChange,\\\\n2375: \\\\tapplyConfig *configs.Resource,\\\\n2376: \\\\tkeyData instances.RepetitionData,\\\\n2377: \\\\tcreateBeforeDestroy bool) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\\\n2378: \\\\n2379: \\\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\\\n2380: \\\\tif state == nil {\\\\n2381: \\\\t\\\\tstate = \\\\u0026states.ResourceInstanceObject{}\\\\n2382: \\\\t}\\\\n2383: \\\\n2384: \\\\tif change.Action == plans.NoOp {\\\\n2385: \\\\t\\\\t// If this is a no-op change then we don't want to actually change\\\\n2386: \\\\t\\\\t// anything, so we'll just echo back the state we were given and\\\\n2387: \\\\t\\\\t// let our internal checks and updates proceed.\\\\n2388: \\\\t\\\\tlog.Printf(\\\\\\\"[TRACE] NodeAbstractResourceInstance.apply: skipping %s because it has no planned action\\\\\\\", n.Addr)\\\\n2389: \\\\t\\\\treturn state, diags\\\\n2390: \\\\t}\\\\n2391: \\\\n2392: \\\\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\\\n2393: \\\\tif err != nil {\\\\n2394: \\\\t\\\\treturn nil, diags.Append(err)\\\\n2395: \\\\t}\\\\n2396: \\\\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceType(n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode, n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type)\\\\n2397: \\\\tif schema == nil {\\\\n2398: \\\\t\\\\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\\\\n2399: \\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"provider does not support resource type %q\\\\\\\", n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type))\\\\n2400: \\\\t\\\\treturn nil, diags\\\\n2401: \\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go%3FL2000-2400\\\"}\", \"annotations\": null, \"meta\": null}], \"structuredContent\": {\"content\": \"2000: \\tvar keyData instances.RepetitionData\\n2001: \\n2002: \\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n2003: \\tif err != nil {\\n2004: \\t\\treturn nil, keyData, diags.Append(err)\\n2005: \\t}\\n2006: \\tif planned != nil && planned.Action != plans.Read && planned.Action != plans.NoOp {\\n2007: \\t\\t// If any other action gets in here then that's always a bug; this\\n2008: \\t\\t// EvalNode only deals with reading.\\n2009: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\n2010: \\t\\t\\t\\\"invalid action %s for %s: only Read is supported (this is a bug in Terraform; please report it!)\\\",\\n2011: \\t\\t\\tplanned.Action, n.Addr,\\n2012: \\t\\t))\\n2013: \\t\\treturn nil, keyData, diags\\n2014: \\t}\\n2015: \\n2016: \\tconfig := *n.Config\\n2017: \\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource)\\n2018: \\tif schema == nil {\\n2019: \\t\\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\\n2020: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"provider %q does not support data source %q\\\", n.ResolvedProvider, n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource.Type))\\n2021: \\t\\treturn nil, keyData, diags\\n2022: \\t}\\n2023: \\n2024: \\tforEach, _, _ := evaluateForEachExpression(config.ForEach, ctx, false)\\n2025: \\tkeyData = EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.Addr.Resource.Key, forEach)\\n2026: \\n2027: \\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\\n2028: \\t\\taddrs.ResourcePrecondition,\\n2029: \\t\\tn.Config.Preconditions,\\n2030: \\t\\tctx, n.Addr, keyData,\\n2031: \\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n2032: \\t)\\n2033: \\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\\n2034: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n2035: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n2036: \\t\\t\\treturn h.PostApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, planned.Before, diags.Err())\\n2037: \\t\\t}))\\n2038: \\t\\treturn nil, keyData, diags // failed preconditions prevent further evaluation\\n2039: \\t}\\n2040: \\n2041: \\tif planned.Action == plans.NoOp {\\n2042: \\t\\t// If we didn't actually plan to read this then we have nothing more\\n2043: \\t\\t// to do; we're evaluating this only for incidentals like the\\n2044: \\t\\t// precondition/postcondition checks.\\n2045: \\t\\treturn nil, keyData, diags\\n2046: \\t}\\n2047: \\n2048: \\tconfigVal, _, configDiags := ctx.EvaluateBlock(config.Config, schema, nil, keyData)\\n2049: \\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\\n2050: \\tif configDiags.HasErrors() {\\n2051: \\t\\treturn nil, keyData, diags\\n2052: \\t}\\n2053: \\n2054: \\tnewVal, readDeferred, readDiags := n.readDataSource(ctx, configVal)\\n2055: \\tif check, nested := n.nestedInCheckBlock(); nested {\\n2056: \\t\\taddr := check.Addr().Absolute(n.Addr.Module)\\n2057: \\n2058: \\t\\t// We're just going to jump in here and hide away any errors for nested\\n2059: \\t\\t// data blocks.\\n2060: \\t\\tif readDiags.HasErrors() {\\n2061: \\t\\t\\tctx.Checks().ReportCheckFailure(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0, readDiags.Err().Error())\\n2062: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.OverrideAll(readDiags, tfdiags.Warning, func() tfdiags.DiagnosticExtraWrapper {\\n2063: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn &addrs.CheckRuleDiagnosticExtra{\\n2064: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tCheckRule: addrs.NewCheckRule(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0),\\n2065: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n2066: \\t\\t\\t}))\\n2067: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, keyData, diags\\n2068: \\t\\t}\\n2069: \\n2070: \\t\\t// Even though we know there are no errors here, we still want to\\n2071: \\t\\t// identify these diags has having been generated from a check block.\\n2072: \\t\\treadDiags = tfdiags.OverrideAll(readDiags, tfdiags.Warning, func() tfdiags.DiagnosticExtraWrapper {\\n2073: \\t\\t\\treturn &addrs.CheckRuleDiagnosticExtra{\\n2074: \\t\\t\\t\\tCheckRule: addrs.NewCheckRule(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0),\\n2075: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2076: \\t\\t})\\n2077: \\n2078: \\t\\t// If no errors, just remember to report this as a success and continue\\n2079: \\t\\t// as normal.\\n2080: \\t\\tctx.Checks().ReportCheckResult(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0, checks.StatusPass)\\n2081: \\t}\\n2082: \\n2083: \\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\\n2084: \\tif readDiags.HasErrors() {\\n2085: \\t\\treturn nil, keyData, diags\\n2086: \\t}\\n2087: \\n2088: \\tif readDeferred != nil {\\n2089: \\t\\t// Just skip data sources that are being deferred. Nothing, that\\n2090: \\t\\t// references them should be calling them.\\n2091: \\t\\treturn nil, keyData, diags\\n2092: \\t}\\n2093: \\n2094: \\tstate := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\n2095: \\t\\tValue:  newVal,\\n2096: \\t\\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\\n2097: \\t}\\n2098: \\n2099: \\treturn state, keyData, diags\\n2100: }\\n2101: \\n2102: // evalApplyProvisioners determines if provisioners need to be run, and if so\\n2103: // executes the provisioners for a resource and returns an updated error if\\n2104: // provisioning fails.\\n2105: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) evalApplyProvisioners(ctx EvalContext, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, createNew bool, when configs.ProvisionerWhen) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\\n2106: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2107: \\n2108: \\tif state == nil {\\n2109: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] evalApplyProvisioners: %s has no state, so skipping provisioners\\\", n.Addr)\\n2110: \\t\\treturn nil\\n2111: \\t}\\n2112: \\tif when == configs.ProvisionerWhenCreate && !createNew {\\n2113: \\t\\t// If we're not creating a new resource, then don't run provisioners\\n2114: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] evalApplyProvisioners: %s is not freshly-created, so no provisioning is required\\\", n.Addr)\\n2115: \\t\\treturn nil\\n2116: \\t}\\n2117: \\tif state.Status == states.ObjectTainted {\\n2118: \\t\\t// No point in provisioning an object that is already tainted, since\\n2119: \\t\\t// it's going to get recreated on the next apply anyway.\\n2120: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] evalApplyProvisioners: %s is tainted, so skipping provisioning\\\", n.Addr)\\n2121: \\t\\treturn nil\\n2122: \\t}\\n2123: \\n2124: \\tvar allProvs []*configs.Provisioner\\n2125: \\tswitch {\\n2126: \\tcase n.Config != nil && n.Config.Managed != nil:\\n2127: \\t\\tallProvs = n.Config.Managed.Provisioners\\n2128: \\tcase n.RemovedConfig != nil && n.RemovedConfig.Managed != nil:\\n2129: \\t\\tallProvs = n.RemovedConfig.Managed.Provisioners\\n2130: \\t}\\n2131: \\n2132: \\tprovs := filterProvisioners(allProvs, when)\\n2133: \\tif len(provs) == 0 {\\n2134: \\t\\t// We have no provisioners, so don't do anything\\n2135: \\t\\treturn nil\\n2136: \\t}\\n2137: \\n2138: \\t// Call pre hook\\n2139: \\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n2140: \\t\\treturn h.PreProvisionInstance(n.HookResourceIdentity(), state.Value)\\n2141: \\t}))\\n2142: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n2143: \\t\\treturn diags\\n2144: \\t}\\n2145: \\n2146: \\t// If there are no errors, then we append it to our output error\\n2147: \\t// if we have one, otherwise we just output it.\\n2148: \\tdiags = diags.Append(n.applyProvisioners(ctx, state, when, provs))\\n2149: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n2150: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] evalApplyProvisioners: %s provisioning failed, but we will continue anyway at the caller's request\\\", n.Addr)\\n2151: \\t\\treturn diags\\n2152: \\t}\\n2153: \\n2154: \\t// Call post hook\\n2155: \\treturn diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n2156: \\t\\treturn h.PostProvisionInstance(n.HookResourceIdentity(), state.Value)\\n2157: \\t}))\\n2158: }\\n2159: \\n2160: // filterProvisioners filters the provisioners on the resource to only\\n2161: // the provisioners specified by the \\\"when\\\" option.\\n2162: func filterProvisioners(configured []*configs.Provisioner, when configs.ProvisionerWhen) []*configs.Provisioner {\\n2163: \\tif len(configured) == 0 {\\n2164: \\t\\treturn nil\\n2165: \\t}\\n2166: \\n2167: \\tresult := make([]*configs.Provisioner, 0, len(configured))\\n2168: \\tfor _, p := range configured {\\n2169: \\t\\tif p.When == when {\\n2170: \\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, p)\\n2171: \\t\\t}\\n2172: \\t}\\n2173: \\n2174: \\treturn result\\n2175: }\\n2176: \\n2177: // applyProvisioners executes the provisioners for a resource.\\n2178: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) applyProvisioners(ctx EvalContext, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, when configs.ProvisionerWhen, provs []*configs.Provisioner) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\\n2179: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2180: \\n2181: \\t// this self is only used for destroy provisioner evaluation, and must\\n2182: \\t// refer to the last known value of the resource.\\n2183: \\tself := state.Value\\n2184: \\n2185: \\tvar evalScope func(EvalContext, hcl.Body, cty.Value, *configschema.Block) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics)\\n2186: \\tswitch when {\\n2187: \\tcase configs.ProvisionerWhenDestroy:\\n2188: \\t\\tevalScope = n.evalDestroyProvisionerConfig\\n2189: \\tdefault:\\n2190: \\t\\tevalScope = n.evalProvisionerConfig\\n2191: \\t}\\n2192: \\n2193: \\t// If there's a connection block defined directly inside the resource block\\n2194: \\t// then it'll serve as a base connection configuration for all of the\\n2195: \\t// provisioners.\\n2196: \\tvar baseConn hcl.Body\\n2197: \\tswitch {\\n2198: \\tcase n.Config != nil && n.Config.Managed != nil && n.Config.Managed.Connection != nil:\\n2199: \\t\\tbaseConn = n.Config.Managed.Connection.Config\\n2200: \\tcase n.RemovedConfig != nil && n.RemovedConfig.Managed != nil && n.RemovedConfig.Managed.Connection != nil:\\n2201: \\t\\tbaseConn = n.RemovedConfig.Managed.Connection.Config\\n2202: \\t}\\n2203: \\n2204: \\tfor _, prov := range provs {\\n2205: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] applyProvisioners: provisioning %s with %q\\\", n.Addr, prov.Type)\\n2206: \\n2207: \\t\\t// Get the provisioner\\n2208: \\t\\tprovisioner, err := ctx.Provisioner(prov.Type)\\n2209: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n2210: \\t\\t\\treturn diags.Append(err)\\n2211: \\t\\t}\\n2212: \\n2213: \\t\\tschema, err := ctx.ProvisionerSchema(prov.Type)\\n2214: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n2215: \\t\\t\\t// This error probably won't be a great diagnostic, but in practice\\n2216: \\t\\t\\t// we typically catch this problem long before we get here, so\\n2217: \\t\\t\\t// it should be rare to return via this codepath.\\n2218: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\\n2219: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n2220: \\t\\t}\\n2221: \\n2222: \\t\\tconfig, configDiags := evalScope(ctx, prov.Config, self, schema)\\n2223: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\\n2224: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n2225: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n2226: \\t\\t}\\n2227: \\n2228: \\t\\t// If the provisioner block contains a connection block of its own then\\n2229: \\t\\t// it can override the base connection configuration, if any.\\n2230: \\t\\tvar localConn hcl.Body\\n2231: \\t\\tif prov.Connection != nil {\\n2232: \\t\\t\\tlocalConn = prov.Connection.Config\\n2233: \\t\\t}\\n2234: \\n2235: \\t\\tvar connBody hcl.Body\\n2236: \\t\\tswitch {\\n2237: \\t\\tcase baseConn != nil && localConn != nil:\\n2238: \\t\\t\\t// Our standard merging logic applies here, similar to what we do\\n2239: \\t\\t\\t// with _override.tf configuration files: arguments from the\\n2240: \\t\\t\\t// base connection block will be masked by any arguments of the\\n2241: \\t\\t\\t// same name in the local connection block.\\n2242: \\t\\t\\tconnBody = configs.MergeBodies(baseConn, localConn)\\n2243: \\t\\tcase baseConn != nil:\\n2244: \\t\\t\\tconnBody = baseConn\\n2245: \\t\\tcase localConn != nil:\\n2246: \\t\\t\\tconnBody = localConn\\n2247: \\t\\t}\\n2248: \\n2249: \\t\\t// start with an empty connInfo\\n2250: \\t\\tconnInfo := cty.NullVal(connectionBlockSupersetSchema.ImpliedType())\\n2251: \\n2252: \\t\\tif connBody != nil {\\n2253: \\t\\t\\tvar connInfoDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2254: \\t\\t\\tconnInfo, connInfoDiags = evalScope(ctx, connBody, self, connectionBlockSupersetSchema)\\n2255: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(connInfoDiags)\\n2256: \\t\\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n2257: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n2258: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2259: \\t\\t}\\n2260: \\n2261: \\t\\t{\\n2262: \\t\\t\\t// Call pre hook\\n2263: \\t\\t\\terr := ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n2264: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn h.PreProvisionInstanceStep(n.HookResourceIdentity(), prov.Type)\\n2265: \\t\\t\\t})\\n2266: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n2267: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn diags.Append(err)\\n2268: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2269: \\t\\t}\\n2270: \\n2271: \\t\\t// The output function\\n2272: \\t\\toutputFn := func(msg string) {\\n2273: \\t\\t\\tctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n2274: \\t\\t\\t\\th.ProvisionOutput(n.HookResourceIdentity(), prov.Type, msg)\\n2275: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn HookActionContinue, nil\\n2276: \\t\\t\\t})\\n2277: \\t\\t}\\n2278: \\n2279: \\t\\t// If our config or connection info contains any marked values, ensure\\n2280: \\t\\t// those are stripped out before sending to the provisioner. Unlike\\n2281: \\t\\t// resources, we have no need to capture the marked paths and reapply\\n2282: \\t\\t// later.\\n2283: \\t\\tunmarkedConfig, configMarks := config.UnmarkDeep()\\n2284: \\t\\tunmarkedConnInfo, _ := connInfo.UnmarkDeep()\\n2285: \\n2286: \\t\\t// Marks on the config might result in leaking sensitive values through\\n2287: \\t\\t// provisioner logging, so we conservatively suppress all output in\\n2288: \\t\\t// this case. This should not apply to connection info values, which\\n2289: \\t\\t// provisioners ought not to be logging anyway.\\n2290: \\t\\tif len(configMarks) > 0 {\\n2291: \\t\\t\\toutputFn = func(msg string) {\\n2292: \\t\\t\\t\\tctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n2293: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\th.ProvisionOutput(n.HookResourceIdentity(), prov.Type, \\\"(output suppressed due to sensitive value in config)\\\")\\n2294: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn HookActionContinue, nil\\n2295: \\t\\t\\t\\t})\\n2296: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2297: \\t\\t}\\n2298: \\n2299: \\t\\toutput := CallbackUIOutput{OutputFn: outputFn}\\n2300: \\t\\tresp := provisioner.ProvisionResource(provisioners.ProvisionResourceRequest{\\n2301: \\t\\t\\tConfig:     unmarkedConfig,\\n2302: \\t\\t\\tConnection: unmarkedConnInfo,\\n2303: \\t\\t\\tUIOutput:   &output,\\n2304: \\t\\t})\\n2305: \\t\\tapplyDiags := resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(prov.Config, n.Addr.String())\\n2306: \\n2307: \\t\\t// Call post hook\\n2308: \\t\\thookErr := ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\\n2309: \\t\\t\\treturn h.PostProvisionInstanceStep(n.HookResourceIdentity(), prov.Type, applyDiags.Err())\\n2310: \\t\\t})\\n2311: \\n2312: \\t\\tswitch prov.OnFailure {\\n2313: \\t\\tcase configs.ProvisionerOnFailureContinue:\\n2314: \\t\\t\\tif applyDiags.HasErrors() {\\n2315: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[WARN] Errors while provisioning %s with %q, but continuing as requested in configuration\\\", n.Addr, prov.Type)\\n2316: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n2317: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Maybe there are warnings that we still want to see\\n2318: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(applyDiags)\\n2319: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2320: \\t\\tdefault:\\n2321: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(applyDiags)\\n2322: \\t\\t\\tif applyDiags.HasErrors() {\\n2323: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[WARN] Errors while provisioning %s with %q, so aborting\\\", n.Addr, prov.Type)\\n2324: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n2325: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2326: \\t\\t}\\n2327: \\n2328: \\t\\t// Deal with the hook\\n2329: \\t\\tif hookErr != nil {\\n2330: \\t\\t\\treturn diags.Append(hookErr)\\n2331: \\t\\t}\\n2332: \\t}\\n2333: \\n2334: \\treturn diags\\n2335: }\\n2336: \\n2337: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) evalProvisionerConfig(ctx EvalContext, body hcl.Body, self cty.Value, schema *configschema.Block) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n2338: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2339: \\n2340: \\tforEach, _, forEachDiags := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, false)\\n2341: \\tdiags = diags.Append(forEachDiags)\\n2342: \\n2343: \\tkeyData := EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, forEach)\\n2344: \\n2345: \\tconfig, _, configDiags := ctx.EvaluateBlock(body, schema, n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource, keyData)\\n2346: \\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\\n2347: \\n2348: \\treturn config, diags\\n2349: }\\n2350: \\n2351: // during destroy a provisioner can only evaluate within the scope of the parent resource\\n2352: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) evalDestroyProvisionerConfig(ctx EvalContext, body hcl.Body, self cty.Value, schema *configschema.Block) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n2353: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2354: \\n2355: \\t// For a destroy-time provisioner forEach is intentionally nil here,\\n2356: \\t// which EvalDataForInstanceKey responds to by not populating EachValue\\n2357: \\t// in its result. That's okay because each.value is prohibited for\\n2358: \\t// destroy-time provisioners.\\n2359: \\tkeyData := EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, nil)\\n2360: \\n2361: \\tevalScope := ctx.EvaluationScope(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource, nil, keyData)\\n2362: \\tconfig, evalDiags := evalScope.EvalSelfBlock(body, self, schema, keyData)\\n2363: \\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\\n2364: \\n2365: \\treturn config, diags\\n2366: }\\n2367: \\n2368: // apply accepts an applyConfig, instead of using n.Config, so destroy plans can\\n2369: // send a nil config. The keyData information can be empty if the config is\\n2370: // nil, since it is only used to evaluate the configuration.\\n2371: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) apply(\\n2372: \\tctx EvalContext,\\n2373: \\tstate *states.ResourceInstanceObject,\\n2374: \\tchange *plans.ResourceInstanceChange,\\n2375: \\tapplyConfig *configs.Resource,\\n2376: \\tkeyData instances.RepetitionData,\\n2377: \\tcreateBeforeDestroy bool) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n2378: \\n2379: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2380: \\tif state == nil {\\n2381: \\t\\tstate = &states.ResourceInstanceObject{}\\n2382: \\t}\\n2383: \\n2384: \\tif change.Action == plans.NoOp {\\n2385: \\t\\t// If this is a no-op change then we don't want to actually change\\n2386: \\t\\t// anything, so we'll just echo back the state we were given and\\n2387: \\t\\t// let our internal checks and updates proceed.\\n2388: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] NodeAbstractResourceInstance.apply: skipping %s because it has no planned action\\\", n.Addr)\\n2389: \\t\\treturn state, diags\\n2390: \\t}\\n2391: \\n2392: \\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\\n2393: \\tif err != nil {\\n2394: \\t\\treturn nil, diags.Append(err)\\n2395: \\t}\\n2396: \\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceType(n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode, n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type)\\n2397: \\tif schema == nil {\\n2398: \\t\\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\\n2399: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"provider does not support resource type %q\\\", n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type))\\n2400: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n2401: \", \"url\": \"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go%3FL2000-2400\"}, \"isError\": false}"
