new value even when errors\\\\n2517: \\\\t\\\\t// occur, but sometimes they don't and so in that case we'll patch that up\\\\n2518: \\\\t\\\\t// by just using the prior state, so we'll at least keep track of the\\\\n2519: \\\\t\\\\t// object for the user to retry.\\\\n2520: \\\\t\\\\tnewVal = change.Before\\\\n2521: \\\\n2522: \\\\t\\\\t// As a special case, we'll set the new value to null if it looks like\\\\n2523: \\\\t\\\\t// we were trying to execute a delete, because the provider in this case\\\\n2524: \\\\t\\\\t// probably left the newVal unset intending it to be interpreted as \\\\\\\"null\\\\\\\".\\\\n2525: \\\\t\\\\tif change.After.IsNull() {\\\\n2526: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tnewVal = cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType())\\\\n2527: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2528: \\\\n2529: \\\\t\\\\tif !diags.HasErrors() {\\\\n2530: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\\\n2531: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\ttfdiags.Error,\\\\n2532: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"Provider produced invalid object\\\\\\\",\\\\n2533: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\\n2534: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"Provider %q produced an invalid nil value after apply for %s.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\\\\\",\\\\n2535: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tn.ResolvedProvider.String(), n.Addr.String(),\\\\n2536: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t),\\\\n2537: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t))\\\\n2538: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2539: \\\\t}\\\\n2540: \\\\n2541: \\\\tvar conformDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\\\n2542: \\\\tfor _, err := range newVal.Type().TestConformance(schema.ImpliedType()) {\\\\n2543: \\\\t\\\\tconformDiags = conformDiags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\\\n2544: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\ttfdiags.Error,\\\\n2545: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"Provider produced invalid object\\\\\\\",\\\\n2546: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\\n2547: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"Provider %q produced an invalid value after apply for %s. The result cannot not be saved in the Terraform state.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\\\\\",\\\\n2548: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tn.ResolvedProvider.String(), tfdiags.FormatErrorPrefixed(err, n.Addr.String()),\\\\n2549: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t),\\\\n2550: \\\\t\\\\t))\\\\n2551: \\\\t}\\\\n2552: \\\\tdiags = diags.Append(conformDiags)\\\\n2553: \\\\tif conformDiags.HasErrors() {\\\\n2554: \\\\t\\\\t// Bail early in this particular case, because an object that doesn't\\\\n2555: \\\\t\\\\t// conform to the schema can't be saved in the state anyway -- the\\\\n2556: \\\\t\\\\t// serializer will reject it.\\\\n2557: \\\\t\\\\treturn nil, diags\\\\n2558: \\\\t}\\\\n2559: \\\\n2560: \\\\t// After this point we have a type-conforming result object and so we\\\\n2561: \\\\t// must always run to completion to ensure it can be saved. If n.Error\\\\n2562: \\\\t// is set then we must not return a non-nil error, in order to allow\\\\n2563: \\\\t// evaluation to continue to a later point where our state object will\\\\n2564: \\\\t// be saved.\\\\n2565: \\\\n2566: \\\\t// By this point there must not be any unknown values remaining in our\\\\n2567: \\\\t// object, because we've applied the change and we can't save unknowns\\\\n2568: \\\\t// in our persistent state. If any are present then we will indicate an\\\\n2569: \\\\t// error (which is always a bug in the provider) but we will also replace\\\\n2570: \\\\t// them with nulls so that we can successfully save the portions of the\\\\n2571: \\\\t// returned value that are known.\\\\n2572: \\\\tif !newVal.IsWhollyKnown() {\\\\n2573: \\\\t\\\\t// To generate better error messages, we'll go for a walk through the\\\\n2574: \\\\t\\\\t// value and make a separate diagnostic for each unknown value we\\\\n2575: \\\\t\\\\t// find.\\\\n2576: \\\\t\\\\tcty.Walk(newVal, func(path cty.Path, val cty.Value) (bool, error) {\\\\n2577: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif !val.IsKnown() {\\\\n2578: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tpathStr := tfdiags.FormatCtyPath(path)\\\\n2579: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\\\n2580: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\ttfdiags.Error,\\\\n2581: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"Provider returned invalid result object after apply\\\\\\\",\\\\n2582: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\\n2583: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"After the apply operation, the provider still indicated an unknown value for %s%s. All values must be known after apply, so this is always a bug in the provider and should be reported in the provider's own repository. Terraform will still save the other known object values in the state.\\\\\\\",\\\\n2584: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tn.Addr, pathStr,\\\\n2585: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t),\\\\n2586: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t))\\\\n2587: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2588: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn true, nil\\\\n2589: \\\\t\\\\t})\\\\n2590: \\\\n2591: \\\\t\\\\t// NOTE: This operation can potentially be lossy if there are multiple\\\\n2592: \\\\t\\\\t// elements in a set that differ only by unknown values: after\\\\n2593: \\\\t\\\\t// replacing with null these will be merged together into a single set\\\\n2594: \\\\t\\\\t// element. Since we can only get here in the presence of a provider\\\\n2595: \\\\t\\\\t// bug, we accept this because storing a result here is always a\\\\n2596: \\\\t\\\\t// best-effort sort of thing.\\\\n2597: \\\\t\\\\tnewVal = cty.UnknownAsNull(newVal)\\\\n2598: \\\\t}\\\\n2599: \\\\n2600: \\\\t// If we have paths to mark, mark those on this new value we need to\\\\n2601: \\\\t// re-check the value against the schema, because nested computed values\\\\n2602: \\\\t// won't be included in afterPaths, which are only what was read from the\\\\n2603: \\\\t// After plan value.\\\\n2604: \\\\tnewVal = newVal.MarkWithPaths(afterPaths)\\\\n2605: \\\\tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(newVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\\\\n2606: \\\\t\\\\tnewVal = marks.MarkPaths(newVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\\\\n2607: \\\\t}\\\\n2608: \\\\n2609: \\\\tif change.Action != plans.Delete \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 !diags.HasErrors() {\\\\n2610: \\\\t\\\\t// Only values that were marked as unknown in the planned value are allowed\\\\n2611: \\\\t\\\\t// to change during the apply operation. (We do this after the unknown-ness\\\\n2612: \\\\t\\\\t// check above so that we also catch anything that became unknown after\\\\n2613: \\\\t\\\\t// being known during plan.)\\\\n2614: \\\\t\\\\t//\\\\n2615: \\\\t\\\\t// If we are returning other errors anyway then we'll give this\\\\n2616: \\\\t\\\\t// a pass since the other errors are usually the explanation for\\\\n2617: \\\\t\\\\t// this one and so it's more helpful to let the user focus on the\\\\n2618: \\\\t\\\\t// root cause rather than distract with this extra problem.\\\\n2619: \\\\t\\\\tif errs := objchange.AssertObjectCompatible(schema, change.After, newVal); len(errs) \\\\u003e 0 {\\\\n2620: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif resp.LegacyTypeSystem {\\\\n2621: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// The shimming of the old type system in the legacy SDK is not precise\\\\n2622: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// enough to pass this consistency check, so we'll give it a pass here,\\\\n2623: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// but we will generate a warning about it so that we are more likely\\\\n2624: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// to notice in the logs if an inconsistency beyond the type system\\\\n2625: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// leads to a downstream provider failure.\\\\n2626: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tvar buf strings.Builder\\\\n2627: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tfmt.Fprintf(\\\\u0026buf, \\\\\\\"[WARN] Provider %q produced an unexpected new value for %s, but we are tolerating it because it is using the legacy plugin SDK.\\\\\\\\n    The following problems may be the cause of any confusing errors from downstream operations:\\\\\\\", n.ResolvedProvider.String(), n.Addr)\\\\n2628: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tfor _, err := range errs {\\\\n2629: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tfmt.Fprintf(\\\\u0026buf, \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n      - %s\\\\\\\", tfdiags.FormatError(err))\\\\n2630: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2631: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tlog.Print(buf.String())\\\\n2632: \\\\n2633: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// The sort of inconsistency we won't catch here is if a known value\\\\n2634: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// in the plan is changed during apply. That can cause downstream\\\\n2635: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// problems because a dependent resource would make its own plan based\\\\n2636: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// on the planned value, and thus get a different result during the\\\\n2637: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// apply phase. This will usually lead to a \\\\\\\"Provider produced invalid plan\\\\\\\"\\\\n2638: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// error that incorrectly blames the downstream resource for the change.\\\\n2639: \\\\n2640: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t} else {\\\\n2641: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tfor _, err := range errs {\\\\n2642: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\\\n2643: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\ttfdiags.Error,\\\\n2644: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"Provider produced inconsistent result after apply\\\\\\\",\\\\n2645: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\\n2646: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"When applying changes to %s, provider %q produced an unexpected new value: %s.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\\\\\",\\\\n2647: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tn.Addr, n.ResolvedProvider.String(), tfdiags.FormatError(err),\\\\n2648: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t),\\\\n2649: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t))\\\\n2650: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2651: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2652: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2653: \\\\t}\\\\n2654: \\\\n2655: \\\\t// If a provider returns a null or non-null object at the wrong time then\\\\n2656: \\\\t// we still want to save that but it often causes some confusing behaviors\\\\n2657: \\\\t// where it seems like Terraform is failing to take any action at all,\\\\n2658: \\\\t// so we'll generate some errors to draw attention to it.\\\\n2659: \\\\tif !diags.HasErrors() {\\\\n2660: \\\\t\\\\tif change.Action == plans.Delete \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 !newVal.IsNull() {\\\\n2661: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\\\n2662: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\ttfdiags.Error,\\\\n2663: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"Provider returned invalid result object after apply\\\\\\\",\\\\n2664: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\\n2665: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"After applying a %s plan, the provider returned a non-null object for %s. Destroying should always produce a null value, so this is always a bug in the provider and should be reported in the provider's own repository. Terraform will still save this errant object in the state for debugging and recovery.\\\\\\\",\\\\n2666: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tchange.Action, n.Addr,\\\\n2667: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t),\\\\n2668: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t))\\\\n2669: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2670: \\\\t\\\\tif change.Action != plans.Delete \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 newVal.IsNull() {\\\\n2671: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\\\n2672: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\ttfdiags.Error,\\\\n2673: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"Provider returned invalid result object after apply\\\\\\\",\\\\n2674: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\\n2675: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"After applying a %s plan, the provider returned a null object for %s. Only destroying should always produce a null value, so this is always a bug in the provider and should be reported in the provider's own repository.\\\\\\\",\\\\n2676: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tchange.Action, n.Addr,\\\\n2677: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t),\\\\n2678: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t))\\\\n2679: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2680: \\\\t}\\\\n2681: \\\\n2682: \\\\tswitch {\\\\n2683: \\\\tcase diags.HasErrors() \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 newVal.IsNull():\\\\n2684: \\\\t\\\\t// Sometimes providers return a null value when an operation fails for\\\\n2685: \\\\t\\\\t// some reason, but we'd rather keep the prior state so that the error\\\\n2686: \\\\t\\\\t// can be corrected on a subsequent run. We must only do this for null\\\\n2687: \\\\t\\\\t// new value though, or else we may discard partial updates the\\\\n2688: \\\\t\\\\t// provider was able to complete. Otherwise, we'll continue using the\\\\n2689: \\\\t\\\\t// prior state as the new value, making this effectively a no-op.  If\\\\n2690: \\\\t\\\\t// the item really _has_ been deleted then our next refresh will detect\\\\n2691: \\\\t\\\\t// that and fix it up.\\\\n2692: \\\\t\\\\treturn state.DeepCopy(), diags\\\\n2693: \\\\n2694: \\\\tcase diags.HasErrors() \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 !newVal.IsNull():\\\\n2695: \\\\t\\\\t// if we have an error, make sure we restore the object status in the new state\\\\n2696: \\\\t\\\\tnewState := \\\\u0026states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\\\n2697: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tStatus:              state.Status,\\\\n2698: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tValue:               newVal,\\\\n2699: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tPrivate:             resp.Private,\\\\n2700: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tCreateBeforeDestroy: createBeforeDestroy,\\\\n2701: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2702: \\\\n2703: \\\\t\\\\t// if the resource was being deleted, the dependencies are not going to\\\\n2704: \\\\t\\\\t// be recalculated and we need to restore those as well.\\\\n2705: \\\\t\\\\tif change.Action == plans.Delete {\\\\n2706: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tnewState.Dependencies = state.Dependencies\\\\n2707: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2708: \\\\n2709: \\\\t\\\\treturn newState, diags\\\\n2710: \\\\n2711: \\\\tcase !newVal.IsNull():\\\\n2712: \\\\t\\\\t// Non error case with a new state\\\\n2713: \\\\t\\\\tnewState := \\\\u0026states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\\\n2714: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tStatus:              states.ObjectReady,\\\\n2715: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tValue:               newVal,\\\\n2716: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tPrivate:             resp.Private,\\\\n2717: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tCreateBeforeDestroy: createBeforeDestroy,\\\\n2718: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2719: \\\\t\\\\treturn newState, diags\\\\n2720: \\\\n2721: \\\\tdefault:\\\\n2722: \\\\t\\\\t// Non error case, were the object was deleted\\\\n2723: \\\\t\\\\treturn nil, diags\\\\n2724: \\\\t}\\\\n2725: }\\\\n2726: \\\\n2727: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) prevRunAddr(ctx EvalContext) addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\\\\n2728: \\\\treturn resourceInstancePrevRunAddr(ctx, n.Addr)\\\\n2729: }\\\\n2730: \\\\n2731: func resourceInstancePrevRunAddr(ctx EvalContext, currentAddr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\\\\n2732: \\\\ttable := ctx.MoveResults()\\\\n2733: \\\\treturn table.OldAddr(currentAddr)\\\\n2734: }\\\\n2735: \\\\n2736: func getAction(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, priorVal, plannedNewVal cty.Value, createBeforeDestroy bool, forceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance, reqRep cty.PathSet) (action plans.Action, actionReason plans.ResourceInstanceChangeActionReason) {\\\\n2737: \\\\t// The user might also ask us to force replacing a particular resource\\\\n2738: \\\\t// instance, regardless of whether the provider thinks it needs replacing.\\\\n2739: \\\\t// For example, users typically do this if they learn a particular object\\\\n2740: \\\\t// has become degraded in an immutable infrastructure scenario and so\\\\n2741: \\\\t// replacing it with a new object is a viable repair path.\\\\n2742: \\\\tmatchedForceReplace := false\\\\n2743: \\\\tfor _, candidateAddr := range forceReplace {\\\\n2744: \\\\t\\\\tif candidateAddr.Equal(addr) {\\\\n2745: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tmatchedForceReplace = true\\\\n2746: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tbreak\\\\n2747: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2748: \\\\n2749: \\\\t\\\\t// For \\\\\\\"force replace\\\\\\\" purposes we require an exact resource instance\\\\n2750: \\\\t\\\\t// address to match. If a user forgets to include the instance key\\\\n2751: \\\\t\\\\t// for a multi-instance resource then it won't match here, but we\\\\n2752: \\\\t\\\\t// have an earlier check in NodePlannableResource.Execute that should\\\\n2753: \\\\t\\\\t// prevent us from getting here in that case.\\\\n2754: \\\\t}\\\\n2755: \\\\n2756: \\\\t// Unmark for this test for value equality.\\\\n2757: \\\\teqV := plannedNewVal.Equals(priorVal)\\\\n2758: \\\\teq := eqV.IsKnown() \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 eqV.True()\\\\n2759: \\\\n2760: \\\\tswitch {\\\\n2761: \\\\tcase priorVal.IsNull():\\\\n2762: \\\\t\\\\taction = plans.Create\\\\n2763: \\\\tcase eq \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 !matchedForceReplace:\\\\n2764: \\\\t\\\\taction = plans.NoOp\\\\n2765: \\\\tcase matchedForceReplace || !reqRep.Empty():\\\\n2766: \\\\t\\\\t// If the user \\\\\\\"forced replace\\\\\\\" of this instance of if there are any\\\\n2767: \\\\t\\\\t// \\\\\\\"requires replace\\\\\\\" paths left _after our filtering above_ then this\\\\n2768: \\\\t\\\\t// is a replace action.\\\\n2769: \\\\t\\\\tif createBeforeDestroy {\\\\n2770: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\taction = plans.CreateThenDelete\\\\n2771: \\\\t\\\\t} else {\\\\n2772: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\taction = plans.DeleteThenCreate\\\\n2773: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2774: \\\\t\\\\tswitch {\\\\n2775: \\\\t\\\\tcase matchedForceReplace:\\\\n2776: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tactionReason = plans.ResourceInstanceReplaceByRequest\\\\n2777: \\\\t\\\\tcase !reqRep.Empty():\\\\n2778: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tactionReason = plans.ResourceInstanceReplaceBecauseCannotUpdate\\\\n2779: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2780: \\\\tdefault:\\\\n2781: \\\\t\\\\taction = plans.Update\\\\n2782: \\\\t\\\\t// \\\\\\\"Delete\\\\\\\" is never chosen here, because deletion plans are always\\\\n2783: \\\\t\\\\t// created more directly elsewhere, such as in \\\\\\\"orphan\\\\\\\" handling.\\\\n2784: \\\\t}\\\\n2785: \\\\n2786: \\\\treturn\\\\n2787: }\\\\n2788: \\\\n2789: // getRequiredReplaces returns a list of paths to attributes whose changes mean\\\\n2790: // that we must replace rather than update an existing remote object.\\\\n2791: //\\\\n2792: // The provider produces a list of paths to attributes whose changes mean\\\\n2793: // that we must replace rather than update an existing remote object.\\\\n2794: // However, we only need to do that if the identified attributes _have_\\\\n2795: // actually changed -- particularly after we may have undone some of the\\\\n2796: // changes in processIgnoreChanges -- so now we'll filter that list to\\\\n2797: // include only where changes are detected.\\\\n2798: //\\\\n2799: // Both the priorVal and plannedNewVal should be unmarked before calling this\\\\n2800: // function. This function exposes nothing about the priorVal or plannedVal\\\\n2801: // except for the paths that require replacement which can be deduced from the\\\\n2802: // type with or without marks.\\\\n2803: func getRequiredReplaces(priorVal, plannedNewVal cty.Value, requiredReplaces []cty.Path, providerAddr tfaddr.Provider, addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) (cty.PathSet, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\\\n2804: \\\\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\\\n2805: \\\\n2806: \\\\treqRep := cty.NewPathSet()\\\\n2807: \\\\tif len(requiredReplaces) \\\\u003e 0 {\\\\n2808: \\\\t\\\\tfor _, path := range requiredReplaces {\\\\n2809: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif priorVal.IsNull() {\\\\n2810: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// If prior is null then we don't expect any RequiresReplace at all,\\\\n2811: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// because this is a Create action.\\\\n2812: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tcontinue\\\\n2813: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2814: \\\\n2815: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tpriorChangedVal, priorPathDiags := hcl.ApplyPath(priorVal, path, nil)\\\\n2816: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tplannedChangedVal, plannedPathDiags := hcl.ApplyPath(plannedNewVal, path, nil)\\\\n2817: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif plannedPathDiags.HasErrors() \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 priorPathDiags.HasErrors() {\\\\n2818: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// This means the path was invalid in both the prior and new\\\\n2819: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// values, which is an error with the provider itself.\\\\n2820: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\\\n2821: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\ttfdiags.Error,\\\\n2822: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"Provider produced invalid plan\\\\\\\",\\\\n2823: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\\n2824: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\\\\"Provider %q has indicated \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"requires replacement\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" on %s for a non-existent attribute path %#v.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\\\\\",\\\\n2825: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tproviderAddr, addr, path,\\\\n2826: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t),\\\\n2827: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t))\\\\n2828: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tcontinue\\\\n2829: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2830: \\\\n2831: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// Make sure we have valid Values for both values.\\\\n2832: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// Note: if the opposing value was of the type\\\\n2833: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// cty.DynamicPseudoType, the type assigned here may not exactly\\\\n2834: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// match the schema. This is fine here, since we're only going to\\\\n2835: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// check for equality, but if the NullVal is to be used, we need to\\\\n2836: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// check the schema for th true type.\\\\n2837: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tswitch {\\\\n2838: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tcase priorChangedVal == cty.NilVal \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 plannedChangedVal == cty.NilVal:\\\\n2839: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// this should never happen without ApplyPath errors above\\\\n2840: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tpanic(\\\\\\\"requires replace path returned 2 nil values\\\\\\\")\\\\n2841: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tcase priorChangedVal == cty.NilVal:\\\\n2842: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tpriorChangedVal = cty.NullVal(plannedChangedVal.Type())\\\\n2843: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tcase plannedChangedVal == cty.NilVal:\\\\n2844: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tplannedChangedVal = cty.NullVal(priorChangedVal.Type())\\\\n2845: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2846: \\\\n2847: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\teqV := plannedChangedVal.Equals(priorChangedVal)\\\\n2848: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif !eqV.IsKnown() || eqV.False() {\\\\n2849: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\treqRep.Add(path)\\\\n2850: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2851: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2852: \\\\t\\\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\\\n2853: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn reqRep, diags\\\\n2854: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n2855: \\\\t}\\\\n2856: \\\\n2857: \\\\treturn reqRep, diags\\\\n2858: }\\\\n2859: \\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go%3FL2400-2859\\\"}\", \"annotations\": null, \"meta\": null}], \"structuredContent\": {\"content\": \"2400: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n2401: \\t}\\n2402: \\n2403: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[INFO] Starting apply for %s\\\", n.Addr)\\n2404: \\n2405: \\tconfigVal := cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType)\\n2406: \\tif applyConfig != nil {\\n2407: \\t\\tvar configDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2408: \\t\\tconfigVal, _, configDiags = ctx.EvaluateBlock(applyConfig.Config, schema, nil, keyData)\\n2409: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\\n2410: \\t\\tif configDiags.HasErrors() {\\n2411: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n2412: \\t\\t}\\n2413: \\t}\\n2414: \\n2415: \\tif !configVal.IsWhollyKnown() {\\n2416: \\t\\t// We don't have a pretty format function for a path, but since this is\\n2417: \\t\\t// such a rare error, we can just drop the raw GoString values in here\\n2418: \\t\\t// to make sure we have something to debug with.\\n2419: \\t\\tvar unknownPaths []string\\n2420: \\t\\tcty.Transform(configVal, func(p cty.Path, v cty.Value) (cty.Value, error) {\\n2421: \\t\\t\\tif !v.IsKnown() {\\n2422: \\t\\t\\t\\tunknownPaths = append(unknownPaths, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%#v\\\", p))\\n2423: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2424: \\t\\t\\treturn v, nil\\n2425: \\t\\t})\\n2426: \\n2427: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n2428: \\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n2429: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Configuration contains unknown value\\\",\\n2430: \\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"configuration for %s still contains unknown values during apply (this is a bug in Terraform; please report it!)\\\\n\\\"+\\n2431: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"The following paths in the resource configuration are unknown:\\\\n%s\\\",\\n2432: \\t\\t\\t\\tn.Addr,\\n2433: \\t\\t\\t\\tstrings.Join(unknownPaths, \\\"\\\\n\\\"),\\n2434: \\t\\t\\t),\\n2435: \\t\\t))\\n2436: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n2437: \\t}\\n2438: \\n2439: \\tmetaConfigVal, metaDiags := n.providerMetas(ctx)\\n2440: \\tdiags = diags.Append(metaDiags)\\n2441: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n2442: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n2443: \\t}\\n2444: \\n2445: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[DEBUG] %s: applying the planned %s change\\\", n.Addr, change.Action)\\n2446: \\n2447: \\t// If our config, Before or After value contain any marked values,\\n2448: \\t// ensure those are stripped out before sending\\n2449: \\t// this to the provider\\n2450: \\tunmarkedConfigVal, _ := configVal.UnmarkDeep()\\n2451: \\tunmarkedBefore, beforePaths := change.Before.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\\n2452: \\tunmarkedAfter, afterPaths := change.After.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\\n2453: \\n2454: \\t// If we have an Update action, our before and after values are equal,\\n2455: \\t// and only differ on their sensitivity, the newVal is the after val\\n2456: \\t// and we should not communicate with the provider. We do need to update\\n2457: \\t// the state with this new value, to ensure the sensitivity change is\\n2458: \\t// persisted.\\n2459: \\teqV := unmarkedBefore.Equals(unmarkedAfter)\\n2460: \\teq := eqV.IsKnown() && eqV.True()\\n2461: \\tif change.Action == plans.Update && eq && !marks.MarksEqual(beforePaths, afterPaths) {\\n2462: \\t\\t// Copy the previous state, changing only the value\\n2463: \\t\\tnewState := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\n2464: \\t\\t\\tCreateBeforeDestroy: state.CreateBeforeDestroy,\\n2465: \\t\\t\\tDependencies:        state.Dependencies,\\n2466: \\t\\t\\tPrivate:             state.Private,\\n2467: \\t\\t\\tStatus:              state.Status,\\n2468: \\t\\t\\tValue:               change.After,\\n2469: \\t\\t}\\n2470: \\t\\treturn newState, diags\\n2471: \\t}\\n2472: \\n2473: \\tvar resp providers.ApplyResourceChangeResponse\\n2474: \\tif n.override != nil {\\n2475: \\t\\t// As with the planning stage, we only need to worry about computed\\n2476: \\t\\t// values the first time the object is created. Otherwise, we're happy\\n2477: \\t\\t// to just apply whatever the user asked for.\\n2478: \\t\\tif change.Action == plans.Create {\\n2479: \\t\\t\\toverride, overrideDiags := mocking.ApplyComputedValuesForResource(unmarkedAfter, mocking.MockedData{\\n2480: \\t\\t\\t\\tValue: n.override.Values,\\n2481: \\t\\t\\t\\tRange: n.override.ValuesRange,\\n2482: \\t\\t\\t}, schema)\\n2483: \\t\\t\\tresp = providers.ApplyResourceChangeResponse{\\n2484: \\t\\t\\t\\tNewState:    override,\\n2485: \\t\\t\\t\\tDiagnostics: overrideDiags,\\n2486: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2487: \\t\\t} else {\\n2488: \\t\\t\\tresp = providers.ApplyResourceChangeResponse{\\n2489: \\t\\t\\t\\tNewState: unmarkedAfter,\\n2490: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2491: \\t\\t}\\n2492: \\t} else {\\n2493: \\t\\tresp = provider.ApplyResourceChange(providers.ApplyResourceChangeRequest{\\n2494: \\t\\t\\tTypeName:       n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\\n2495: \\t\\t\\tPriorState:     unmarkedBefore,\\n2496: \\t\\t\\tConfig:         unmarkedConfigVal,\\n2497: \\t\\t\\tPlannedState:   unmarkedAfter,\\n2498: \\t\\t\\tPlannedPrivate: change.Private,\\n2499: \\t\\t\\tProviderMeta:   metaConfigVal,\\n2500: \\t\\t})\\n2501: \\t}\\n2502: \\tapplyDiags := resp.Diagnostics\\n2503: \\tif applyConfig != nil {\\n2504: \\t\\tapplyDiags = applyDiags.InConfigBody(applyConfig.Config, n.Addr.String())\\n2505: \\t}\\n2506: \\tdiags = diags.Append(applyDiags)\\n2507: \\n2508: \\t// Even if there are errors in the returned diagnostics, the provider may\\n2509: \\t// have returned a _partial_ state for an object that already exists but\\n2510: \\t// failed to fully configure, and so the remaining code must always run\\n2511: \\t// to completion but must be defensive against the new value being\\n2512: \\t// incomplete.\\n2513: \\tnewVal := resp.NewState\\n2514: \\n2515: \\tif newVal == cty.NilVal {\\n2516: \\t\\t// Providers are supposed to return a partial new value even when errors\\n2517: \\t\\t// occur, but sometimes they don't and so in that case we'll patch that up\\n2518: \\t\\t// by just using the prior state, so we'll at least keep track of the\\n2519: \\t\\t// object for the user to retry.\\n2520: \\t\\tnewVal = change.Before\\n2521: \\n2522: \\t\\t// As a special case, we'll set the new value to null if it looks like\\n2523: \\t\\t// we were trying to execute a delete, because the provider in this case\\n2524: \\t\\t// probably left the newVal unset intending it to be interpreted as \\\"null\\\".\\n2525: \\t\\tif change.After.IsNull() {\\n2526: \\t\\t\\tnewVal = cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType())\\n2527: \\t\\t}\\n2528: \\n2529: \\t\\tif !diags.HasErrors() {\\n2530: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n2531: \\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n2532: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider produced invalid object\\\",\\n2533: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n2534: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider %q produced an invalid nil value after apply for %s.\\\\n\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\",\\n2535: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tn.ResolvedProvider.String(), n.Addr.String(),\\n2536: \\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n2537: \\t\\t\\t))\\n2538: \\t\\t}\\n2539: \\t}\\n2540: \\n2541: \\tvar conformDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2542: \\tfor _, err := range newVal.Type().TestConformance(schema.ImpliedType()) {\\n2543: \\t\\tconformDiags = conformDiags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n2544: \\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n2545: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider produced invalid object\\\",\\n2546: \\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n2547: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider %q produced an invalid value after apply for %s. The result cannot not be saved in the Terraform state.\\\\n\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\",\\n2548: \\t\\t\\t\\tn.ResolvedProvider.String(), tfdiags.FormatErrorPrefixed(err, n.Addr.String()),\\n2549: \\t\\t\\t),\\n2550: \\t\\t))\\n2551: \\t}\\n2552: \\tdiags = diags.Append(conformDiags)\\n2553: \\tif conformDiags.HasErrors() {\\n2554: \\t\\t// Bail early in this particular case, because an object that doesn't\\n2555: \\t\\t// conform to the schema can't be saved in the state anyway -- the\\n2556: \\t\\t// serializer will reject it.\\n2557: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n2558: \\t}\\n2559: \\n2560: \\t// After this point we have a type-conforming result object and so we\\n2561: \\t// must always run to completion to ensure it can be saved. If n.Error\\n2562: \\t// is set then we must not return a non-nil error, in order to allow\\n2563: \\t// evaluation to continue to a later point where our state object will\\n2564: \\t// be saved.\\n2565: \\n2566: \\t// By this point there must not be any unknown values remaining in our\\n2567: \\t// object, because we've applied the change and we can't save unknowns\\n2568: \\t// in our persistent state. If any are present then we will indicate an\\n2569: \\t// error (which is always a bug in the provider) but we will also replace\\n2570: \\t// them with nulls so that we can successfully save the portions of the\\n2571: \\t// returned value that are known.\\n2572: \\tif !newVal.IsWhollyKnown() {\\n2573: \\t\\t// To generate better error messages, we'll go for a walk through the\\n2574: \\t\\t// value and make a separate diagnostic for each unknown value we\\n2575: \\t\\t// find.\\n2576: \\t\\tcty.Walk(newVal, func(path cty.Path, val cty.Value) (bool, error) {\\n2577: \\t\\t\\tif !val.IsKnown() {\\n2578: \\t\\t\\t\\tpathStr := tfdiags.FormatCtyPath(path)\\n2579: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n2580: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n2581: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider returned invalid result object after apply\\\",\\n2582: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n2583: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"After the apply operation, the provider still indicated an unknown value for %s%s. All values must be known after apply, so this is always a bug in the provider and should be reported in the provider's own repository. Terraform will still save the other known object values in the state.\\\",\\n2584: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tn.Addr, pathStr,\\n2585: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n2586: \\t\\t\\t\\t))\\n2587: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2588: \\t\\t\\treturn true, nil\\n2589: \\t\\t})\\n2590: \\n2591: \\t\\t// NOTE: This operation can potentially be lossy if there are multiple\\n2592: \\t\\t// elements in a set that differ only by unknown values: after\\n2593: \\t\\t// replacing with null these will be merged together into a single set\\n2594: \\t\\t// element. Since we can only get here in the presence of a provider\\n2595: \\t\\t// bug, we accept this because storing a result here is always a\\n2596: \\t\\t// best-effort sort of thing.\\n2597: \\t\\tnewVal = cty.UnknownAsNull(newVal)\\n2598: \\t}\\n2599: \\n2600: \\t// If we have paths to mark, mark those on this new value we need to\\n2601: \\t// re-check the value against the schema, because nested computed values\\n2602: \\t// won't be included in afterPaths, which are only what was read from the\\n2603: \\t// After plan value.\\n2604: \\tnewVal = newVal.MarkWithPaths(afterPaths)\\n2605: \\tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(newVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\\n2606: \\t\\tnewVal = marks.MarkPaths(newVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\\n2607: \\t}\\n2608: \\n2609: \\tif change.Action != plans.Delete && !diags.HasErrors() {\\n2610: \\t\\t// Only values that were marked as unknown in the planned value are allowed\\n2611: \\t\\t// to change during the apply operation. (We do this after the unknown-ness\\n2612: \\t\\t// check above so that we also catch anything that became unknown after\\n2613: \\t\\t// being known during plan.)\\n2614: \\t\\t//\\n2615: \\t\\t// If we are returning other errors anyway then we'll give this\\n2616: \\t\\t// a pass since the other errors are usually the explanation for\\n2617: \\t\\t// this one and so it's more helpful to let the user focus on the\\n2618: \\t\\t// root cause rather than distract with this extra problem.\\n2619: \\t\\tif errs := objchange.AssertObjectCompatible(schema, change.After, newVal); len(errs) > 0 {\\n2620: \\t\\t\\tif resp.LegacyTypeSystem {\\n2621: \\t\\t\\t\\t// The shimming of the old type system in the legacy SDK is not precise\\n2622: \\t\\t\\t\\t// enough to pass this consistency check, so we'll give it a pass here,\\n2623: \\t\\t\\t\\t// but we will generate a warning about it so that we are more likely\\n2624: \\t\\t\\t\\t// to notice in the logs if an inconsistency beyond the type system\\n2625: \\t\\t\\t\\t// leads to a downstream provider failure.\\n2626: \\t\\t\\t\\tvar buf strings.Builder\\n2627: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \\\"[WARN] Provider %q produced an unexpected new value for %s, but we are tolerating it because it is using the legacy plugin SDK.\\\\n    The following problems may be the cause of any confusing errors from downstream operations:\\\", n.ResolvedProvider.String(), n.Addr)\\n2628: \\t\\t\\t\\tfor _, err := range errs {\\n2629: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \\\"\\\\n      - %s\\\", tfdiags.FormatError(err))\\n2630: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n2631: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Print(buf.String())\\n2632: \\n2633: \\t\\t\\t\\t// The sort of inconsistency we won't catch here is if a known value\\n2634: \\t\\t\\t\\t// in the plan is changed during apply. That can cause downstream\\n2635: \\t\\t\\t\\t// problems because a dependent resource would make its own plan based\\n2636: \\t\\t\\t\\t// on the planned value, and thus get a different result during the\\n2637: \\t\\t\\t\\t// apply phase. This will usually lead to a \\\"Provider produced invalid plan\\\"\\n2638: \\t\\t\\t\\t// error that incorrectly blames the downstream resource for the change.\\n2639: \\n2640: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n2641: \\t\\t\\t\\tfor _, err := range errs {\\n2642: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n2643: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n2644: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider produced inconsistent result after apply\\\",\\n2645: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n2646: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"When applying changes to %s, provider %q produced an unexpected new value: %s.\\\\n\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\",\\n2647: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tn.Addr, n.ResolvedProvider.String(), tfdiags.FormatError(err),\\n2648: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n2649: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t))\\n2650: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n2651: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2652: \\t\\t}\\n2653: \\t}\\n2654: \\n2655: \\t// If a provider returns a null or non-null object at the wrong time then\\n2656: \\t// we still want to save that but it often causes some confusing behaviors\\n2657: \\t// where it seems like Terraform is failing to take any action at all,\\n2658: \\t// so we'll generate some errors to draw attention to it.\\n2659: \\tif !diags.HasErrors() {\\n2660: \\t\\tif change.Action == plans.Delete && !newVal.IsNull() {\\n2661: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n2662: \\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n2663: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider returned invalid result object after apply\\\",\\n2664: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n2665: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"After applying a %s plan, the provider returned a non-null object for %s. Destroying should always produce a null value, so this is always a bug in the provider and should be reported in the provider's own repository. Terraform will still save this errant object in the state for debugging and recovery.\\\",\\n2666: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tchange.Action, n.Addr,\\n2667: \\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n2668: \\t\\t\\t))\\n2669: \\t\\t}\\n2670: \\t\\tif change.Action != plans.Delete && newVal.IsNull() {\\n2671: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n2672: \\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n2673: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider returned invalid result object after apply\\\",\\n2674: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n2675: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"After applying a %s plan, the provider returned a null object for %s. Only destroying should always produce a null value, so this is always a bug in the provider and should be reported in the provider's own repository.\\\",\\n2676: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tchange.Action, n.Addr,\\n2677: \\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n2678: \\t\\t\\t))\\n2679: \\t\\t}\\n2680: \\t}\\n2681: \\n2682: \\tswitch {\\n2683: \\tcase diags.HasErrors() && newVal.IsNull():\\n2684: \\t\\t// Sometimes providers return a null value when an operation fails for\\n2685: \\t\\t// some reason, but we'd rather keep the prior state so that the error\\n2686: \\t\\t// can be corrected on a subsequent run. We must only do this for null\\n2687: \\t\\t// new value though, or else we may discard partial updates the\\n2688: \\t\\t// provider was able to complete. Otherwise, we'll continue using the\\n2689: \\t\\t// prior state as the new value, making this effectively a no-op.  If\\n2690: \\t\\t// the item really _has_ been deleted then our next refresh will detect\\n2691: \\t\\t// that and fix it up.\\n2692: \\t\\treturn state.DeepCopy(), diags\\n2693: \\n2694: \\tcase diags.HasErrors() && !newVal.IsNull():\\n2695: \\t\\t// if we have an error, make sure we restore the object status in the new state\\n2696: \\t\\tnewState := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\n2697: \\t\\t\\tStatus:              state.Status,\\n2698: \\t\\t\\tValue:               newVal,\\n2699: \\t\\t\\tPrivate:             resp.Private,\\n2700: \\t\\t\\tCreateBeforeDestroy: createBeforeDestroy,\\n2701: \\t\\t}\\n2702: \\n2703: \\t\\t// if the resource was being deleted, the dependencies are not going to\\n2704: \\t\\t// be recalculated and we need to restore those as well.\\n2705: \\t\\tif change.Action == plans.Delete {\\n2706: \\t\\t\\tnewState.Dependencies = state.Dependencies\\n2707: \\t\\t}\\n2708: \\n2709: \\t\\treturn newState, diags\\n2710: \\n2711: \\tcase !newVal.IsNull():\\n2712: \\t\\t// Non error case with a new state\\n2713: \\t\\tnewState := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\\n2714: \\t\\t\\tStatus:              states.ObjectReady,\\n2715: \\t\\t\\tValue:               newVal,\\n2716: \\t\\t\\tPrivate:             resp.Private,\\n2717: \\t\\t\\tCreateBeforeDestroy: createBeforeDestroy,\\n2718: \\t\\t}\\n2719: \\t\\treturn newState, diags\\n2720: \\n2721: \\tdefault:\\n2722: \\t\\t// Non error case, were the object was deleted\\n2723: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n2724: \\t}\\n2725: }\\n2726: \\n2727: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) prevRunAddr(ctx EvalContext) addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\\n2728: \\treturn resourceInstancePrevRunAddr(ctx, n.Addr)\\n2729: }\\n2730: \\n2731: func resourceInstancePrevRunAddr(ctx EvalContext, currentAddr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\\n2732: \\ttable := ctx.MoveResults()\\n2733: \\treturn table.OldAddr(currentAddr)\\n2734: }\\n2735: \\n2736: func getAction(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, priorVal, plannedNewVal cty.Value, createBeforeDestroy bool, forceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance, reqRep cty.PathSet) (action plans.Action, actionReason plans.ResourceInstanceChangeActionReason) {\\n2737: \\t// The user might also ask us to force replacing a particular resource\\n2738: \\t// instance, regardless of whether the provider thinks it needs replacing.\\n2739: \\t// For example, users typically do this if they learn a particular object\\n2740: \\t// has become degraded in an immutable infrastructure scenario and so\\n2741: \\t// replacing it with a new object is a viable repair path.\\n2742: \\tmatchedForceReplace := false\\n2743: \\tfor _, candidateAddr := range forceReplace {\\n2744: \\t\\tif candidateAddr.Equal(addr) {\\n2745: \\t\\t\\tmatchedForceReplace = true\\n2746: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n2747: \\t\\t}\\n2748: \\n2749: \\t\\t// For \\\"force replace\\\" purposes we require an exact resource instance\\n2750: \\t\\t// address to match. If a user forgets to include the instance key\\n2751: \\t\\t// for a multi-instance resource then it won't match here, but we\\n2752: \\t\\t// have an earlier check in NodePlannableResource.Execute that should\\n2753: \\t\\t// prevent us from getting here in that case.\\n2754: \\t}\\n2755: \\n2756: \\t// Unmark for this test for value equality.\\n2757: \\teqV := plannedNewVal.Equals(priorVal)\\n2758: \\teq := eqV.IsKnown() && eqV.True()\\n2759: \\n2760: \\tswitch {\\n2761: \\tcase priorVal.IsNull():\\n2762: \\t\\taction = plans.Create\\n2763: \\tcase eq && !matchedForceReplace:\\n2764: \\t\\taction = plans.NoOp\\n2765: \\tcase matchedForceReplace || !reqRep.Empty():\\n2766: \\t\\t// If the user \\\"forced replace\\\" of this instance of if there are any\\n2767: \\t\\t// \\\"requires replace\\\" paths left _after our filtering above_ then this\\n2768: \\t\\t// is a replace action.\\n2769: \\t\\tif createBeforeDestroy {\\n2770: \\t\\t\\taction = plans.CreateThenDelete\\n2771: \\t\\t} else {\\n2772: \\t\\t\\taction = plans.DeleteThenCreate\\n2773: \\t\\t}\\n2774: \\t\\tswitch {\\n2775: \\t\\tcase matchedForceReplace:\\n2776: \\t\\t\\tactionReason = plans.ResourceInstanceReplaceByRequest\\n2777: \\t\\tcase !reqRep.Empty():\\n2778: \\t\\t\\tactionReason = plans.ResourceInstanceReplaceBecauseCannotUpdate\\n2779: \\t\\t}\\n2780: \\tdefault:\\n2781: \\t\\taction = plans.Update\\n2782: \\t\\t// \\\"Delete\\\" is never chosen here, because deletion plans are always\\n2783: \\t\\t// created more directly elsewhere, such as in \\\"orphan\\\" handling.\\n2784: \\t}\\n2785: \\n2786: \\treturn\\n2787: }\\n2788: \\n2789: // getRequiredReplaces returns a list of paths to attributes whose changes mean\\n2790: // that we must replace rather than update an existing remote object.\\n2791: //\\n2792: // The provider produces a list of paths to attributes whose changes mean\\n2793: // that we must replace rather than update an existing remote object.\\n2794: // However, we only need to do that if the identified attributes _have_\\n2795: // actually changed -- particularly after we may have undone some of the\\n2796: // changes in processIgnoreChanges -- so now we'll filter that list to\\n2797: // include only where changes are detected.\\n2798: //\\n2799: // Both the priorVal and plannedNewVal should be unmarked before calling this\\n2800: // function. This function exposes nothing about the priorVal or plannedVal\\n2801: // except for the paths that require replacement which can be deduced from the\\n2802: // type with or without marks.\\n2803: func getRequiredReplaces(priorVal, plannedNewVal cty.Value, requiredReplaces []cty.Path, providerAddr tfaddr.Provider, addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) (cty.PathSet, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n2804: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n2805: \\n2806: \\treqRep := cty.NewPathSet()\\n2807: \\tif len(requiredReplaces) > 0 {\\n2808: \\t\\tfor _, path := range requiredReplaces {\\n2809: \\t\\t\\tif priorVal.IsNull() {\\n2810: \\t\\t\\t\\t// If prior is null then we don't expect any RequiresReplace at all,\\n2811: \\t\\t\\t\\t// because this is a Create action.\\n2812: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n2813: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2814: \\n2815: \\t\\t\\tpriorChangedVal, priorPathDiags := hcl.ApplyPath(priorVal, path, nil)\\n2816: \\t\\t\\tplannedChangedVal, plannedPathDiags := hcl.ApplyPath(plannedNewVal, path, nil)\\n2817: \\t\\t\\tif plannedPathDiags.HasErrors() && priorPathDiags.HasErrors() {\\n2818: \\t\\t\\t\\t// This means the path was invalid in both the prior and new\\n2819: \\t\\t\\t\\t// values, which is an error with the provider itself.\\n2820: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n2821: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n2822: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider produced invalid plan\\\",\\n2823: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n2824: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider %q has indicated \\\\\\\"requires replacement\\\\\\\" on %s for a non-existent attribute path %#v.\\\\n\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\",\\n2825: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tproviderAddr, addr, path,\\n2826: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n2827: \\t\\t\\t\\t))\\n2828: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n2829: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2830: \\n2831: \\t\\t\\t// Make sure we have valid Values for both values.\\n2832: \\t\\t\\t// Note: if the opposing value was of the type\\n2833: \\t\\t\\t// cty.DynamicPseudoType, the type assigned here may not exactly\\n2834: \\t\\t\\t// match the schema. {
  "command": "curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:18973/mcp -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n  -d '{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":3,\"method\":\"tools/call\",\"params\":{\"name\":\"read_file\",\"arguments\":{\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3\",\"path\":\"internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go\"}}}' \\\n  2>&1 | grep \"^data:\" | python3 -c \"\nimport sys, json\n\nlines = sys.stdin.read().strip().split('\\n')\nfor line in lines:\n    if line.startswith('data:'):\n        data = json.loads(line[5:])\n        if 'result' in data:\n            content = data['result']['content']\n            for item in content:\n                if item['type'] == 'text':\n                    raw = item['text']\n                    # Strip line numbers (format: 'N: content\\n')\n                    import re\n                    go_lines = re.sub(r'^\\d+: ', '', raw, flags=re.MULTILINE)\n                    # Apply substitutions\n                    go_lines = go_lines.replace('NodeAbstractResouceInstance', 'NodeResourceInstanceBase')\n                    go_lines = go_lines.replace('NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance', 'NewNodeResourceInstanceBase')\n                    go_lines = go_lines.replace('NodeAbstractResourceInstance', 'NodeResourceInstanceBase')\n                    print(go_lines, end='')\n                    break\n        break\n\" > /workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go\necho \"Exit code: $?\"\nwc -l /workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go",
  "security_risk": "MEDIUM"
}

{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/fetch_and_transform.py << 'EOF'\nimport subprocess, json, re, sys\n\nresult = subprocess.run(\n    ['curl', '-s', '-X', 'POST', 'http://127.0.0.1:18973/mcp',\n     '-H', 'Content-Type: application/json',\n     '-d', json.dumps({\n         \"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 3, \"method\": \"tools/call\",\n         \"params\": {\"name\": \"read_file\", \"arguments\": {\n             \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3\",\n             \"path\": \"internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go\"\n         }}\n     })],\n    capture_output=True, text=True\n)\n\nfor line in result.stdout.split('\\n'):\n    if line.startswith('data:'):\n        data = json.loads(line[5:])\n        if 'result' in data:\n            for item in data['result']['content']:\n                if item['type'] == 'text':\n                    raw = item['text']\n                    # Strip line numbers\n                    go_lines = re.sub(r'^\\d+: ', '', raw, flags=re.MULTILINE)\n                    # Apply substitutions\n                    go_lines = go_lines.replace('NodeAbstractResouceInstance', 'NodeResourceInstanceBase')\n                    go_lines = go_lines.replace('NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance', 'NewNodeResourceInstanceBase')\n                    go_lines = go_lines.replace('NodeAbstractResourceInstance', 'NodeResourceInstanceBase')\n                    with open('/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go', 'w') as f:\n                        f.write(go_lines)\n                    print(f\"Written {len(go_lines.splitlines())} lines\")\n                    sys.exit(0)\n\nprint(\"ERROR: Could not fetch file\", file=sys.stderr)\nsys.exit(1)\nEOF\npython3 /tmp/fetch_and_transform.py",
  "security_risk": "MEDIUM"
}

Output

"cat > /tmp/fetch_and_transform.py << 'EOF'\nimport subprocess, json, re, sys\nresult = subprocess.run(\n    ['curl', '-s', '-X', 'POST', 'http://127.0.0.1:18973/mcp',\n     '-H', 'Content-Type: application/json',\n     '-d', json.dumps({\n         \"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 3, \"method\": \"tools/call\",\n         \"params\": {\"name\": \"read_file\", \"arguments\": {\n             \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3\",\n             \"path\": \"internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go\"\n         }}\n     })],\n    capture_output=True, text=True\n)\nfor line in result.stdout.split('\\n'):\n    if line.startswith('data:'):\n        data = json.loads(line[5:])\n        if 'result' in data:\n            for item in data['result']['content']:\n                if item['type'] == 'text':\n                    raw = item['text']\n                    # Strip line numbers\n                    go_lines = re.sub(r'^\\d+: ', '', raw, flags=re.MULTILINE)\n                    # Apply substitutions\n                    go_lines = go_lines.replace('NodeAbstractResouceInstance', 'NodeResourceInstanceBase')\n                    go_lines = go_lines.replace('NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance', 'NewNodeResourceInstanceBase')\n                    go_lines = go_lines.replace('NodeAbstractResourceInstance', 'NodeResourceInstanceBase')\n                    with open('/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_abstract_instance.go', 'w') as f:\n                        f.write(go_lines)\n                    print(f\"Written {len(go_lines.splitlines())} lines\")\n                    sys.exit(0)\nprint(\"ERROR: Could not fetch file\", file=sys.stderr)\nsys.exit(1)\nEOF\n\n\npython3 /tmp/fetch_and_transform.py\nWritten 1 lines"
